NixOS/nixpkgs#454608.body: ### Nixpkgs version - Unstable (25.11) ### Describe the bug It appears that the `mbedtls` that godot provides isn't aligning properly. This does not allow for using the Asset Library functionality and, of lesser importance (for more obvious reasons), the update checker. ### Steps to reproduce Install `godot`, run `godot`, attempt to use Asset Library or observe the update checker at the bottom right of the startup window. ### Expected behaviour Should permit use of the asset library. ### Screenshots ### Relevant log output ```console ERROR: Cannot open X509CertificateMbedTLS file 'False'. at: load (modules/mbedtls/crypto_mbedtls.cpp:160) ... /build/source/library/ssl_tls13_generic.c:1552: 0x564e29f765b0: Perform PSA-based ECDH/FFDH computation. /build/source/library/ssl_tls13_client.c:1950: 0x564e29f765b0: Switch to handshake keys for inbound traffic /build/source/library/ssl_tls.c:9946: 0x564e29f765b0: x509_verify_cert() returned -9984 (-0x2700) ERROR: TLS handshake error: -9984 at: _do_handshake (modules/mbedtls/stream_peer_mbedtls.cpp:88) mbedtls error: returned -0x2700 ``` ### Additional context Similar issues have happened on other distros like Arch: https://gitlab.archlinux.org/archlinux/packaging/packages/godot/-/issues/5 ### System metadata - system: `"x86_64-linux"` - host os: `Linux 6.17.3, NixOS, 25.11 (Xantusia), 25.11.20251020.cb82756` - multi-user?: `yes` - sandbox: `yes` - version: `nix-env (Nix) 2.31.2` - channels(root): `"nixos, nixpkgs"` - nixpkgs: `/nix/store/422l3k4bzdy5k6jx0j84f9yvrp6a8k16-nixpkgs/nixpkgs` ### Notify maintainers @shiryel @corngood --- **Note for maintainers:** Please tag this issue in your pull request description. (i.e. `Resolves #ISSUE`.) ### I assert that this issue is relevant for Nixpkgs - [x] I assert that this is a bug and not a support request. - [x] I assert that this is not a [duplicate of an existing issue](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%220.kind%3A+bug%22+-label%3A%226.topic%3A+darwin%22+-label%3A%226.topic%3A+nixos%22). - [x] I assert that I have read the [NixOS Code of Conduct](https://github.com/NixOS/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) and agree to abide by it. ### Is this issue important to you? Add a :+1: [reaction] to [issues you find important]. [reaction]: https://github.blog/2016-03-10-add-reactions-to-pull-requests-issues-and-comments/ [issues you find important]: https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+sort%3Areactions-%2B1-desc
NixOS/nixpkgs#454608.title: godot: TLS handshake fails, not allowing AssetLib use

flathub/org.godotengine.Godot#187.body:
A new feature in Godot 4.3 is the ability to set whether you'd like to use the built-in file dialog for godot or use the OS native dialog. No matter what this option is set to it always defaults to the OS native dialog:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3dbb519d-e6c5-4bfe-bdab-b301745aa0de)

 (for me this is the GTK dialog):

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/201e66e7-f01f-42a1-8451-e9806c8fbef9)

While this wouldn't be a big deal on it's own, combined with [this bug](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/92754) it makes flatpak godot 4.3 somewhat unusable for me right now.

Version: `v4.3.stable.flathub [77dcf97d8]`
OS: Guix System 6.9.12
flathub/org.godotengine.Godot#187.title: "Use Native File Dialogs" has no effect

godotengine/godot#17110.body:
**Godot version:**
3.0.1

**OS/device including version:**
Windows 10 / Microsoft Surface Pro 4

**Issue description:**
3 usability issues in the Autoload dialog (see accompanying screenshot)

1) the input fields for node name and path are reversed from the order they appear in the table. Recommend keeping same order so user's mental model - established during input matches the table shown on-screen.

2) the 'Singleton' column is cut-off and the table columns don't appear to be adjustable.

3) the toggle icon for a node in the Singleton column is illegible - cannot tell whether on or off

1. open the settings dialog
2. click the autoload tab
3. add an autoload node

![godot_301_settings_autoload_dialog](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/253436/36802313-d897901c-1cb4-11e8-9901-aabc8231d783.PNG)

godotengine/godot#27467.body:
**Godot version:** 3.1

**OS/device including version:** Ubuntu 18.04

**Issue description:**
Code completion does not provide parameter hints for custom functions if they are used from a script other than they are declared in.

```gdscript
# one script
extends Object
class_name KnownClass

func test(param: String):
	pass
```

```gdscript
# another script
extends Node2D

func _ready():
	var kc: KnownClass = KnownClass.new()
	kc.test("here completion does not show parameter list")
```

**Minimal reproduction project:**
[repro_cc_custom_func.tar.gz](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/3015179/repro_cc_custom_func.tar.gz)
godotengine/godot#27467.title: [3.x] Code completion is not full for custom functions

godotengine/godot#37604.body:
**Godot version:**
4.0.dev.custom_build. 321ce4d4c

**Issue description:**

With help of https://github.com/qarmin/godot_signal_checker (which could be rewritten to python to be able to put it inside CI in Godot repository) I found lot of potential unused signals with this methodology:

Added signals have at the beginning of line `ADD_SIGNAL(MethodInfo(\"`
Emitted signals have at the beginning of line `emit_signal(CoreStringNames::get_singleton()->`, `emit_signal(SceneStringNames::get_singleton()->` or `emit_signal(\"`
Connected signals have at the beginning of line `->connect(\"` or `connect_compat(\"`

**Signals which are emitted and connected but never added**
- [x] - dpi_changed
- [x] - drop_attempted
- [x] - modal_closed (#41920)

**Signals which are added but never emitted or connected**
- [x] - bake_finished
- [x] - clear_request
- [x] - copy_request
- [x] - item_custom_button_pressed
- [x] - item_group_status_changed
- [x] - item_lock_status_changed
- [x] - pause_pressed
- [x] - search

**Signals which are added and connected but never emitted**
- [x] - autoload_changed
- [x] - export_presets_updated
- [x] - group_edited
- [x] - inputmap_changed
- [x] - localization_changed
- [x] - multiple_properties_changed
- [x] - node_added
- [x] - node_removed
- [x] - preview_invalidated
- [x] - property_edited
- [x] - removed_from_graph

Our unit test coverage is currently fairly low. We'd like to increase our unit test coverage; any help is welcome.

**Interested in writing new unit tests?** See the [unit tests documentation](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/development/cpp/unit_testing.html) and [compiling instructions](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/compiling/).
If you have further questions, join the [Godot Contributors Chat](https://chat.godotengine.org/).

When opening a pull request, please link back to this issue (`#43440`) in the PR description so that we can keep track of it more easily.

> If you have the appropriate permissions, feel free to edit this issue to add new items or check items for which a PR has been opened.

godotengine/godot#48573.body:
**Godot version:** 3.3, `master` Git 7050e4d3077c278bda35d21ed60d7068cc4bc9e9

**OS/device including version:** Fedora 33, GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA 465.27)

**Issue description:**

Lines have more washed out colors in the 3D editor, regardless of tonemapping settings.

The axis colors themselves are identical. Notice how the rotation gizmo in the top-right corner has the same colors on both 3.3 and `master`.

I'm not sure what's the cause of this – maybe sRGB conversions in the line drawing or rendering code?

Both screenshots below use linear tonemapping, yet lines in Vulkan are brighter:

![2021-05-09_10 56 06](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/180032/117566232-b5c3a880-b0b5-11eb-9e9e-272c087830ea.png)

**Steps to reproduce:**

- In `3.x`, open the 3D editor, click the **View** in the top-left corner of the viewport then uncheck **View Environment**.
- In `master`, open the 3D editor and click the WorldEnvironment icon at the top of the 3D editor to disable the environment preview;

**Minimal reproduction project:** N/A
godotengine/godot#48573.title: Vulkan: Lines have more washed out colors in the 3D editor

godotengine/godot#51299.body:
### Godot version

4.0dev (1f69582)

### System information

Windows 10, Vulkan, RTX 2080

### Issue description

I'm trying to create a "puddle" decal that's painted on top of a street. A common case in video games, the puddle needs to be a pure mirror, while the street, not. 

The street roughness appears to affect the decal roughness, so I can't achieve this effect.


### Steps to reproduce

I created a simple ORM texture with no ambient occlusion, full gloss, full metallic, basically a mirror.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/87843097/128435454-d0ee777b-356b-4af2-b63b-efda4c016f8f.png)

The resulting ORM texture is magenta.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/87843097/128435479-a0c89349-73cf-48de-942c-cd86084a865c.png)

Now I've placed a puddle decal on the street that has this ORM texture, when the street itself has "1" metallic and "0" roughness, I see a reflection across the entire street, including the puddle decal.

(Street texture settings)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/87843097/128435626-b07f9e6a-b8c1-4b64-899d-146f02dd457c.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/87843097/128435581-3685092f-df71-400a-a5f9-948946e15b3c.png)

However, when I turn the roughness up on the street texture, it also affects the puddle texture to the point the puddle is no longer reflective.

(Street texture settings)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/87843097/128435885-62f28f8d-9bdd-4a4f-8956-3cd052905ebc.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/87843097/128435841-925065e1-7f40-41ce-bf55-f742fb2e4422.png)


I don't know what the logic is behind how much a source texture affects the decal, but I basically need control over how this blends the ORM texture.

### Minimal reproduction project

[Decal ORM Bug.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/6943335/Decal.ORM.Bug.zip)

Left: white plane has roughness: 1
Right: white plane has roughness: 0
Expected: both black decals show a reflection of the orange cube

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/87843097/128463075-02e74b5d-3c65-4c09-937f-4524a302fd84.png)
godotengine/godot#51299.title: Decal ORM implementation not completely overriding source texture?

godotengine/godot#51773.body:
### Godot version

3.3.2.stable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

The connected joypads array isn't consistent between an app restart after a joystick is connected mid game.
Expected always the same joystick order.
With this I can "patch" the [missing unique gamepadid](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/51772) issue using the controller index.
I expect the same order always from that array if the same joysticks are connected.
Maybe sort the joypads  array at start and when a new joystick is connected?

### Steps to reproduce

connect a xbox controller (xinput_1), connect any controller, in my case a virtual one, (vjoy_1 ), start godot app and  Input.get_connected_joypads() return:
```
0. xinput_1
1. vjoy_1
```

connect another xbox controller (xinput_2) without closing the godot app and print joypads again:
```
0. xinput_1
1. vjoy_1
2. xinput_2
```

restart godot app without disconecting the controllers and print the joypads:
```
0. xinput_1
1. xinput_2
2. vjoy_1
```


### Minimal reproduction project

_No response_
godotengine/godot#51773.title: Input.get_connected_joypads() joystick order isn't consistent.

### Godot version

3.3.4, 3.4-rc2

### System information

openSUSE 15.3

### Issue description

When I want to drag a node to the end of scene tree, it's often a hassle.

I can only release the dragged after the current last node if either the current last node has no children or it's in collapsed state. So I have to artificially create a situation where at least one of these conditions is true, as a workaround.

If the expanded node is not the last in the tree, it's possible to release a node after it. Depending whether one releases the node a bit higher or lower, it's possible to choose whether it should be a child or a sibling of the expanded node. But when the expanded node is the last one, the only option is a child.

### Steps to reproduce

First, have a node with children in the end of the scene tree, expanded (Exit on the picture below).
Second, drag some other node in the end of the scene tree (Camera on the picture below).

It's impossible to release Camera in a way that it's placed after Exit as a sibling.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11571820/139489318-d3766eb5-d9a6-4619-9925-c9aac6cb2518.png)

_No response_

godotengine/godot#73299.body:
### Godot version

4.0.rc1 (custom build with double_precision)

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

In my project I'm interpolating moving mesh positions to their physics-body's location with the `get_physics_interpolation_fraction()` and `Transform3D::interpolate_with`. At some point I got this error message twice (this is taken from the provided example):

```
E 0:00:02:0117   InterpolationTest.gd:27 @ _ready(): Basis must be normalized in order to be casted to a Quaternion. Use get_rotation_quaternion() or call orthonormalized() if the Basis contains linearly independent vectors.
  <C++ Fehler>   Condition "!is_rotation()" is true. Returning: Quaternion()
  <C++ Source>   core\math\basis.cpp:702 @ Basis::get_quaternion()
  <Stacktrace>   InterpolationTest.gd:27 @ _ready()
```

followed by:

```
E 0:00:02:0127   RendererSceneCull::instance_set_transform: Condition "!v.is_finite()" is true.
  <C++ Source>   servers\rendering\renderer_scene_cull.cpp:906 @ RendererSceneCull::instance_set_transform()
```

`orthonormalized()` did not help, but in the end it turned out that I divided by zero at some point in my angles calculation and rotated the basis by `NaN`. This did not cause any issue (correctly?) but crashed when I tried to interpolate with a transform that resulted from that basis.

I feel like rotating the basis by `NaN` should have already resulted in an error or - if it is allowed to `move_and_slide` with a `NaN` transform - that the error message of `interpolate_with` should be more helpful here.

### Steps to reproduce

Run this scene to reproduce the issue

```gdscript
extends CharacterBody3D


var _mesh_instance := MeshInstance3D.new()
var _this_is_zero := 0.0

func _init() -> void:
    _mesh_instance.top_level = true
    add_child(_mesh_instance)


func _ready() -> void:
    # Set the rotation of this CharacterBody3D
    var new_basis := Basis()
    new_basis = new_basis.rotated(Vector3.UP, deg_to_rad(1 / _this_is_zero))
    # The basis now has NaN for all its values.
    transform.basis = new_basis
        
    # Move forward
    var old_transform = global_transform
    velocity = Vector3.FORWARD
    move_and_slide()
    var new_transform = global_transform
    
    # Interpolate the mesh half way to the physical position
    var current_transform = global_transform
    _mesh_instance.transform = old_transform.interpolate_with(new_transform, 0.5)

```

### Minimal reproduction project

N/A
godotengine/godot#73299.title: Unhelpful error message when interpolating on a NaN transform

godotengine/godot#80127.body:
### Godot version

4.1stable

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

A common use case for a Label in some applications is to encapsulate it within a ScrollContainer, allowing the user to scroll through the entirety of the text. However, Godot does not permit this use case because the entirety of the text is processed every frame, so a Label with lots of text is unreasonably slow. Further, there does not seem to be a simple workaround to "hack" seamless scrolling into the application.

Any of the label settings or font settings do not noticeably affect the processing of the label. The label is just as slow with word wrap turned off, so it's not a word wrap processing issue. The only factor I could find that affected the time the label spent processing was the amount of text, which sounds like undesirable behavior.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Label
2. Set the `text `property to a large amount of text
3. Check the process time of the application

### Minimal reproduction project

The following is a very minimal project consisting of a label with around 100k characters.

[laggy_text_mrp.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/12232283/laggy_text_mrp.zip)
godotengine/godot#80127.title: Laggy Label with large amounts of characters

### Godot version

4.1.1.stable.official

Windows 10, Vulkan forward +

### Issue description

Under certain conditions, it's possible for the normal map to effectively invert the lighting, as seen here:
![revolver_normal](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/7751182/c0ab812a-c6fd-4d89-bbfd-8dc409f176fb)

I tried to make some simpler repro cases in the background, but, for some reason, the issue only happens with my revolver.  This was originally happening with a procedurally rotated bone using skeleton.set_bone_pose_rotation, but I tested with an animation driving the bone and the issue still happened.

My hypothesis is that the tangent and/or binormal are getting miscalculated because a cross product is attempted with 2 vectors that are the same somehow, but I could be way off.

### Steps to reproduce

I attempted to recreate this using a simple skinned 6-sided cylinder mesh with a rotation animation by itself, but, alas, the issue only seems to repro with my KOOK game asset, so I've included that in the repro project:

godotengine/godot#82424.body:
### Godot version

v4.2.dev.custom_build [2048fe5df]

### System information

Godot v4.2.dev (2048fe5df) - Gentoo 2.14 - Wayland - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6600 (RADV NAVI23) () - AMD Ryzen 5 5600 6-Core Processor (12 Threads)

### Issue description

There is an issue with the way `ConfigFile` encodes floating-point values. The low encoding precision is limited to the 6 biggest numbers, and since Godot's floating-point number is 32 bits, its `ConfigFile` encoding should fail at a minimum of 1e+14.

### Examples
* 123456.0 gets encoded as "123456.0," and parsed to 123456.0, but the number 1234567.0 will be encoded as "1.23457e+06," and parsed to 1234570.0, which is wrong.

* 11111111111111.0, gets encoded as "1.11111e+13," and parsed to 11111100000000.0.

### Proposed Fix
I suggest to only encode floats greater than 1e+14 in scientific notation, and to keep 14 digits of precision when doing so, while omitting trailing 0's. Otherwise, standard notation should be used.

### Note
This is potentially a good issue to receive the label "good first issue."

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
extends Node2D

func _ready():
	var cfg = ConfigFile.new()
	cfg.set_value("section", "key", 1000001.0)
	# The issue only occurs when parsing encoded text,
	# such as with `parse()` or `load()`
	cfg.parse(cfg.encode_to_text())
	assert(cfg.get_value("section", "key") == 1000001.0)
```

### Minimal reproduction project

[godot.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/12735143/godot.zip)
godotengine/godot#82424.title: ConfigFile saving floats with low precision and unable to handle floats > 1e+06

### Tested versions

### System information

### Issue description

I have a key rebinding menu where player is allowed to have two sets of keybindings for actions (one of them being and alternative input for keyboard)
![image](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/13497925/7710eb43-90a6-404b-a2f6-13e06ceea2de)
If I were to edit the second event to be the same as the first one (W press), and then in the code go over the events, there will be only two events mapped to the action (W and joystick)

The issue is about making sure that what is visible in the Project's Settings Input map matches the thing we get in code.
If I see 3 events mapped to the action, I expect  ```InputMap.action_get_events("action").size()``` return 3.

Possible solutions:
* Making sure InputMap in code has all events defined in Project Settings
* If making it work with exact events mapped to the action is problematic then we should see a warning that this event is already added and not be allowed to add it. There also needs to be same check when editing existing event

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create an action (called "action" for example) in the Project's Settings Input map with 3 or more events, two or more should be the same
2. Create a script and put the following line in the _ready
3. ```print(InputMap.action_get_events("action").size());```
4. The number printed will be less than the number of events mapped to the action

### Issue description

When you add a curve to the _Spawn_ > _Angle_ setting of a `ParticleProcessMaterial`, this is initialized with a range of -360 to to 360. ### Tested versions

v4.3.beta.custom_build [557f63d03]

Custom build for the `precision=double` flag

### System information

Godot v4.3.beta (557f63d03) - Windows 10.0.22631 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.3598) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800H with Radeon Graphics (16 Threads)

### Issue description

Using a script's path in a `const XXX := preload()` function, and then renaming the script using Godot's "Rename" menu command, gives a "Error at (xx, yy): Could not resolve script "res://script_name.gd".

The error persists even after retyping the expression. Using "=" instead of ":=" also does not make the error go away. Renaming the script back and using the old name in `preload()` also does not resolve the issue.

It's undoable. Gives vibes of "Anton Chigurh" in "No Country for Old Men".

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create any script. e.g. "script_a.gd".
2. In another script, load the first script into a `const` using `preload()`. e.g. `const ScriptA := preload("res://script_a.gd")`.
3. Rename the first script's name. e.g. "script_b.gd".
4. Error appears in the second script.
5. Edit the name in the `preload()` function, rename the first script to original name, close and reopen the second script, and try a few other things, but the error persists.
6. Reload project, error gone.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

The steps above to reproduce the issue are sufficient to display the problem, and no MRP is needed.

_Bugsquad Edit: MRP from duplicate issue: [move-gdscript.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16325085/move-gdscript.zip)_ (uses move instead of rename)
godotengine/godot#92007.title: Changing name of script after using it in `preload()` generates "Could not resolve script" error, recoverable only by reloading project

godotengine/godot#96504.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.3.stable.flathub [77dcf97d82cbfe4e4615475fa52ca03da645dbd8]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable (77dcf97d8) - Freedesktop SDK 23.08 (Flatpak runtime) - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) HD Graphics 5500 (BDW GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-5300U CPU @ 2.30GHz (4 Threads)

### Issue description

In GDShader `#if` condition expressions, you can use several operators (more than I expected it to allow) and even functions (e.g. math like `sqrt`, `sin`), which will be evaluated in the preprocessor. Which ones are undocumented.

At first I expected those to at least match the GDShader functions and operator syntax. But I found it's not always the case (e.g. ternary `? :` doesn't work, `inversesqrt(...)` doesn't either).
After some more tests (see https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/96253#issuecomment-2318882070 for background), I found it's accepting functions in `@GlobalScope` as well as some constructors like `bool` etc. It allows even functions very weird to allow, like `randf`, `instance_from_id` and `rid_from_int64`.
***Allowing even stuff like `bytes_to_var_with_objects` seems like it could be particularly dangerous (ACE risk?).***
But I don't know for sure to which extent it allows functions with side-effects and whether it affects the editor.

In any case, I'm surprised things like functions work at all. I was expecting that dealing with integers and booleans would be enough for a preprocessor. In fact, ***if the intention is to match C/GLSL, then GDShader is doing way more than it should*** in the preprocessor `#if` directives. ***Not even C deals with float or boolean constants in the preprocessor, let alone math functions. GLSL doesn't either.*** They do handle integers without the `u` suffix (so no `uint` support).
C does handle arbitrary names, treating them like `0` (even `true` is treated like `0`), but GLSL doesn't allow them on `#if`.
GDShader allows most literals (bool, int_decimal, int_hex, uint_decimal, float), except for uint_hex like `0x0u`.

Comments from @pirey0:
> 7 - function calls in #if directives:
> Really interesting stuff! Looks like it could be very useful and at the same time very dangerous. Again probably a topic for a rendering meeting (unless this was already discussed in the past.)

> I brought up this Issue in the 2024-09-03 Rendering Meeting, these are the key points:
> - Aim for glsl-like behavior, so that shaders can be ported over very easily
> - Do not bloat the preprocessor codebase
> - May need to scope back some of the eval() stuff in #if directives

IMO, GDShader should match GLSL, and be as safe as possible:
- only deterministic behavior must be allowed
- no side-effects
- no preprocessor functions, only macros
- no dealing with float at all; no boolean literals either
    - there's no need to extrapolate C/GLSL behavior
- keep current GLSL-like (not C-like) behavior of not expanding undefined macro names as `0`
    - so even though `true` isn't a keyword, it won't expand to `0` by default; raise "undefined macro" error instead
- only integer literals should be allowed, as well as macros that evaluate to integers;
    - like expected in C and GLSL, have 0 mean false and non-zero mean true when on the context of boolean operators (like `&&` and `||`) and the final `#if` expression boolean result
- no arbitrary expressions, only allow:
    - int32 literals (decimal and hex) without `u` suffix
    - parentheses
    - macro expansions (simple and function-like calls)
    - logical and arithmetic operators to deal with integers as said above
    - the `defined(macro_name)` (also `defined macro_name`) preprocessor keyword is the only "function" allowed

A lot of these behaviors are explicitly defined in the [GLSL ES 3.00 spec](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenGL/specs/es/3.0/GLSL_ES_Specification_3.00.pdf) pages 13~14.

---
Moved from [#96253 (sub-issue 7).](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/96253#issuecomment-2318882070)

### Steps to reproduce

`bug-global-fn-evaluation.gdshader`
```glsl
shader_type spatial;

#if (sin(0) + typeof(print(rid_allocate_id())) - typeof(print(bytes_to_var_with_objects([]))))
// can typing here have editor side-effects? it prints to console at least...
// bytes_to_var_with_objects = arbitrary code execution risk? no idea
code, etc
#endif

#if randf() < 0.5
I have heard of non deterministic compilation but this is crazy
// 50% chance of this code being included every time I type
#endif

$ // error on purpose to log preprocessor output
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#96504.title: GDShader preprocessor evaluates weird functions in `#if` condition expressions

### Issue description

The documentation of the InputEventMouseMotion velocity and screen_velocity talks about how it should be used with Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED. Which is confusing as in captured mode both return `(0,0)`. Which is intended according to this: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/32578 (it's an old one, so idk if this still is intended).

IMO either velocity should return a value in captured mode or the documentation should be adjusted to remove the note about captured mouse and instead document that it returns Vector2(0, 0) in that case.

Current documentation for reference:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2970ebd8-26da-4d9c-b5c8-7cf9b78beaa0)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aecb9565-9331-4396-be26-62d79dec730c)

 

### Steps to reproduce

add this script and see it printing (0,0). _If you comment out the mouse capture it prints a velocity_:

```
func _ready() -> void:
	Input.mouse_mode = Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED

func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event is InputEventMouseMotion:
		print(event.get_velocity())
		print(event.get_screen_velocity())
```

### Issue description

When setting `current_tab` on a `TabContainer` instance, the visibility of the tabs does not get updated immediately.

In my particular case, this was causing an issue because I wanted to set the UI focus after switching the tab, but `%my_tab.find_next_valid_focus()` was returning an element outside the tab.

To set the focus one needs to either:

3. Wait a frame

godotengine/godot#101830.body:
### Tested versions

Current master (discovered during https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100694 / https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100619).

### System information

All systems are affected.

### Issue description

The GDScript parser has circular ownership (cyclic references). Through it, objects (at least `CowData`, potentially more) are accessed that are in the midst of destructing themselves. This may lead to leaks, segmentation faults, and unexpected behavior (UB). 

The cyclic reference should be sanitized, because it is partially relying on UB (accessing half destructed objects) and can lead to problems when attempting seemingly innocent refactors. I ran into it when working on https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100619. I managed to avoid leaks in this particular PR by papering over the issue  - specifically improving robustness against misuse of `CowData` through https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100694.


### Steps to reproduce

The objects involved in the cyclic reference are logged [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/actions/runs/12435300159/job/34720869143).

It is difficult for me to identify the problematic code further because I am not familiar with the GDScript parsing code.

It may be possible to identify what code accesses `CowData` while it is destructing by storing in it a boolean `is_destructing`, and if it is accessed while the boolean is true, it shall log the stack trace.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#101830.title: Circular references in GDScript code may lead to leaks, UB and segmentation faults

godotengine/godot#102173.body:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.3.stable.mono (2a6663838) (Custom Build)

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable.mono (2a6663838) - Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS 24.04 - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 Ti (nvidia; 565.57.01) - AMD Ryzen 9 3900X 12-Core Processor (24 Threads)

### Issue description

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6e9c0f3-d81c-40c9-8eb7-a6dd8b6f9ae6)

This function is mainly used for gathering information to then show in [Performance.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/ee4acfbfbf15b3528c34a8d4f65d66097e636044/main/performance.cpp#L191), however due to its implementation it's updated every frame in [RenderingServerDefault.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/ee4acfbfbf15b3528c34a8d4f65d66097e636044/servers/rendering/rendering_server_default.cpp#L180) even when not needed.

The problem is that [vmaCalculateStatistics](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/thirdparty/vulkan/vk_mem_alloc.h#L1712) is slow (read the doc comment on it for more information).

Ideally, this function would be updated to a more efficient method, or it would only be called when required.

### Steps to reproduce

Launch a large 3d project with a lot of assets (the issue is not as visible in smaller projects)
Attach a profiler
Look at the flame graph

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

You can use the [TPS Demo](https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/678) to see this issue to a certain extent.
godotengine/godot#102173.title: RenderingDeviceDriverVulkan::get_total_memory_used() is very slow

### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4-beta1, 4.4-rc1


### System information

Godot v4.4.rc1 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

Attempting to assign a variable typed to resource class creates error message if it was accessed from an array or dictionary of another constant resource with a variable used as an index. Loading it, instead of preloading, does not have this issue.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a custom resourcesuch as:

```gdscript
class_name MyResource
extends Resource

@export var sub_resource_arr: Array[MySubResource]
@export var sub_resource_dict: Dictionary[int, MySubResource]
```

And another with:

```gdscript
class_name MySubResource
extends Resource
```

Create a new resource of that type, then attempt to assign a variable like so:

```gdscript
class_name MyClass
extends Node

const resource: MyResource = preload("res://my_resource.tres")

func _ready():
	var index: int = 0
	var sub_resource: MySubResource
	var loaded_resource: MyResource = load("res://my_resource.tres")
	sub_resource = loaded_resource.sub_resource_arr[index]
	sub_resource = resource.sub_resource_arr[index]
	sub_resource = resource.sub_resource_dict[index]
```

The editor throws an error for the last two lines in _ready().

```
Line 11:Value of type "res://my_sub_resource.gd" cannot be assigned to a variable of type "MySubResource".
Line 12:Value of type "res://my_sub_resource.gd" cannot be assigned to a variable of type "MySubResource".
```


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#103200.title: Editor cannot cast constant subresource when accessed by through variable index

### Issue description

When trying to use a hint string in export_custom to export nested Dictionaries, I am unable to figure out how to specify the type to use for the values. Anything after ```;``` seems to be ignored and the second type is always Variant. I tried many different combinations of separators and could not figure out a combination that works. I'm not even sure that any combination exists, because it seems like the ```;``` immediately ends the current type at the top level and starts a new one. This seems like something that should exist, as arrays can properly use nested types in the hint string.

### Steps to reproduce

Use the following code:
```@export_custom(PROPERTY_HINT_DICTIONARY_TYPE, "4:;27/38:3:;3:") var nested_dictionaries: Dictionary[String, Dictionary]```
The intention is that the exported variable should be a Dictionary with keys of String and values of Dictionary, and the nested Dictionary should have keys of float and values of float, but instead the nested Dictionary has values of type Variant, and anything after the second ```;``` is unused.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#104259.title: Unable to represent nested dictionary types in hint strings

godotengine/godot#105027.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3.stable, 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads)

### Issue description

CanvasItem `draw_*()` antialiasing makes lines appear thicker than intended. This is due to the feather being added to the existing line thickness, without the line thickness being reduced to compensate for the feather's width.

This is reproducible on all rendering methods.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2e07d8f5-53c8-4eff-99f0-46f11e3fe883

If you compensate for width by using a width of `0.5` instead of `1.0` when using antialiasing (for thin lines), or subtracting `1.0` to the line width (for thick lines), you get a much closer result:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6c1d0e0b-25dd-479e-b32c-9668ab449b48

We should perform this width compensaton at the RenderingServer level so that it's automatically applied, without needing to modify your project.

2D MSAA is not affected by this issue, but it's slower and not supported in Compatibility.

### Steps to reproduce

- Enable antialiasing in a CanvasItem `draw()` method that supports it by passing `true` for the `antialiased` parameter.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[custom_drawing_no_width_compensation.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19610364/custom_drawing_no_width_compensation.zip)

This is the same as https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/pull/1185, but with line width compensation edited out. Any fix should be developed using this MRP specifically.
godotengine/godot#105027.title: CanvasItem `draw_*()` antialiasing makes lines appear thicker than intended

### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5-dev2, 4.4.1-stable, 4.3-stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon 780M Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12020.7004) - AMD Ryzen 7 8745HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

When you use the **Compatibility** rendering method, set up a **TileMapLayer** and set its content using a script, and then set a **Camera2D** with a Zoom property of (0.1, 0.1).   

Run the game and you will see that there is nothing on the screen.   
But once you change the camera's zoom or position, the TileMapLayer will reappear.

This bug only occurs in **Compatibility** mode.

In my full project (which uses some UI nodes), this bug causes a crash without any warnings, and in the Minimal reproduction project provided below, it occasionally crashes in the same way, but I couldn't find a way to reproduce it.

I noticed that this bug seems to be related to the resolution of the window.  
If you set the window height to 900 and then set the zoom to (0.5, 0.5), the same bug will occur when the camera's `position.x` is at `the window width + (0~200)` or `the negative window width - (0~200)`.

godotengine/godot#107070.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1 and 4.5-dev4

### System information

Windows 10 (build 19045), NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 Ti, Core(TM) i5-10300H CPU @ 2.50GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

When using the Compatibility (and also the Mobile) renderer I was expecting to have up to 8-Omni and 8-Spot lights render on each mesh.  Designing the scene to have more small meshes, and respecting the total light limit, I still had meshes that looked unlit.  In the following procedural office scene there are 28 Omnilights (close to the floor so it's easy to see where they are) and one Spotlight and 1 directional light.  Each room is its own mesh and has at least 1 light, and the yellow rooms have 3x2 and 2x2 lights.  You can see where lights were not rendering as you might expect:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5e29a868-34e8-400c-b55e-aacc4fede71f)

Digging into the code, I found that the Compatibility and Mobile renderers both have a function named **pair_light_instances**, and in that function only the first N lights that pass the culling stage are linked.  I changed the code to keep only the closest N lights and it rendered as expected.  Then I tried changing the project settings to allow 256 lights total, with 16 per mesh and tried minimal test project, but still some meshes picked weird lights:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a93c8c9f-4e0b-4e4a-a30a-d5d1df593f0b)

I then found that **RendererSceneCull::_scene_cull** has a fixed upper limit of **MAX_INSTANCE_PAIRS = 32**, so the culling code will never consider more than 32 lights total.  I can easily change the limit up to 256, yielding this:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f945162-1664-4257-93e2-18f9c8313c61)

That isn't super efficient, however...it might be best to move the "keep the best N lights" into the culling code, and directly link them there as well.

Note that a fix for this might also fix [Issue 67508](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/67508)

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP attached below.  Go to the 3D preview.  You should see something like the following:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/96aaf5cb-1957-4b5c-b372-9d8098fe1326)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[incorrect_lights_per_mesh.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20560152/incorrect_lights_per_mesh.zip)
godotengine/godot#107070.title: Irrelevant 3D lights are chosen per mesh in Mobile and Compatibility renderers

godotengine/godot#107316.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5dev5

### System information

Debian GNU/Linux 12.10

### Issue description

Running a project in embed mode at a different resolution than defined in the project, produces AVI files with incorrect resolution and aspect ratio.

The problem is that in embed mode, the initialization of MovieWriter sometimes receives the project viewport size, and sometimes the embedded viewport size. But the  frames sent have always the embedded viewport size.

There was a similar error, same effect, different cause, that was reported [in the forum](https://forum.godotengine.org/t/movie-maker-mode-produces-strange-recordings-when-trying-to-use-a-different-resolution/62445) and #71254.

**Edit:** The issue doesn't happen only in embed game mode, it can happen at anytime if we're not careful to avoid window size changes. We should probably think of a way to handle window size changes in movie mode, implement a way to prevent them, or warn the user when it happens. If the movie writer isn't prepared to handle resolution changes, it could even make Godot crash. See comments below.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open any project.
- Make the viewport size in the Project Settings big enoough so that it has to be scaled down to fit the embedded window.
- Enable Movie Maker mode.
- Run the project.
- Check the resulting video.

This might take several tries because it doesn't always happen. In my case it happens like 4 out of 5 times. It looks like a race condition between the embedded process and the MovieWriter initialization.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any project. The viewport has to be big enough to not fit the embedded window.
godotengine/godot#107316.title: Window size changes when in Movie Mode aren't handled correctly

godotengine/godot#110417.body:
### Tested versions

v4.5.rc2

### System information

Windows 10, NVDA screen reader v2024.4.2

### Issue description

The popup/text field for renaming a node in the Scene dock is not accessible to screen readers.

When a user selects a node and presses `F2` or `Enter` to rename it, a text field appears. While typing works visually, the screen reader (NVDA) provides no spoken feedback for the characters being typed or for the text field itself. This makes it impossible for visually impaired users to verify their input or use this core feature effectively.

This appears to be a regression, as this functionality was accessible in version `4.5.dev3`.

For comparison, the search box in the "Add Node" dialog **is** accessible and provides the expected spoken feedback, suggesting this is an issue specific to the Scene dock's rename implementation.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch Godot with a screen reader (e.g., NVDA on Windows) running.
2. Create a new project or open an existing one.
3. In the **Scene** dock, select any node (e.g., the default root node).
4. Press `F2` or `Enter` to trigger the rename action.
5. Attempt to type a new name for the node.

**Expected result:** The screen reader should announce the text field is active and speak each character as it is typed.
**Actual result:** The text is updated visually in the editor, but the screen reader provides no auditory feedback, making the input "blind."

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This is an editor accessibility issue and can be reproduced in any project, including a new, empty one. A minimal reproduction project is not required.
godotengine/godot#110417.title: Accessibility: Node rename popup in Scene dock lacks screen reader feedback

godotengine/godot#110489.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.rc2

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc2 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - AMD Ryzen 7 7745HX with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 63.18 GiB memory

### Issue description

With debug/gdscript/warnings/return_value_discarded set to warning or error, using a function call as the pattern guard in a match statement triggers the warning.
For example
```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:
    match name:
        "Node" when is_inside_tree(): # (RETURN_VALUE_DISCARDED): The function "is_inside_tree()" returns a value that will be discarded if not used.
            pass
```
Ideally a pattern guard should consume the return value and not raise a warning or error.

### Steps to reproduce

Take the match statement in the previous code snippet and add it to any function in any project

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22310873/example.zip)
godotengine/godot#110489.title: `RETURN_VALUE_DISCARDED` warning raised when using any function call as `match` pattern guard

godotengine/godot#111218.body:
Godot has longer compile times than we'd like. According to previous analyses, the main cause of this is parsing of header files.

See https://benchmarks.godotengine.org/graph/build-time/ for progress on this task.

### Workaround

If you're just looking to decrease your personal compile time during development, use `scu_build=yes` with SCons:
```bash
scons scu_build=yes
```

If you're looking to help decrease the compile time for everyone, keep reading.

### Methodology

To most effectively reduce the amount of cross includes between our headers, we need metrics. I came up with two:

- **Fresh compile cost:** An estimation of the from-scratch compile time caused the header. It is calculated from adding self time across all compile units' time traces, and partial responsibility over includees.
    - Optimizing this is useful for all contributors, and anyone else compiling the engine.
- **Recompile cost:** An estimation of the potential of each file to cause recompiles. It is calculated from self time across all compile units' time traces, weighed by its size (to estimate churn potential), and partial responsibility over includees.
    - Optimizing this is useful for regular contributors and CI.

Both are mainly based on `clang --ftime-trace` and include trees (while the second one also uses file size to estimate churn potential). I've consolidated my efforts into a public tool:

https://github.com/Ivorforce/clang-project-profiler

I also used betweenness centrality in an earlier version. You can find the source code for that here:

<details>
<summary>measure_includes_centrality.py</summary>

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import io

import json
import subprocess
import os
import sys
import pathlib
import shlex
from collections import deque, defaultdict

import concurrent.futures
import multiprocessing


def graph_from_compile_entry(idx: int, entry: dict):
    directory = entry.get("directory", os.getcwd())
    command = entry.get("command")
    arguments = entry.get("arguments")

    # Prefer 'arguments' if present, otherwise split 'command'
    if arguments:
        cmd = arguments
    else:
        cmd = shlex.split(command)

    if idx % 10 == 0:
        print(f"{idx:04d}", cmd[2])

    cmd = [cmd[0]] + cmd[3:] + ["--trace-includes"]
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=directory, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    # Not sure why but it prints to stderr
    graph = parse_graph(result.stderr)

    return graph


def parse_graph(input_str: str) -> dict[str, set[str]]:
    graph: dict[str, set[str]] = defaultdict(set)
    stack: list[tuple[int, str]] = []

    for line in input_str.strip().splitlines():
        depth = len(line) - len(line.lstrip('.'))
        node = line[depth:].strip()

        # Adjust the stack to the current depth
        while stack and stack[-1][0] >= depth:
            stack.pop()

        # If there is a parent node, link it
        if stack:
            graph[stack[-1][1]].add(node)

        stack.append((depth, node))

    return dict(graph)


def betweenness_centrality(graph: dict[str, set[str]], directed: bool = False) -> dict[str, float]:
    # Include isolated nodes
    all_nodes = set(graph.keys()) | {n for nbrs in graph.values() for n in nbrs}
    graph = {n: graph.get(n, set()) for n in all_nodes}

    centrality = dict.fromkeys(graph.keys(), 0.0)

    for s in graph:
        stack = []
        predecessors = {v: [] for v in graph}
        sigma = dict.fromkeys(graph, 0.0)
        sigma[s] = 1.0
        distance = dict.fromkeys(graph, -1)
        distance[s] = 0

        queue = deque([s])
        while queue:
            v = queue.popleft()
            stack.append(v)
            for w in graph[v]:
                if distance[w] < 0:
                    queue.append(w)
                    distance[w] = distance[v] + 1
                if distance[w] == distance[v] + 1:
                    sigma[w] += sigma[v]
                    predecessors[w].append(v)

        delta = dict.fromkeys(graph, 0.0)
        while stack:
            w = stack.pop()
            for v in predecessors[w]:
                delta[v] += (sigma[v] / sigma[w]) * (1.0 + delta[w])
            if w != s:
                centrality[w] += delta[w]

    # Normalise
    n = len(graph)
    if n > 2:
        scale = 1.0 / ((n - 1) * (n - 2))
        if not directed:
            scale *= 0.5
        for v in centrality:
            centrality[v] *= scale

    return centrality

def main():
    # Default filename
    filename = "compile_commands.json"
    if len(sys.argv) > 1:
        filename = sys.argv[1]

    # Read the compile_commands.json file
    try:
        with open(filename, "r") as f:
            compile_commands = json.load(f)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error reading {filename}: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)

    print(f"Starting {len(compile_commands)} commands...")

    all_graph: dict[str, set[str]] = {}

    failure_count = 0

    executor = concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor(multiprocessing.cpu_count())
    futures = [executor.submit(graph_from_compile_entry, *item) for item in enumerate(compile_commands)]
    completed, not_completed = concurrent.futures.wait(futures)
    for future in completed:
        try:
            graph = future.result()
            for filename, targets in graph.items():
                prev_targets: set[str] = all_graph.setdefault(filename, set())
                prev_targets.update(targets)
        except:
            failure_count += 1

    centrality = betweenness_centrality(all_graph)
    centrality = { key: value for key, value in centrality.items() if key.startswith("./") and value > 0 }

    centrality_list = list(centrality.items())
    centrality_list.sort(key=lambda kv: kv[1], reverse=True)

    final_string = "\n".join(f"{kv[0]}: {kv[1]}" for kv in centrality_list)

    print(f"Done. {failure_count} object files failed to analyze.")
    result_path = pathlib.Path("./betweenness-centrality.txt")
    result_path.write_text(final_string)

    print(result_path.absolute())

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

</details>

## How to contribute

You can contribute by picking out any of the above files, and investigating its contents, includes, and includers. Some ideas:

- Can any include be removed? Prioritize includees that also appear on the list, otherwise your change might not be impactful.
- Can some includers be changed not to include this file? Prioritize includers that also appear on the list, otherwise your change might not be impactful.
- Can this file be split into independent headers with less contents or includes?

If you believe there's nothing that can be improved about a header, please comment this on this tracker.

If you manage to remove the include, recompile the engine. It is likely that some files will error on compile, because they're missing includes. In small numbers, this is expected. However, if too many files fail to compile, it may be time to reconsider: This may mean that the include was logical after all, and most files that includes the header you were trying to improve also needs to include the other include you removed.

In some cases, you may want to guard against regressions. As a guideline, you should guard against a regression if it is illogical for the header to need to include the file / type (and you expect it to happen by accident). To do this, use `STATIC_ASSERT_INCOMPLETE_TYPE` in the associated `.cpp` file of the header. You can find some examples of how it's done in the repository.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/6d33ad2917fd82df63861aa66e3a780633d03450/core/typedefs.h#L453-L458

Finally, if you feel comfortable with your changes, submit a PR, and link back to this tracker. With enough of these kinds of PRs, hopefully we can decrease Godot's compile time to a minimum.

godotengine/godot#111741.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in godot 4.6.dev [25981be] and later

### System information

NixOS - Godot v4.6.dev 

### Issue description

With the “Insert at current time” toggle enabled (added in [PR #107511](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107511)), inserting keyframes repeatedly at the same timeline position creates multiple keys with tiny time offsets (like 1.00, 1.01, 1.02…).

This happens because the insertion logic shifts the key slightly instead of overwriting the existing one. While this prevents data loss, it leads to clutter and isn’t what most users would expect.

Ideally, it should either:

* Overwrite the existing key at that position
or
* Skip insertion and show a warning or a message

Here’s a quick demo of the issue:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/08c9a019-a164-40da-9762-feb9783d3fcf


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new AnimationPlayer.
2. Enable “Insert at current time”.
3. Move the timeline cursor to a specific time (e.g. 1.0).
4. Press the insert key multiple times.

You'll see multiple keyframes stacked very close together.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#111741.title: Key insertion at timeline cursor creates offset duplicates

godotengine/godot#111993.body:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4+

### System information

Any system

### Issue description

Unlike Godot Physics, the Jolt module does not currently support wind or gravity overrides to a [SoftBody3D](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/classes/class_softbody3d.html#class-softbody3d) from an [Area3D](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/classes/class_area3d.html#class-area3d).

Jolt internally does support detecting interactions through the `SoftBodyContactListener::OnSoftBodyContactAdded` callback. Overlapping areas can be queried through the `SoftBodyManifold::GetNumSensorContacts / GetSensorContactBodyID` interfaces.

See the example: https://github.com/jrouwe/JoltPhysics/blob/2bce8a6bdcafdf6b1140a080ebedcfbcdba74313/Samples/Tests/SoftBody/SoftBodySensorTest.cpp#L58-L68

There is already an entry point (DEBUG only) for this in the Jolt module that can be extended to keep track of which areas a soft body overlaps with:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/084d5d407e62efcd5be9de44148c5dedce3b9386/modules/jolt_physics/spaces/jolt_contact_listener_3d.cpp#L81-L87

Note: When this is fixed, also update the documentation and remove [this section](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/tutorials/physics/using_jolt_physics.html#area3d-and-softbody3d).

### Steps to reproduce

Create an Area3D with a SoftBody3D inside, override gravity and wind on the Area3D and observe the SoftBody3D not reacting when Jolt Physics is enabled.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[soft-body-vs-area.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23124751/soft-body-vs-area.zip)

Note: Switch to GodotPhysics3D to see wind + gravity interaction.
godotengine/godot#111993.title: Feature parity Jolt Physics: Add support for interactions between Area3D and SoftBody3D

### Tested versions

### System information

### Issue description

When dragging a not-selected node into the inspector, the inspector stays on the old object (good!) but the tree selection goes to the new object (bad!). This puts the inspector in a confusing state, because what is now selected in the scene tree is not showing in the inspector. To add insult to injury, clicking on the now newly selected object in the tree again also does not select that object in the inspector.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f5054634-07e9-4d6f-9069-39265252046d

The expected behavior is that the selection in the tree returns to the object that is being selected – primarily because that's what the inspector is showing.

### Steps to reproduce

Select an object from the scene tree and drag another object from the tree anywhere.

godotengine/godot#112469.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.6.dev3 (9d84f3d13) - Not reproducible in 4.6.dev2 (7864ac801) - Regression from #110077 ### System information Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon 780M Graphics (RADV PHOENIX) - AMD Ryzen 7 8845HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads) - 60.60 GiB memory ### Issue description #110077 changed the default value for `Environment.glow_hdr_threshold` from 1.0 to 0.0, and this changes drastically how glow looks like. So this breaks compat and existing projects, such as Kenney starter kits: https://github.com/KenneyNL/Starter-Kit-3D-Platformer 4.6-dev2: 4.6-dev3: 4.6-dev3 with `Environment.glow_hdr_threshold` set back to 1.0 in the WorldEnvironment: I'm not sure that default value change was intentional in #110077, or if it was a debug change that slipped through. The documentation hasn't been adjusted for it either, inferring that good defaults are 1.0 for Forward+ and 0.9 for Mobile: ``` The lower threshold of the HDR glow. When using the Mobile rendering method (which only supports a lower dynamic range up to [code]2.0[/code]), this may need to be below [code]1.0[/code] for glow to be visible. A value of [code]0.9[/code] works well in this case. This value also needs to be decreased below [code]1.0[/code] when using glow in 2D, as 2D rendering is performed in SDR. ``` The issue is reproducible with all 3 rendering methods in the Kenney 3D Platformer. ### Steps to reproduce - Run https://github.com/KenneyNL/Starter-Kit-3D-Platformer ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) https://github.com/KenneyNL/Starter-Kit-3D-Platformer is not minimal but should be simple enough to debug.
godotengine/godot#112469.title: Glow behavior compatibility breakage due to changing default `Environment.glow_hdr_threshold` to 0 (regression from #110077)

godotengine/godot#112472.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3.stable, 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.1.stable, 4.6.dev [eb3d6d8cd35df40df0a7f12a33917b65a96a6518]

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (eb3d6d8cd) - Fedora Linux 42 (Adams) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

This might be an outright duplicate of #105854, but I figured since this relates more to scene inheritance, and actually results in broken layout, it might warrant its own issue.

I don't fully understand this issue, but basically, the issue of "Custom" anchor presets being displayed as some other random preset is more than just an inspector quirk, as inherited scenes seem to also believe that its base scene has this other random preset when they're serialized, causing property values to first be omitted, and then when you save it again you end up actually serializing the random preset that it thinks it's inheriting, causing layout issues.

The MRP and repro steps should hopefully make things clearer.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open `my_base_scene.tscn` from the MRP in a text editor
2. Note how `Label` has a custom anchors preset (`anchors_preset = -1`)
3. Open the project in Godot
4. Open `my_base_scene.tscn`
5. Note how `Label` incorrectly displays its "Anchors Preset" as "Top Left"
6. Note how `Label` is sized and positioned
7. Open `my_inherited_scene.tscn`
8. Note how `Label` is positioned differently
9. Note how `Label` still reports having "Top Left" as its "Anchors Preset"
10. Set `Label` in `my_inherited_scene.tscn` to use "Custom" as its "Anchors Preset" and set its "Anchor Offsets" to be the same values as what they are in `my_base_scene.tscn`, i.e. -80, -80, 80, 80 respectively.
11. Note how the positioning now lines up with `my_base_scene.tscn`
12. Save `my_inherited_scene.tscn`
13. Close and re-open `my_inherited_scene.tscn`
14. Save `my_inherited_scene.tscn` again
15. Close and re-open `my_inherited_scene.tscn` again
16. Note how the positioning is now incorrect again

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[scene-inheritance-anchors.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23395069/scene-inheritance-anchors.zip)
godotengine/godot#112472.title: "Custom" anchors preset is not recognized as such by inherited scenes, causing serialization problems

godotengine/godot#113854.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.5.1.stable.stable.steam.f62fdbde1 ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable (f62fdbde1) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7628) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3470 CPU @ 3.20GHz (4 threads) - 31.96 GiB memory ### Issue description When draw_multiline_string()'s width is set to -1, HorizontalAlignment flag is ignored. Based on #80163, > -1 is passed as the width so the exact width needed for drawing the text is used and hence there's no space left for the horizontal alignment While it makes sense for draw_string() that there can be no alignment if the string's full width is used, for draw_multiline_string() this appears to not be true. Actual behavior with HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_LEFT, HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_CENTER, and HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_RIGHT: If -1 results in the bounding box taking the full width, I can clearly see that the first line has plenty of space on the right remaining. Expected behavior: Workaround: ```gdscript draw_multiline_string(font, Vector2(32, 60*4), "GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_LEFT, font.get_multiline_string_size("GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_LEFT, -1, get_theme_default_font_size()).x, ) draw_multiline_string(font, Vector2(32, 60*5), "GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_CENTER, font.get_multiline_string_size("GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_CENTER, -1, get_theme_default_font_size()).x, ) draw_multiline_string(font, Vector2(32, 60*6), "GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_RIGHT, font.get_multiline_string_size("GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_RIGHT, -1, get_theme_default_font_size()).x, ) ``` If -1 means taking the full width of the text area, then alignment should see the bounding box. If -1 means that width is just not calculated (and cropping remains disabled), it feels kind of counterintuitive. ### Steps to reproduce ```gdscript draw_multiline_string(font, Vector2(32, 60*1), "GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_LEFT, -1) draw_multiline_string(font, Vector2(32, 60*2), "GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_CENTER, -1) draw_multiline_string(font, Vector2(32, 60*3), "GODOT\nGODOTGODOT", HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_RIGHT, -1) ``` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [bug-demo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24083174/bug-demo.zip)
godotengine/godot#113854.title: width -1 for draw_multiline_string makes the alignment be ignored

godotengine/godot#113952.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in Godot 4.4 onward ### System information Any with Mobile or Forward+ renderers. Does NOT reproduce with Compatibility renderer. ### Issue description When the [LinearToSRGB ColorFunc](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97388) was added to visual shaders, clamping of values to the range of [0, 1] was included in the conversion. This is not necessary for nonlinear sRGB encoding and if this clamping is desired by the user it can be applied quite simply by using the existing `Clamp` node. Additionally, this clamping behaviour does NOT happen with the Compatibility renderer; the clamping behaviour of LinearToSRGB is inconsistent between these renderers. This issue primarily affects projects where "overbright"/HDR values should be maintained when performing operations with the LinearToSRGB ColorFunc. Some examples are when HDR 2D is enabled on the project and tonemapping or [HDR output](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/94496) is used. As an example, let's say I have a shader that blends nicely with HDR 2D disabled (first row of the table). I want to maintain the same appearance, but with HDR 2D enabled so that I can get the benefits of HDR output or better internal HDR fidelity. I can simply rewrite the existing shader with the LinearToSRGB visual shader node to produce the same appearance when HDR 2D is enabled (second row of the table): | Configuration | Visual Shader | Result --|--|-- HDR 2D disabled | | HDR 2D enabled | | So far so good! But the reason that I switched to HDR 2D was to get higher HDR fidelity and/or HDR output. So when I make the background image extra-bright and apply a tonemapper, suddenly the blended object makes my HDR background clip. This is because of the clipping that is included in the LinearToSRGB node. If I modify Godot to exclude this clipping from the node, I get the expected behaviour shown here: | Problem: background clouds are clipped | Expected: background clouds remain overbright for good tonemapping --|-- | ### Steps to reproduce 1. Use the LinearToSRGB ColorFunc visual shader node with an overbright or negative input 2. Note that the output has clamped the values to [0, 1] ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [hdr-srgb-blending.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24133874/hdr-srgb-blending.zip)
godotengine/godot#113952.title: Visual shader LinearToSRGB ColorFunc causes undesirable clamping

godotengine/godot#114130.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1] and v4.6.beta.custom_build [79603b2f2]
- Only reproducible when opening the project in the editor, builds don't have this problem
- Only reproducible if `.godot` folder is not present, when it's the first time you open the project

### System information

macOS 15.6.1

### Issue description

In a project that has a script written in a custom scripting language (implementations of `ScriptLanguageExtension` registered via `Engine::register_script_language`), when opening the editor for the first time some methods of the `ScriptLanguageExtension` are called before its `_init` method.

In my case, `LuaScriptingLanguage` from [Lua GDExtension](https://github.com/gilzoide/lua-gdextension),  when opening the project in the editor for the first time, some methods such as `_get_global_class_name` (if any Lua script is present) or `_add_named_global_constant` (when there are Autoloads registered) are called before `_init`, leading to crashes due to the language being uninitialized.

The fact that the GDExtension is being called correctly means it has already been initialized before the script is being loaded in this first filesystem scan. Only running `ScriptLanguageExtension::_init` seem to be missing from this flow.

Sure, I could move all initialization code to the instance's constructor as a workaround, but when there's a method called `_init` this just doesn't feel right at all. And possibly other scripting language devs may be seeing the same problem.

In the attached MRP repo's README there are 2 symbolicated stacktraces of the crash I'm seeing (one for v4.5.1-stable and the other in a very recent commit).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clone the reproduction project
2. Make sure `.godot` folder doesn't exist (remove it if reattempting to reproduce the crash)
3. Open the project in the editor

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

https://github.com/gilzoide/godot-script-language-extension-crash-project
godotengine/godot#114130.title: `ScriptLanguageExtension` methods are called before its `_init` when opening the editor for the first time

godotengine/godot#114224.body:
### Tested versions

- reproducible in 28bd72a7e1146a2de409

This can be worked around in gdscript by forcing a RID change:

```
	# Force swapchain recreation by temporarily disconnecting the viewport.
	# The OpenXR composition layer's set_viewport() only updates viewport_size when
	# the viewport RID changes. Setting layer_viewport to null then back to the
	# viewport forces the RID to change, which triggers the size update in
	# openxr_composition_layer_extension.cpp. The swapchain is then recreated in
	# update_and_acquire_swapchain() when it detects swapchain_size != viewport_size.
	comp_layer.layer_viewport = null
	get_tree().create_timer(0.1).timeout.connect(func() -> void:
		comp_layer.layer_viewport = viewport
	)
```

### System information

Quest 3

### Issue description

I have quad layers that change size when buttons become visible or invisible, etc. The OpenXRCompositionLayer does not reallocate its swapchain when this happens unless I employ the above workaround.

### Steps to reproduce

Change the resolution of a SubViewport that an OpenXRCompositionLayerQuad is displaying dynamically, and you'll see that the quad resolution does not change along with it.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Don't have a minimal reproduction project, but I think you can see the problem by inspection as indicated in the commented gdscript above.
godotengine/godot#114224.title: OpenXRCompositionLayer does not handle layer_viewport resolution changes

godotengine/godot#114432.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.4.rc2.official [01545c995]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable unknown - Windows 11 (build 22621) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.3713) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13620H (16 threads)

### Issue description

SDFGI ignores visual layers of visual instances and currently active camera, if a visual instance is not visible to the camera due to incompatible layers, it will still be used by SDFGI

### Steps to reproduce

Create a scene with SDFGI

godotengine/godot#114619.body:
### Tested versions - Reproducible in: v4.6.beta.custom_build [8dcf5b4eaf] (latest master) - Not reproducible in: v4.6.beta2.official [551ce8d47] ### System information Godot v4.6.beta2 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 580 2048SP (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 31.0.21923.11000) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.91 GiB memory ### Issue description After PR #114330 when you attempt to import an existing project with editor settings for create a new project as Forward or Mobile renderer, the confirmation button to import will be always disabled, even with a valid project selected. This happens because #114330 rely on a variable that's only setted when you click on the `+Create` button for create a new project. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/32e355bd-2cbe-4284-9eda-eed35a8e4984
When the Project Manager starts, `ProjectDialog::_renderer_selected()` is called and based in the default renderer setted in editor, `rd_error` will be setted using the inverse value of `rendering_device_supported`. https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/8dcf5b4eafff013b7d7022c5c1b3e33dc15e5947/editor/project_manager/project_dialog.cpp#L483-L526
The problem is `rendering_device_supported` default value is `false` and it's only setted when you click on the `+Create` button, so in `ProjectDialog::_renderer_selected()` `rd_error` will be always `true` and this will disable the confirmation button unless your default renderer is Compatibility or until you open the New Project dialog at least once. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Make sure your editor settings is setted to create a new project with `Forward` or `Mobile` renderer (Settings -> Edit All Settings -> Project Manager -> Default Renderer) 2. Attempt to import any existing project ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A
godotengine/godot#114619.title: Project import confirmation button stays disabled even with a valid project selected

godotengine/godot#114920.body:
### Tested versions

 Godot version
4.5





### System information

Android - Android 15 - Vulkan/Mobile

### Issue description

When an Android plugin emits a signal using `GodotPlugin.emitSignal()` and passes a `java.util.HashMap` (or `java.util.Map`) as a parameter, the game crashes immediately.
The JNI layer attempts to call a method named `get_keys()` on the Java object, which is a method specific to Godot's internal `GodotDictionary` class, not Java's standard `HashMap`.
**Error Log:**
```text
Pending exception java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: no non-static method "Ljava/util/HashMap;.get_keys()[Ljava/lang/String;"
JNI DETECTED ERROR IN APPLICATION: JNI CallObjectMethodV called with pending exception java.lang.NoSuchMethodError

 **source code:**
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4.5/platform/android/jni_utils.cpp#L448
```


### Steps to reproduce

Create an Android plugin extending GodotPlugin.

Emit a signal with a HashMap as an argument:
java
Map<String, Object> map = new HashMap<>();
map.put("key", "value");
emitSignal("my_signal", map); // Crash occurs here

The application crashes with a SIGABRT.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A (Simple code snippet provided above is sufficient).
godotengine/godot#114920.title: Android: Crash when emitting signal with java.util.HashMap (NoSuchMethodError: get_keys)

godotengine/godot#115009.body:
### Tested versions

Version: v4.5.1.stable.mono.official [f62fdbde1]

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8129) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

Using `HeightMapShape3D` for collision causes rapid wobbling / bouncing look when the parent CollisionShape3D is scaled down (scaled down to 0.25 for my case). According to the [latest ](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/classes/class_heightmapshape3d.html) docs, scaling the parent CollisionShape3D is the only way to adjust the scale of HeightMapShape3D.

This seems to make HeightMapShape3D completely unusable if you need any scale less than 1 as the "resolution" cannot be adjusted by any other means (that I can find, the docs are quite lacking for this object).

This happens with both Jolt physics and Godot physics, but it's worse with Godot as the object never seems to settle, whereas jolt just wobbles for a moment then stops, but both give a very undesirable look.

Edit: Tested a little more. Scaling to 2 results in the cube settling unevenly, 1.25 does similar. So it seems even scaling up, and scaling by a factor of 1 also does not behave right.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a CollisionShape3D and use a HeightMapShape3D as the shape, any size seems to work, and the data can all be default (all 0 / completely flat).

Create a RigidBody3D with a cube shape and drop it so it collides at an angle with the HeightMapShape3D. If it falls perfectly flat it does not seem to happen so must tilt the cube a bit.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[height-map-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24655288/height-map-test.zip)

Simply run it and you should see it wobble rapidly before it stops moving, change the scale of the CollisionShape3D back to 1 makes it work normally again.
godotengine/godot#115009.title: HeightMapShape3D physics breaks when scaled down
