godotengine/godot#115095.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.6.rc1.mono.official [481f36ed2], v4.5.1.stable.mono.official [f62fdbde1]

### System information

Linux

### Issue description

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dcad4c13-a31e-45d1-a2aa-7fdf915dd06f

It is not possible to transition nicely from one animation to another whilst `AnimationNodeOneshot` is processing its `fadeout` time.

It is too late to change the `oneshot` animation to a new animation and have the oneshot continue.
If I change the `oneshot` animation before fadeout, it will wait until the new `oneshot` animation has finished before fading to the `in` animation.

This means I need to request the oneshot node to fire again. However if I request the oneshot node to fire during its fadeout time, it very briefly displays the `in` animation.
I presume this is because it needs to finish its fadeout before it can begin fading into a new oneshot, so it processes the fadeout immediately and then the fadein.

This forces the new animation `oneshot` to blend with the `in` animation, rather than its previous animation.

Currently I just check if the oneshot is currently fading out (internal_active == false and active == true means the oneshot is fading out), and if so I set the fadein to 0.
This way it doesn't display the `in` animation because it immediately blends to the `oneshot` animation, but it removes my ability to blend the new animation.

I would love to know if there are some alternative solutions I could use so I can transition more smoothly during oneshot fadeout.


What I would expect to happen, is when I fire the oneshot during fadeout it would stop fading out and begin fading in against the previous oneshot.

The only solution I found that somewhat works, is to duplicate all my oneshot nodes, and during fadeout I use the secondary oneshot node, but this is too hacky in my opinion.

### Steps to reproduce

On the MRP press Mouse1 to begin the animation. It will fade into the animation, and fadeout to the `RESET` animation.

If you press mouse1 during the animation it will not reset, but if you press mouse1 during the `ACTIVE(?)` window (which is when the oneshot is fading out), it will briefly show the `RESET` animation before the new oneshot animation.

You can disable the fadein using the toggle button, which will remove the glitchy back and forth teleportation of the progress bar, but also removes fading into the new animation.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[one-shot-fadeout-skip.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24696049/one-shot-fadeout-skip.zip)
godotengine/godot#115095.title: `AnimationNodeOneShot` displays `in` animation briefly when requesting fire during fadeout time

godotengine/godot#115169.body:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.6 rc 1 (default physics engine is Jolt) -> affected
Godot 4.5.1 (default physics engine is Godot physics) -> unaffected
Godot 4.5.1, manually set the physics engine to Jolt -> affected

### System information

N/A

### Issue description

Set up a simple mechanical system and disable dissipative forces (drag). Run the simulations. Very quickly things start getting out of control: the energy flows into the system over time, making amplitudes larger.

### Steps to reproduce

Just an example:

* Create the ground (static body with a world boundary collision)
* Create the ball (rigid body with a spherical collision)
* Move the ball above the ground
* Add a physical material to the ball, override its bounce to 1.0
* Set the linear and angular damp modes for the ball to `Replace`
* Set up the rest of the scene to your liking, launch and watch

Alternatively, download the MRP below and just click play.

What I would expect: energy is conserved, the ball reaches the same height with each bounce. This is exactly the behavior I see in 4.5.1 + Godot physics.

The new (Jolt) behavior: the ball jumps higher and higher every time. Energy seems to flow into the system from somewhere.

I **no not** believe this can be explained by issues with the numerical precision. To my knowledge, both Jolt and Godot physics use the 0-th order integrator, which is prone to numerical precision loss, however, the Godot physics powered simulation seems to be much more stable. This effect seems to be too severe and the system too simple for precision loss to explain it.

I am also not sure whether this is a problem with Jolt itself, or with the Godot-Jolt integration, though I'm leaning towards the latter.

What I want to test next: playing with `_integrate_forces` and seeing if a 4-th order (Runge-Kutta) integrator would solve this. I have not tried this yet,  will update here if/when I do.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test_46.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24734612/test_46.zip)
godotengine/godot#115169.title: Jolt has run-away energy production in simple mechanical system

godotengine/godot#115232.body:
- *Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/115233.*

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.rc2

### System information

Godot v4.6.rc (76addfff1) - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 580.119.02) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

Engine Compilation Configuration Editor disables too many classes when detecting from project:

- GridMap is disabled, even though the project needs it.
- Several core classes are disabled, which causes compilation to fail until you enable them (see the list below).

List of classes I had to manually remove from the file to get the [3D platformer demo](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/tree/master/3d/platformer) to compile and run correctly:

```diff
@@ -102,7 +102,6 @@
                "FogVolume",
                "FoldableContainer",
                "FoldableGroup",
-               "Font",
                "FramebufferCacheRD",
                "GDExtension",
                "GDExtensionManager",
@@ -136,7 +135,6 @@
```

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.rc2

### System information

Godot v4.6.rc (76addfff1) - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 580.119.02) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

Engine Compilation Configuration Editor does not detect Navigation 2D being unused.

This can be reproduced on the [3D platformer demo](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/tree/master/3d/platformer), or even on an empty project. Neither use 2D navigation functionality, but autodetection will still consider it as if it was used somewhere.

godotengine/godot#115318.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in 4.6.rc1

### System information

Godot v4.6.rc1 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7628) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7500 CPU @ 3.40GHz (4 threads) - 15.96 GiB memory

### Issue description

When calling `FABRIK3D.set_target_node`, there's no warning or error when set to a `NodePath` that does not exist. I'm not sure what the desired behaviour should be (it's my first issue), but I assume there should be some sort of user feedback, so somebody doesn't spend an hour on a very hot day trying to figure out why only some targets are working.

### Steps to reproduce

I have attached an MRP that has an arm that points at two targets, then attempts to point to a third with an invalid name.

Otherwise, to replicate from scratch:
1. Create a Skeleton3D
2. Add a FABRIK3D node and add a setting for your bone chain.
3. Add a target to that setting and see how the IK points to the target.
4. In code, call `FABRIK3D.set_target_node(0, ^"invalid_node_path")` and see how the `Target Node` setting is set to the invalid `NodePath`, but there is no change to the bone positions, and no error or warning shown to the user.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp-fabrik-ik-node-path-validity.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24832491/mrp-fabrik-ik-node-path-validity.zip)
godotengine/godot#115318.title: FABRIK3D has no feedback to the user when an invalid NodePath is used as a target node

godotengine/godot#115322.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5 beta 4
- Not reproducible in 4.5 beta 3

Bisected to #108544

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (5a16e2fc7) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors @ 180Hz - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6614) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-10600KF CPU @ 4.10GHz (12 threads) - 15.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

RigidBody3Ds sometimes ignore updates to their transform made immediately after being instantiated.
Only seems to happen if spawned from _input event e.g.
```gdscript
func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event.is_action_pressed("ui_accept"):
		var ball = TEMPLATE.instantiate()
		add_child(ball)
		ball.position = Vector3(2,0,0)
```

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa3f0d1f-8dd5-408b-8660-24b4fe76ac49

Workaround: defer the positioning of the body with set/call_deferred

### Steps to reproduce

A rigidbody can ignore initial updates to transform if _all_ of the following criteria are met:

- Instantiation/positioning happens in the _input event
- A VehicleBody3D _with_ a VehicleWheel3D child is present in the scene
- Jolt is being used for physics

Lower framerates make the positioning fail more often

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[origin-error-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24833674/origin-error-mrp.zip)
Press space to spawn a rigid body, or tick Auto spawn to continually spawn rigid bodies.
Tick VehicleBodyFrozen to freeze the invisible vehicle body in scene to see the issue disappear
godotengine/godot#115322.title: Jolt RigidBody can ignore transform updates in _input method when VehicleBody and Wheel present

godotengine/godot#115370.body:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.6 RC2



### System information

Windows 11 - Godot 4.6 RC2

### Issue description

When using the Next/Previous Match arrows in the Script Editor search bar, the selection doesn't follow a sequential order. For example, it might jump from match 2 directly to match 5, skipping intermediate results. This erratic behavior makes it difficult to navigate through specific occurrences of a string.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c6a153b5-839b-4e8c-bbbb-3a4154cdeeb1

### Steps to reproduce

Open any GDScript file with multiple occurrences of the same word (at least 5-10 occurrences spread across different lines).

Press Ctrl + F to open the search bar.

Type the word you want to find.

Click the "Next Match" (Down arrow) repeatedly.

Observe the match counter (e.g., "2/10").

Notice that clicking "Next" sometimes jumps the counter and the cursor to a non-sequential match (e.g., jumping from 2/10 to 5/10).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Create a new project and add a single script file (e.g., bug_reproduction.gd).

Paste a block of text where the same keyword appears frequently (at least 10-15 times) across different lines.

Attempt to cycle through the matches using the search arrows.
godotengine/godot#115370.title: Search arrows are not working as expected

godotengine/godot#115376.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.1, 4.4, 4.3, 4.2, 4.2-dev2
- Not reproducible in: 4.2-dev1 and earlier versions
- Not reproducible in: 4.5.1 - Web

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9144) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700H (20 threads) - 63.70 Gio memory

### Issue description

When clicking on a button to change scene with a touchscreen, the following error appears in the debugger : 

```
E 0:00:02:384   can_process: Condition "!is_inside_tree()" is true. Returning: false
  <Source C++>  scene/main/node.cpp:889 @ can_process()

```

This error points to the source file line :  [https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/f62fdbde15035c5576dad93e586201f4d41ef0cb/scene/main/node.cpp#L889](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/f62fdbde15035c5576dad93e586201f4d41ef0cb/scene/main/node.cpp#L889)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a scene with a Node2D
2. Create another scene woth a Control node
3. Attach a script to the Control node
4. Add a child Button node and make it bigger to be able to click on it
5. Connect the on_pressed signal to the script
7. Add the following code to the method
```gdscript
get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://node_2d.tscn")
```
8. Play the control.tscn scene
9. Click on the button using the touchscreen

The error should appear in debugger console.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp-v4.5.1.stable.official-\[f62fdbde1\].zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24849696/mrp-v4.5.1.stable.official-.f62fdbde1.zip)

Changing the game speed to anything other than 1.0x and closing the game by hitting X will cause the game speed text to show the number set before closing the game (Actual game speed will still be 1.0x).

### Steps to reproduce

5. Close the game by hitting the X button (Not by clicking "Stop running project")

Actual result: The text on the game speed will be the same as before closing it (1/4x)

godotengine/godot#115406.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.6 stable on Linux (deb)

### System information

Linux Mint 22.3 Cinnamon 6.6.5 64-bit 

### Issue description


When toggling off and on the screenreader Godot editor crashes with the following message in the terminal:  
```
thread '<unnamed>' (40825) panicked at /home/runner/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/accesskit_consumer-0.31.0/src/tree.rs:136:13:
TreeUpdate includes 11 nodes which are neither in the current tree nor a child of another node from the update: [#648372557971611, #648763399995638, #648376852938908, #648776284897529, #659681206864453, #659685501831750, #648445572415660, #648389737840799, #648385442873502, #648368263004314 ...]
```

From what I could gather, accesskit is receiving orphaned nodes, panics, then kills Godot. So I guess there are two issues:  
1: Godot is sending malformed/partial updates when accesskit no longer has the full tree
2: I don't think Godot should crash if accesskit does

System information:
Linux Mint 22.3 Cinnamon 6.6.5 64-bit
Linux Kernel 6.8.0-90-generic

Processor: Intel© Core™ i5-8300H CPU @ 2.30GHz × 4
Graphics card: Intel Corporation CoffeeLake-H GT2 [UHD Graphics 630]
Graphics card: NVIDIA Corporation GP107M [GeForce GTX 1050 Mobile]

### Steps to reproduce

- Open Godot 4.6-stable (linux), doesn't even have to be a project just the project overview
- Turn on the screenreader
- Turn off the screenreader
- Turn on the screenreader

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#115406.title: Linux Godot 4.6-stable accesskit crash after toggling screenreader

### Tested versions

tested on windows 10 in Godot 4.6 stable mono using d3d12. issue doesn't occur when using vulkan.

### System information

OS: Windows 10 | Version: Godot 4.6 stable mono | GPU: Nvidia RTX 3070Ti | rendering driver: d3d12

### Issue description

while using d3d12 as the rendering driver, setting a material's filter option to "nearest mipmap anisotropic" behaves like it's set to linear. "nearest" and "nearest mipmap" still behave as expected.
the material is expected to be pixelated, which is the case when using vulkan. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. enable d3d12 as rendering driver
2. set a material's filter mode to nearest mipmap anisotropic

godotengine/godot#115427.body:
### Tested versions

4.6.1-rc

### System information

Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
SCons 4.10.1
clang 1:14.0-55~exp2
gcc 4:11.2.0-1ubuntu1

### Issue description

When compiling from source with scons, I get the following compilation error.

```
drivers/unix/file_access_unix_pipe.cpp:47:27: error: expected initializer before '*' token
   47 | typedef typeof(void(int)) *sighandler_t;
      |                           ^
[ 99%] scons: *** [bin/obj/drivers/unix/file_access_unix_pipe.linuxbsd.editor.x86_64.o] Error 1
```

The strange thing is sighandler_t is already defined somewhere else. If I delete these three lines, **I can successfully compile.**

```C++
#ifndef sighandler_t
typedef typeof(void(int)) *sighandler_t;
#endif
```

### Steps to reproduce

Compilation with scons (platform linuxbsd, architecture x86-64, target editor)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

godotengine/godot#115431.body: ### Tested versions Works fine from v4.0.stable.official [92bee43ad] to v4.6.dev2.official [7864ac801] Performance issues found in v4.6.dev3.official [9d84f3d13] Bisected back to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111183 ### System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700K CPU @ 3.80GHz (16 threads) - 31.91 GiB memory ### Issue description Extreme performance drop when drawing many CanvasItems with `canvas_item_add_texture_rect_region` calls. 4.6dev2: 4.6dev3: I had read about D3D12 becoming default in the 4.6 patch notes and tested my game with it. Noticed a large performance difference compared to Vulkan and bisected back to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111183. ### Steps to reproduce Here's a tiny script you can paste on the root `Node2D` of a new scene in a new project that has `icon.svg`. Run it in the affected versions on Vulkan then on D3D12. ```gdscript extends Node2D func _ready() -> void: RenderingServer.viewport_set_measure_render_time(get_viewport().get_viewport_rid(),true) var bounds := get_viewport().get_visible_rect() for i in 20000: var rid := RenderingServer.canvas_item_create() RenderingServer.canvas_item_add_texture_rect_region( rid, Rect2( Vector2( -16, -16 ), Vector2( 32, 32 ) ), preload("res://icon.svg").get_rid(), Rect2( Vector2( 32, 32 ), Vector2( 32, 32 ) ) ) RenderingServer.canvas_item_set_modulate( rid, Color( randf(), randf(), randf() ) ) RenderingServer.canvas_item_set_transform( rid, Transform2D.IDENTITY.scaled( Vector2.ONE * randf() ).rotated( randf() * TAU ).translated( Vector2( randf_range( bounds.position.x, bounds.end.x ), randf_range( bounds.position.y, bounds.end.y ) ) ) ) RenderingServer.canvas_item_set_parent( rid, get_canvas_item() ) func _process( _delta ) -> void: print(Engine.get_frames_per_second()) print(RenderingServer.viewport_get_measured_render_time_cpu(get_viewport().get_viewport_rid()) * 1000) ``` Alternatively you can run the more involved MRP attached below and left click to switch between drawing manually and using CanvasItems. Switch between D3D12 and Vulkan in Project Settings between runs to see the performance difference. No code changes are required. There's an alternate script (`scene_alt.gd`) available that avoids `canvas_item_add_texture_rect_region` by using two textures instead and the performance with Vulkan is actually better going from 18 draw calls to 24000+ than using region rect. To run it drag and drop `scene_alt.gd` from the file browser onto the root node of `scene.tscn`, save, and run the project again. Observe draw calls going up to 24000+ to ensure it worked. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [vulkan-cpu-time-mrp_v.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24877685/vulkan-cpu-time-mrp_v.zip)
godotengine/godot#115431.title: Vulkan CanvasItem texture rect region performance regression in 4.6

After upgrading to `Godot 4.6.0.stable.mono (Windows 11)` I've been getting **frequent crashes** when building and running my game. I haven't been able to find any open/closed issues on this, and this is my first Godot C# project, so this could be something I haven't seen before (but others have). ### System information Godot v4.6.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8180) - AMD Ryzen 7 7700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.14 GiB memory ### Issue description When running my game in Godot (the application build completes correctly in my code editor with `dotnet clean && dotnet build`), I will occasionally get a crash (this has happened maybe 5 times in as many hours). This output in the code usually indicates the game is about to crash and close out the Godot editor, but not always. ### Steps to reproduce I don't know why this error occurs or what can reproduce it, as it appears to be intermittent. I do have UI dialogs (confirmation/accept dialogs), but I'm not sure if that message of: `editor/gui/progress_dialog.cpp` refers to those UI elemtents. Note: I haven't written any custom C++ code, so this is likely coming from Godot's core? ## TEMPORARY WORKAROUND: For those looking for a workaround for this until a full release comes, @akien-mga has kindly created a stable build: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/115460#issuecomment-3834523678 ``` I've made a test build of 4.6-stable + https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106299 reverted, for Windows, macOS and Linux editors: https://github.com/akien-mga/godot/releases/tag/4.6-stable-fix-115460 ```

godotengine/godot#115464.body: ### Tested versions Godot Engine v4.5.1.stable.arch_linux ### System information OS: Arch Linux 6.17.9; GPU: NVIDIA Corporation TU116M [GeForce GTX 1660 Ti Mobile] (rev a1); Drivers: nvidia-dkms 580.105.08-5 ### Issue description Built-in functions description in GDshader says that functions `min` and `max` must return integer value when both arguments are int. For example `min(1,2)` must return `1` and `1` will be integer, so this code must be correct: `int foo = max(1,bar); //bar is int`. But for some reason `max` and `min` (**and probably some other functions**) returns float and error "can't assing 'float' to 'int'" appears until `max/min` wraped with `int`: `int foo = int(max(1,bar)); //bar is int`. I even "force casted" function arguments to int but got the same result: In my opinion it's not a bug, caue GLSL have no bult in `min/max` ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create new project 2. Create new gdshader 3. Type `min(` in fragment part of the shader: variant `int min( int a, int b)` should appear in hint window. 4. Type `int foo = min(1,2);` into fragment part of the shader. 5. Error message appears (see the picture above) - proving that function returns `float` instead of `int`. 6. Do the same to `max` function ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) --//--

godotengine/godot#115476.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.6-stable, 4.5.1, and all previous 4.x releases

### System information

All systems

### Issue description

When animating individual points of a Polygon2D (instead of using bones) the engine slows down very quickly. Even a powerful machine will fail to reach 60 FPS when animating a few dozen Polygon2Ds each frame. Polygon2Ds used to be cheap to create and modify in Godot 3.x, so many users still expect them to be cheap. 

In reality, they are very slow to create and slow to modify, but they internally now work better with GPU skinning than they did in Godot 3.x. So if you are following the expected pathway and using the Polygon2Ds with bones and skeletons, performance is fine. 

Ultimately, many devs need more flexibility. Both the Spine animation plugin and Humble Reeds (developers of Kamaeru and Heidi's Legacy) have run into this issue. Currently the Polygon2D node is a huge footgun and it is easy to get unacceptable bad levels of performance. 

In a test project provided by Humble Reeds, I had a frame rate of 30 FPS on both my M2 MBP and my Desktop gaming computer.

The problem comes from the fact that Polygon2Ds destroy and re-create their backing mesh every time they are changed. Re-creating the polygon is already kind of expensive, but destroying and re-creating the mesh is extremely expensive. 

Luckily the solution is simple. In general, vertices are not added and removed while animating. So instead of destroying the mesh and creating a new one, we can simply update the existing mesh. 

I created a [POC](https://github.com/clayjohn/godot/commit/5ac83f23e38f1f93e1c4bbeda92002d8abe36110) and found that I was able to triple performance in a scene that contained only characters animated this way. 

A proper solution will ensure that the mesh update code path is only run when it is totally safe to do so (i.e. vertex count doesn't change, the presence of colors/uvs hasn't changed, the mesh format hasn't changed, etc.). The POC only checks for vertex count changing. The code can be optimized much further as well, but with a test scene provided by Humble Reed I was able to fully eliminate the overhead of mesh destruction/creation which accounted for over 2/3rds of the overall cost of animating the Polygon2D

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a polygon 2D
2. Use the AnimationPlayer or a script to modify vertices in the polygon2D
3. Notice the performance impact

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[goatMRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24895882/goatMRP.zip)
godotengine/godot#115476.title: Poor performance with animated Polygon2Ds

godotengine/godot#115477.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.stable, 4.5.1.stable, 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable - macOS Tahoe (26.2.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

When debugging GDScript using the built-in debugger in Godot, on macOS specifically (I haven't been able to reproduce this on Linux/X11 at least), it seems there's a problem with how the message queue in `RemoteDebugger` is flushed.

More specifically, when the game runtime has entered into the loop in `RemoteDebugger::debug`, you can through several different paths end up invoking more GDScript code (e.g. `_notification`) which has the "random" possibility of invoking `EngineDebugger::poll_events()` from `OPCODE_LINE` in the GDScript VM, depending on how much code was executed. This will flush all remaining messages from `RemoteDebugger::messages`, without properly handling some of them, including the ones that are later meant to (and can only) be handled in `RemoteDebugger::debug`, like the `"get_stack_dump"` message for example.

This is the code in the GDScript VM that periodically invokes `EngineDebugger::poll_events`:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/8fb5cd8779a3cf4723e332e4240508169eaa9f0e/core/debugger/engine_debugger.h#L127-L133

Here's what the call stack can look like as it improperly flushes a `"get_stack_dump"` message without actually handling it (when not using the embedded game window on macOS):

```
RemoteDebugger::poll_events(bool) (core/debugger/remote_debugger.cpp:686)
EngineDebugger::line_poll() (core/debugger/engine_debugger.h:130)
GDScriptFunction::call(GDScriptInstance*, Variant const**, int, Callable::CallError&, GDScriptFunction::CallState*) (modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp:3927)
GDScriptInstance::notification(int, bool) (modules/gdscript/gdscript.cpp:1943)
Object::_notification_forward(int) (core/object/object.cpp:1026)
Object::notification(int, bool) (core/object/object.h:929)
...
Node::propagate_notification(int) (scene/main/node.cpp:2602)
SceneTree::_notification(int) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:920)
SceneTree::_notification_forwardv(int) (scene/main/scene_tree.h:88)
Object::_notification_forward(int) (core/object/object.cpp:1013)
Object::notification(int, bool) (core/object/object.h:929)
-[GodotApplicationDelegate applicationDidResignActive:] (platform/macos/godot_application_delegate.mm:225)
...
-[GodotApplication sendEvent:] (platform/macos/godot_application.mm:168)
DisplayServerMacOS::_process_events(bool) (platform/macos/display_server_macos.mm:3211)
DisplayServerMacOS::process_events() (platform/macos/display_server_macos.mm:3193)
DisplayServerMacOS::force_process_and_drop_events() (platform/macos/display_server_macos.mm:3268)
RemoteDebugger::debug(bool, bool) (core/debugger/remote_debugger.cpp:645)
ScriptDebugger::debug(ScriptLanguage*, bool, bool) (core/debugger/script_debugger.cpp:90)
GDScriptLanguage::debug_break(String const&, bool) (modules/gdscript/gdscript_editor.cpp:282)
...
GDScriptFunction::call(GDScriptInstance*, Variant const**, int, Callable::CallError&, GDScriptFunction::CallState*) (modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp:1930)
GDScriptInstance::callp(StringName const&, Variant const**, int, Callable::CallError&) (modules/gdscript/gdscript.cpp:1907)
Node::_gdvirtual__process_call(double) (scene/main/node.h:427)
Node::_notification(int) (scene/main/node.cpp:92)
Node::_notification_forwardv(int) (scene/main/node.h:51)
Object::_notification_forward(int) (core/object/object.cpp:1013)
Object::notification(int, bool) (core/object/object.h:929)
SceneTree::_process_group(SceneTree::ProcessGroup*, bool) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:1204)
SceneTree::_process(bool) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:1281)
SceneTree::process(double) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:708)
Main::iteration() (main/main.cpp:4933)
```

Here's what it can look like when using the embedded game window on macOS:

```
RemoteDebugger::poll_events(bool) (core/debugger/remote_debugger.cpp:672)
EngineDebugger::line_poll() (core/debugger/engine_debugger.h:130)
...
GDScriptFunction::call(GDScriptInstance*, Variant const**, int, Callable::CallError&, GDScriptFunction::CallState*) (modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp:1960)
GDScriptInstance::notification(int, bool) (modules/gdscript/gdscript.cpp:1943)
Object::_notification_forward(int) (core/object/object.cpp:1026)
Object::notification(int, bool) (core/object/object.h:929)
...
Node::propagate_notification(int) (scene/main/node.cpp:2602)
SceneTree::_notification(int) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:920)
SceneTree::_notification_forwardv(int) (scene/main/scene_tree.h:88)
Object::_notification_forward(int) (core/object/object.cpp:1013)
Object::notification(int, bool) (core/object/object.h:929)
EmbeddedDebugger::_msg_notification(Array const&) (platform/macos/embedded_debugger.mm:158)
EmbeddedDebugger::parse_message(void*, String const&, Array const&, bool&) (platform/macos/embedded_debugger.mm:178)
EngineDebugger::capture_parse(StringName const&, String const&, Array const&, bool&) (core/debugger/engine_debugger.cpp:104)
RemoteDebugger::_try_capture(String const&, Array const&, bool&) (core/debugger/remote_debugger.cpp:348)
RemoteDebugger::debug(bool, bool) (core/debugger/remote_debugger.cpp:636)
ScriptDebugger::debug(ScriptLanguage*, bool, bool) (core/debugger/script_debugger.cpp:90)
GDScriptLanguage::debug_break(String const&, bool) (modules/gdscript/gdscript_editor.cpp:282)
...
GDScriptFunction::call(GDScriptInstance*, Variant const**, int, Callable::CallError&, GDScriptFunction::CallState*) (modules/gdscript/gdscript_vm.cpp:1930)
GDScriptInstance::callp(StringName const&, Variant const**, int, Callable::CallError&) (modules/gdscript/gdscript.cpp:1907)
Node::_gdvirtual__process_call(double) (scene/main/node.h:427)
Node::_notification(int) (scene/main/node.cpp:92)
Node::_notification_forwardv(int) (scene/main/node.h:51)
Object::_notification_forward(int) (core/object/object.cpp:1013)
Object::notification(int, bool) (core/object/object.h:929)
SceneTree::_process_group(SceneTree::ProcessGroup*, bool) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:1204)
SceneTree::_process(bool) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:1281)
SceneTree::process(double) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:708)
Main::iteration() (main/main.cpp:4933)
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP on macOS.
2. Put a breakpoint on `print("Hello, world!")` in `main.gd`.
3. Run the main scene.
4. Wait for breakpoint to be hit.
5. Note how a debugger break has happened (you can step, etc).
6. Note how no stack frames or stack variables are populated.
7. Do a "step over" in the debugger.
8. Note how the stack frames/variables are now populated.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[blank_debugger_data_2026-01-27_21-30-00.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24894116/blank_debugger_data_2026-01-27_21-30-00.zip)

godotengine/godot#115483.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible: v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439] - Not reproducible: v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1] (regression) ### System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9174) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-13600KF (20 threads) - 15.83 GiB memory ### Issue description A Button with Font with LCD antialiasing and style texture containing semi-transparent colors renders the semi-transparent as fully transparent. This does not happen on the first Button in the scene, but for all further ones. This does not happen with grayscale antialiasing. It's also a regression from Godot 4.5.1. The whole setup and effect is visible here: The Second button is a duplicate for the First one and should look the same, but it's background is fully transparent instead. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Import a font, changing antialiasing to LCD Subpixel 2. Create a Button 3. In Theme Overrides: 3.1. Font: put imported font 3.2. Styles / Normal: put PNG image with alpha (semi-transparent color) 4. Duplicate the Button - the texture on the second one becomes fully transparent ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [stylebox-opacity.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24896711/stylebox-opacity.zip)

### Issue description

If I compile with `debug_symbols=yes`, when a crash occurs:

godotengine/godot#115511.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.6.dev6 and later - Not reproducible in 4.6.dev5 and earlier ### System information Godot v4.6.dev6 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9159) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-14700K (28 threads) - 63.77 GiB memory ### Issue description Previously, it was possible to leave one or more axes of the particle system's directional velocity blank. Now, blank axes will be filled with a default curve. It is possible to clear these default curves, but on reloading the scene the default curve will return. In 4.5.1: In 4.6.dev6 and beyond: ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a particle system with a directional velocity. Leave one or more axes blank. 2. Save and reload the scene. 3. The blank axes are filled with a default curve. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [particle-system-directional-velocity-curves.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24914281/particle-system-directional-velocity-curves.zip) 1. Open particles.tscn 2. Axes Y and Z should be blank on the directional velocity curve. Instead, they are provided with a default curve.

godotengine/godot#115553.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6 stable

I'm only able to test since the introduction of Storage Access Framework, but I find it likely that this is not a regression, or it at least spans across several versions.


### System information

Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19044) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.1659) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 13.85 GiB memory

### Issue description

On Windows and Linux, `position` is passed down as a negative number, according to documentation.

On Android (tested on Huawei TGR-W09), the seek direction is flipped, breaking my game. When I pass a positive number instead, it works as intended.

Additionally, it seems that this specific line is the root cause of the bug:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/5ad8b27d8db0776700c2b08bf68fbea5e0a4680d/platform/android/java/lib/src/main/java/org/godotengine/godot/io/file/DataAccess.kt#L206


### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
# on a non-empty file
func test(file: FileAccess) -> void:
	var size := file.get_length()
	file.seek_end(-1)
	var pos := file.get_position()
	assert(pos < size, "huh")
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[seekbug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24925231/seekbug.zip)
godotengine/godot#115553.title: FileAccess.seek_end has platform dependent seek behavior

If you export a project, it also happens in the standalone version. ### Steps to reproduce Observed behaviour: Two cases where it happens: * Just open the editor and turn on capslock. Expected behaviour - capslock icon only appears when a text-edit field is focussed, and appears beside the field and not floating in the top-left of the window. If a text-entry field is focussed, the capslock symbol appears in the correct place: ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) An empty project will produce the behaviour (i added an empty 3d scene and set required fields to export it to test the behaviour in standalone builds, but I think every project should have it). But if you really want, here's my test project:

godotengine/godot#115599.body: [MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24948378/MRP.zip) ### Tested versions Godot 4.6 stable ### System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated GeForce 920M - Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-5005U CPU @ 2.00GHz (4 threads) - 11.91 ГиБ memory ### Issue description Godot version: 4.6.stable Previously working in: 4.5.stable.mono After upgrading my project from Godot 4.5 to 4.6, the entire rendering pipeline appears to be broken. Lighting that was carefully tuned over weeks is now completely destroyed. The same scenes, same assets, same settings produce drastically worse results in 4.6. Issues observed: - VoxelGI lighting is broken (incorrect light propagation, dirty and overexposed results) - SDFGI quality is significantly degraded compared to 4.5 - Built-in sky shaders render incorrectly - Environment lighting no longer behaves as expected - Overall global illumination and scene balance are completely off This upgrade fully broke my previously finished and polished lighting setup. No materials, shaders, or scene logic were changed during the upgrade. Expected result: Rendering output in Godot 4.6 should match or closely resemble the visual quality of Godot 4.5 ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the same project or scene in Godot 4.5.stable. 2. Observe correct lighting, global illumination (VoxelGI / SDFGI) and sky/environment shaders. 3. Open the same project in Godot 4.6.stable without changing any settings, assets or materials. 4. Observe broken lighting, incorrect VoxelGI and SDFGI behavior, and visually broken built-in sky shaders. 5. Compare the visual result between 4.5 and 4.6 (see attached screenshots). ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) I’ve attached an MVP prototype of the project for review.
godotengine/godot#115599.title: Major rendering regression in Godot 4.6: broken sky shaders, VoxelGI and SDFGI lighting

godotengine/godot#115618.body: ### Tested versions - 4.5.1, 4.6 But I also wonder if it's related to https://github.com/abarichello/godot-ci/issues/150, which was present in 4.1 ### System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21013.1000) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.93 GiB memory ### Issue description I'm getting an error initially importing resources that have a custom importer *in a singleton*. I have some csv files set to load with the [csv data importer addon](https://github.com/timothyqiu/godot-csv-data-importer): I opened the `.import` file to make sure the importer was enabled and noticed a path in the `.godot` folder: ```toml [remap] importer="com.timothyqiu.godot-csv-importer" type="Resource" uid="uid://bd32ynsmpqx33" path="res://.godot/imported/singleton_data.csv-1db633ec39e81e98ec83fe69f840e486.res" [deps] source_file="res://scene_data.csv" dest_files=["res://.godot/imported/singleton_data.csv-1db633ec39e81e98ec83fe69f840e486.res"] [params] delimiter=0 headers=true detect_numbers=true force_float=false ``` Deleting `res://.godot/imported/singleton_data.csv-1db633ec39e81e98ec83fe69f840e486.res` (and `.ctex file`) causes the file to revert to translation on editor restart. Resources preloaded in a regular scene-level script do not have this issue, but this has been a complete blocker in setting up ci builds for my project. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Set a resource to use a custom importer 2. Add a global script that `preload`s the file 3. Delete the related files from `.godot/imported` 4. Chaos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dec690e3-c7b0-4cec-80dd-077cdc0160e3 ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24955647/mrp.zip)

godotengine/godot#115669.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.6.0 stable - Not reproducible in: 4.5.1 stable ### System information Windows 11 - Godot v4.6.0 stable - Compability - Radeon Graphics ### Issue description When trying to use the https://github.com/poingstudios/godot-admob-plugin in the newest version of Godot: https://godotengine.org/releases/4.6/ my users are receiving an error making it impossible to use on the new version. Original Issue: https://github.com/poingstudios/godot-admob-android/issues/270 It seems a bug of Godot, because I don't see any migration recomendation of plugin for this kind of object type, please check it. ### Steps to reproduce Call a method in plugin with Dictionary and Array just as: GDScript: ```gdscript _plugin.set_request_configuration(request_configuration.convert_to_dictionary(), request_configuration.test_device_ids) func convert_to_dictionary() -> Dictionary: return { "max_ad_content_rating" : max_ad_content_rating, "tag_for_child_directed_treatment" : tag_for_child_directed_treatment, "tag_for_under_age_of_consent" : tag_for_under_age_of_consent } ``` Kotlin/Java: ```kotlin @UsedByGodot fun set_request_configuration(requestConfigurationDictionary: Dictionary, testDeviceIds : Array) { val maxAdContentRating = requestConfigurationDictionary["max_ad_content_rating"] as String val tagForChildDirectedTreatment = requestConfigurationDictionary["tag_for_child_directed_treatment"] as Int val tagForUnderAgeOfConsent = requestConfigurationDictionary["tag_for_under_age_of_consent"] as Int val requestConfiguration = RequestConfiguration.Builder() .setMaxAdContentRating(maxAdContentRating) .setTagForChildDirectedTreatment(tagForChildDirectedTreatment) .setTagForUnderAgeOfConsent(tagForUnderAgeOfConsent) .setTestDeviceIds(testDeviceIds.toList()) .build() MobileAds.setRequestConfiguration(requestConfiguration) } ``` Error: `Invalid type in function 'set_request_configuration' in base 'JNISingleton'. The array of argument 2 (Array[String]) does not have the same element type as the expected typed array argument.` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) Clone: https://github.com/poingstudios/godot-admob-plugin Follow the documentation: https://poingstudios.github.io/godot-admob-plugin/stable/ (Enable the plugin -> It will automatically download and install -> Enable Gradle build -> Export and test)
godotengine/godot#115669.title: Android plugin: The array of argument 2 (Array[String]) does not have the same element type as the expected typed array argument.

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.6.  I think it was in earlier versions too, so not a new issue.

### System information

linux (haven't tried others)

### Issue description

In script editor, mouse back and forward navigation button seem to be bound backwards (forward goes back, back goes forward).  Mouse back/forward buttons work as expected in every other program.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Ctrl-click a type in script editor or click a type in help docs.
2. Press mouse back button (does nothing).
3. Press mouse forward button (goes back).
4. Press mouse back button (goes forward to where ctrl-click went in step 1).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

not project-specific

godotengine/godot#115798.body:
### Tested versions

4.6 custom build with profiler=tracy.

### System information

win11 23h2, cpu AMD8845hs, GPU AMD 780M

### Issue description

I just use tracy to profile my game. There are few memory leaks(depending on nodes count in game). The detail cannot be tracked by tracy (except total memory on the right top) of godot memory monitor. Total memory also can be tracked by system task manager.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Just using the engine with tracy build to run a small game. 
2. See memory the task manager of windows or tracy. There has memory increasing every seconds, If you have more nodes more memory leaks. (In my cause I have 50 Mb/s memory increasing)
3. Using official engine or custom build without tracy cannot produce this issue.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

It need custom build. And can be reproduced in editor also.

godotengine/godot#115936.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439], v4.6.dev.custom_build [038f5934e]
- Not reproducible in: v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1], v4.6.dev.custom_build [3bf0f771e]

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (3bf0f771e) - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M2 Max (Apple8) - Apple M2 Max (12 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

After the SwiftUI lifecycle migration introduced in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109974, the following application lifecycle notifications are no longer received on iOS:

- `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_RESUMED` (2014)
- `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_PAUSED` (2015)
- `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_FOCUS_IN` (2016)
- `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_FOCUS_OUT` (2017)

These notifications were previously sent when the app was backgrounded/foregrounded or gained/lost focus. After the SwiftUI lifecycle adoption, these notifications no longer fire on iOS.

**Expected behavior:** `_notification()` should receive `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_PAUSED` and `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_FOCUS_OUT` when app goes to background, and `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_RESUMED` and `NOTIFICATION_APPLICATION_FOCUS_IN` when app returns to foreground.

**Actual behavior:** None of these notifications are received.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the attached reproduction project
2. Export and run the project on iOS device or simulator
3. Press the home button to background the app
4. Return to the app
5. Observe that `2014`, `2015`, `2016`, and `2017` are never printed

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[notification-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25116649/notification-mrp.zip)

godotengine/godot#115999.body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.6 stable. ### System information Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900HX (32 threads) - 31.03 GiB memory ### Issue description Under certain configurations (having multiple threaded node subtrees, or setting `process_thread_group` at runtime), visual transformation of certain Node3Ds are erratic when threaded processing is enabled for them. See steps to reproduce for detailed description of an example case. ### Steps to reproduce In the MRP, we have a scene with this structure: where `direct_child` is the white sphere, `threaded_node_root` is the white torus. The script attached to `parent_main` is: ```gdscript extends Node3D var total_time := 0.0 func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void: total_time += delta position.x = sin(total_time) $threaded_node_root.set_deferred_thread_group(&'total_time', total_time) ``` The script attached to `threaded_node_root` is: ```gdscript extends Node3D var total_time := 0.0 func _ready() -> void: process_thread_messages = Node.FLAG_PROCESS_THREAD_MESSAGES_ALL process_thread_group = Node.PROCESS_THREAD_GROUP_SUB_THREAD func _physics_process(_delta: float) -> void: $direct_child.position.y = cos(total_time) $intermediate/indirect_child.position.y = cos(total_time) ``` These script should move the nodes along a circular trajectory. That is, the expected behavior is as follows: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e49ff37a-1805-4806-95fc-44c3c8ebeb6a However, the actual behavior is different. ### Case 1: When `threaded_node_root.process_thread_group` is set to sub thread *in the editor*, the result is: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e537ab7-34e6-4d6b-97a7-8643235e5d4a As you can see, the `MeshInstance3D` nodes randomly malfunctions (this clearly indicates some race conditions). ### Case 2: When `threaded_node_root.process_thread_group` is defaulted to inherit (manually reset it in the MRP), and is set to sub thread in the code via `_ready`, the result is: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d46cff8-cda1-4ef2-be84-dd3d6fc2b323 This time the meshes do not get stuck, but transform of them are not properly updated. X-axis movement is driven by `parent_in_main`, which runs in the main thread. Y-axis movement is driven by `threaded_node_root`. Only X-axis movement is working in this case, and the debug panel is filled with this message: ``` E 0:00:00:765 threaded_scene.gd:10 @ _physics_process(): /root/Node3D/parent_in_main/threaded_node_root/direct_child: The caller thread can't call the function `set_position()` on this node. Use `call_deferred()` or `call_deferred_thread_group()` instead. Condition "!is_accessible_from_caller_thread()" is true. scene/3d/node_3d.cpp:708 @ set_position() threaded_scene.gd:10 @ _physics_process() ``` which indicates that thread group of children of `threaded_node_root` is not properly setup when `threaded_node_root.process_thread_group` is set through code at runtime. (The `threaded_node_root` is indeed running in another thread, as `Thread.is_main_thread()` returns `false` in its `_physics_process()`) I think I have figured out what's wrong in the code and fixed it, and I am submitting [PR#116000](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116000) now. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [threaded_fix_demo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25144218/threaded_fix_demo.zip)
godotengine/godot#115999.title: Transform of 3D nodes is broken when using threaded processing.

godotengine/godot#116012.body:
Addresses [issue#116011](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116011), and potentially some other issues. The MRP of that issue should now have correct behavior when the commit of this PR is applied.

Behavior of the MRP of [issue#116011](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116011), with this PR:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/117079db-07c0-4b7c-8353-501c859d4048

## Explanation

Node3D simultaneously stores a `Transform3D` and a pair of euler angles and scale for some reason (documented in `node_3d.h`, right above definition of `enum TransformDirty`). Both representations are readable and writable.

It uses a dirty-flagging system to transparently handle this: when transform is set, euler angle and scale is flagged as dirty; when euler angle or scale is set, transform is flagged as dirty. As an optimization, actual update of the data is deferred until they are read.

In multi-threading mode (i.e., when thread group is set to sub), this dirty flag is implemented as a bitset based on atomic uint32 (`SafeNumeric<uint32_t>`, which is a wrapper over `std::atomic<uint32_t>`).

The problem is, when testing for certain bits in the atomic, what Node3D did is `flags.bit_and(mask)`. However, `SafeNumeric<>::bit_and` is implemented as `std::atomic<>::fetch_and`, whose semantic is "atomically set the atomic variable `v` to `v & mask`, and return the original value of `v`".

This would result in the following problem: any bit set in the dirty flag would cause a query with any mask to return true. And if the query is using a mask that *should* return false, the dirty flag will be effectively cleared.

The correct semantic should be "load the value of `v` and return `v & mask`, without modifying `v`". So it should be implemented as `flags.load() & mask`, instead.

This PR did exactly this.

- Bugsquad: Fixes #116012
godotengine/godot#116012.title: Fix erroneous use of atomic in the dirty flags of Node3D

godotengine/godot#116022.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.7.dev, 4.6.1.rc1, 4.5.1.stable, 4.5.stable, 4.4.1.stable, 4.3.rc2

### System information

godotengine/godot#116050.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.stable
- Not reproducible in: 4.5.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

In short, when using Jolt Physics, the `SoftBody3D.total_mass` property has seemingly changed from a default value of 0 kg in Godot 4.5 to instead show a default value of 0.001 kg in Godot 4.6. This "regression" seems to stem from #108758.

Note that 0.001 kg is not the actual value being used either way. The actual value being used is still 0 kg. This is purely a misleading visual artifact.

This gets a bit messy, but basically, the `SoftBody3D` node as currently implemented with Jolt has ([for questionable reasons](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/111993#issuecomment-3864596572)) been inadvertently exploiting the behavior of the `SoftBody3D.total_mass` property hint to show a value of 0.

The previous value of 0 was (as mentioned in the link above) meant to communicate that the default mass was undefined/automatic, and should rightfully have been changed long ago. This new value of 0.001 kg does however seem even more problematic, since it suggests an actual concrete mass, way off from what the actual mass will end up being.

Note that this was made possible to begin with due to the fact that the `SoftBody3D` node doesn't properly store/cache its own property values, like the other physics nodes do, and instead relies on the physics server to report them, as seen here:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/56f3e2611daadb0b1894b46737ee3000e82e33f9/scene/3d/physics/soft_body_3d.cpp#L639-L641

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP in Godot 4.5.1.
2. Note how the `total_mass` property of the `SoftBody` node is `0`.
3. Open the MRP in Godot 4.6.
4. Note how the `total_mass` property is now `0.001` instead.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[soft_body_default_mass_2026-02-08_17-10-03.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25162637/soft_body_default_mass_2026-02-08_17-10-03.zip)
godotengine/godot#116050.title: Jolt Physics: `SoftBody3D.total_mass` changed default value between 4.5 and 4.6

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.1.stable, 4.6.stable, 4.7.dev [56f3e2611daadb0b1894b46737ee3000e82e33f9]

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev (56f3e2611) - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

When a `SoftBody3D` has a low per-point mass, the behavior of Jolt Physics is often erratic, sometimes resulting in the simulation exploding.

Here's what two 50x50 subdivided meshes look like with Jolt Physics, with the following values:

```
Total Mass: 1.01 kg
Linear Stiffness: 0.5
Shrinking Factor: 0.0
Damping Coefficient: 0.01
```

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4d7bee08-3a77-4d19-917f-e5b9a904c122

Here's what the same setup looks like with Godot Physics:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8cdec7f8-6f80-45bc-9926-55488b04f922

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the MRP.
2. Note the right-hand body behaving erratically, often exploding.
3. Switch to Godot Physics.
4. Note the bodies being more well-behaved.
5. Switch back to Jolt Physics.
6. Reduce the subdivision of the bodies.
7. Note how it behaves better.

godotengine/godot#116086.body:
### Tested versions

Found in v4.6.stable
Confirmed in v4.4.1-stable

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9186) - AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.58 GiB memory

### Issue description

When I used a function to assign a _default_ value for the Dictionary.get(key, default) method, I noticed that function being run, even when the _key_ existed.

It appears _default_ is being evaluated prior to the _key_ being checked, since the unexpected output precedes the expected output.

I expected the implied has(_key_) operation to return false before the second argument (_default_) was evaluated.

### Steps to reproduce

Try the script below. Any code added to my_function will be run, even though the get method can successfully find the key, "Car", in the dictionary. Note the unexpected text in the Output Dock precedes the expected output.

I incrementally increased the my_function input argument to determine if the method would wait indefinitely before retrieving the key value. It appears so. At 20, the expected output was the last of 21895 lines. At 21, ERROR: [output overflow, print less text!]
```gdscript
extends Node

var my_dict: Dictionary = { "Car": 3, "Boat": 2, "Airplane": 1 }



func print_vehicle_qty() -> void:
	# Unexpectedly, default calls my_function and processes code.
	var quantity = my_dict.get("Car", my_function(3)) # Caution! Increase input argument slowly.
	print("Car quantity: ", quantity)


# Arbitrary cpu burning code.
func my_function(n: int) -> int:
	if n <= 1:
		print(n)
		return n
	else:
		print(n)
		return my_function(n-1) + my_function(n-2)


func _ready() -> void:	
	print_vehicle_qty()
```
Output Dock
```
3
2
1
0
1
Car quantity: 3
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#116086.title: Dictionary.get method always evaluates default value first

The same project works without issues on Godot 4.5.1. **Expected behavior:** The app should launch and run normally on iPhone SE 2nd generation, as it did with Godot 4.5.1. **Actual behavior:** The app aborts on startup with a Metal API Validation failure: ``` validateMTLSamplerDescriptor:76: failed assertion `Sampler Descriptor Validation MTLSamplerBorderColorTransparentBlack is not supported on this device ``` ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the attached minimal reproduction project in Godot 4.6 2. Export the project for iOS (the project is already configured with Mobile renderer) 3. Open the exported Xcode project and run it on an iPhone SE 2nd generation **via Xcode debugger** (Debug run) 4. Observe that the app aborts with a Metal API Validation failure (`MTLSamplerBorderColorTransparentBlack is not supported on this device`) **Note:** Simply building and deploying without Xcode debugger may not surface this error. The Metal API Validation assertion is triggered when running under Xcode's debug session. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [se2-crash-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25179061/se2-crash-mrp.zip)

godotengine/godot#116104.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.0.4.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.7.dev [377b5a31752ad5e9c53733639ba0d263e1158645]

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev (377b5a317) - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

`encode_variant` does not zero out the padding bytes that are added to align a serialized `NodePath` to a 4-byte boundary, as seen here:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/377b5a31752ad5e9c53733639ba0d263e1158645/core/io/marshalls.cpp#L1512-L1525

This means the last few bytes (assuming non-aligned `NodePath`) can have random values in them, from uninitialized memory.

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
@tool
extends EditorScript

func _run() -> void:
	var node_path: NodePath = ^"a"

	var samples: Array[PackedByteArray] = []
	for i in range(1000):
		samples.append(var_to_bytes(node_path))

	var first: PackedByteArray = samples[0]
	if samples.count(first) != samples.size():
		print("NodePath '%s' does NOT serialize deterministically" % node_path)
		var mismatches: Array[PackedByteArray] = samples.filter(func (x): return x != first)
		print("%s != %s" % [first, mismatches[0]])
	else:
		print("NodePath DOES serialize deterministically")

```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#116104.title: `NodePath` does not serialize deterministically

master  (92a90a8e6f23a42281b41bfbf01257156f1c1407)

### Issue description

Issue:
When creating a new C# Script *outside* of Godot and annotating it with `[GlobalClass]` the script won't appear as a custom node unless the editor is restarted. 

Expected: Creating a file in an external editor should properly register a file as GlobalClass when Annotation is used without GodotEditor restart.




Note: When creating the same file inside Godot it works perfectly fine. Rebuilding has no effect on this.
While testing around it seems Godot fails to extract the global class name properly when scanning the file. 



### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new c# script in an external editor.
2. Annotate it with `[GlobalClass]`.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any project works; no point in sharing MRP since a new load properly registers the classes.

godotengine/godot#116176.body:
### Tested versions - Reproducible in: v4.6.stable.steam [89cea1439] ### System information Godot v4.6.stable (89cea1439) - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8157) - AMD Ryzen 7 7700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.21 GiB memory ### Issue description Windows does not like it if your file has a trailing space or dot (do not know if other operating systems have the same issue) and prevents you from making them by removing the trailing characters. However, `FileAccess` and `DirAccess` fail to remove these trailing characters, and will create the invalid files. ```gdscript var dirAccess : DirAccess = DirAccess.open("user://") dirAccess.make_dir("dot.") ```

Using File Explorer, the file shows up but you cannot perform any actions on it; this is what happens if you try to open "**dot.**" for example:

While `DirAccess` cannot open **dot.**, it can still delete it through the `remove` method. Another way to remove the invalid file is through the command below: ### Steps to reproduce 1. Use Windows 2. Make a directory through `DirAccess` or make a file through `FileAccess` 3. End the file name with dots or spaces ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A
godotengine/godot#116176.title: `FileAccess` & `DirAccess` can create invalid files on Windows

Godot v4.7.dev (2363720b5) - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9174) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-12900K (24 threads) - 63.78 GiB memory

### Issue description

There is an issue with saving a GDScript. The file is not written to the disk and not marked as dirty in the editor.
This only seems to happen if the script has a "compilation" issue before editing.
Sometime a second Ctrl+S correctly save the script but sometime it's seems you are just editing a ghost clone and can't never save the script.

If the script is in a not compiling state we can't save the file until the Editor say that the script is valid.

I used git bisect and this issue seems caused by #114917 but it's a big PR, not sure in that where the problem is.

### Steps to reproduce

### Case 1 saving a valid script too quickly that was invalid before
- Open the file `invalid_script.gd` in MRP
- Select the following text on line 8 `var test = `
- Delete the selected text
- Quickly press Ctrl+S before the red line of the error goes away

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1b338867-7685-4729-ae0d-2ec37cbd3f91)

### Case 2 saving a invalid script that was already invalid before
- Open the file `invalid_script.gd` in MRP
- Add anything to the script
- Press Ctrl+S

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6bf5623f-16e5-4629-88fe-cbe92d5669d1)

See that both time the script was not saved to the disk and not marked as dirty (*)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp_script_edit_2026-02-11_22-03-08.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25244368/mrp_script_edit_2026-02-11_22-03-08.zip)
godotengine/godot#116183.title: Saving a GDScript file that was invalid on last save is not saved

godotengine/godot#116191.body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in custom build of master (some just-fixed issues stepped in before this issue has a chance to reveal itself in stable builds). ### System information Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900HX (32 threads) - 31.03 GiB memory ### Issue description When there are threaded nodes, enabling physics interpolation could randomly crash the engine. The probability of crashing increases when the number of threaded nodes increase. With standard, production builds, the engine crashes with a single line of message ranging from (but not necessarily limited to): - malloc(): invalid size (unsorted) - double free or corruption (out) - corrupted size vs. prev_size - realloc(): invalid next size The custom crash handler did not have a chance to opt-in. This message is printed in the allocation functions of glibc (they abort()-ed right after that, I guess). This clearly indicates a nasty heap corruption. Most likely due to some wild writes that did not trigger SEGV immediately. ### Note In stable releases, this issue could have been hidden by more prominent issues of the process group mechanism. This new issue is reproduced with [PR#116000](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116000) and [PR#116012](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116012) already applied. ### Steps to reproduce Run the scene in the MRP. The MRP contains 64 threaded rotating boxes in one scene. On my computer this would almost certainly result in an immediate crash. I made [PR#116192](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116192) to address this, technical inspection and break down would be given in the PR. The scene in MRP: ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [threaded_fti.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25249765/threaded_fti.zip)
godotengine/godot#116191.title: Enabling physics interpolation may crash the engine when there are threaded nodes

The time it shows is the minimum of file modification time and editor startup time. If you open a new scene and modify it, the message will say that the scene was last saved whatever long you happen to have the editor open right now. Also nitpick, but even if it's the first scene you opened, saying it was last saved when you opened it is technically not correct either. godotengine/godot#116231.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.1-rc1
- Also reproducible in earlier versions (e.g. 4.6) but the hashing algorithm is different. See #115473. (So this is not technically a "regression" as 4.6.1 is an improvement over 4.6, but I am demonstrating that the issue is still possible.)

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.rc1 - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9174) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 15.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

I am following up on #115407: in 4.6 animation track paths (i.e. `node_path:property_name`) could be applied to the wrong node or property due to a hash collision. This was fixed in #115473 by changing the hashing algorithm from a simple XOR to using the more convoluted hashing library, thus making trivial collisions (e.g. `A:B` and `B:A` resulting in the same hash) not possible.

However, the fundamental issue remains: if two track paths have a hash collision, they will affect each other in weird ways. It is still fairly easy to find a hash collision, as the hashing algorithm isn't a cryptographic one, it just isn't trivial to do by accident. However, a large collection of procedurally generated nodes with incrementing integer names will eventually result in a collision (having ~100,000 objects named "SomethingX" where X is an incrementing integer will typically result in two objects having the same hash). What happens when they collide is very unpredictable since the two tracks seem to have their values summed rather than one overwriting the other.

This was mentioned by @beicause in [this related discussion](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110400#issuecomment-3278706272):
> Any hash algorithm may have collisions. If a value with the same hash already exists in the container, we should handle hash collisions (comparing if the values are actually equal always, placing elements with the same hash into a bucket when inserting).
>
> It seems we are not doing so, using hash directly as a key seems problematic, which may be a separate issue.

### Steps to reproduce

I have written a little Python script to find two names that hash the same: `Char23288:position` and `Char104673:position`. The script is attached should anyone want to play with it:

[hashcollide.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25276743/hashcollide.py)

Steps to reproduce are therefore:

1. Create two Node2D or Node3D nodes called `Char23288` and `Char104673`. (Note that this could happen naturally if a game was creating thousands and thousands of objects procedurally.)
2. Create an AnimationPlayer and animate both objects' `position` properties. In my example, I made a white square that is supposed to move down and up, and a black square that is supposed to move right and left.
3. Play the animation in the editor or in the running game.

Note that this **only** reproduces in 4.6.1. Previous versions used a different hashing algorithm so while it's still a problem, it won't work with these exact names.

Expected behaviour: the white square moves down and up; the black square moves right and left. (You can get this behaviour by renaming either of the nodes.)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0c8162ff-be75-440c-a051-d5dceb49de2d

Observed behaviour: the white square moves diagonally down-right and up-left (it is summing the transform from both tracks). The black square does not move at all.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8a3577f7-b857-4228-9909-e200c355e092

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[wrong_animations_repro_4.6.1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25276790/wrong_animations_repro_4.6.1.zip)
godotengine/godot#116231.title: Animation path hash collisions in 4.6.1 (animations applied to wrong property even after previous fix)

godotengine/godot#116320.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.6.stable.mono.official [89cea1439], v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439]

It's also Reproducible in the previous release v4.5.1.stable.mono.official [f62fdbde1] but with slightly different steps due to UI changes (see steps to reproduce)

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21037.1004) - AMD Ryzen 7 3700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.94 GiB memory

### Issue description

Godot crashes in the editor when changing the currently edited animation while at least two keyframes are selected in the Bezier curve editor.

**Expected Behavior**: Godot should switch animations normally without crashing.

**Actual Behavior:** Godot immediately crashes when switching animations.

In Godot 4.5.1. switching animation doesn't lead to a crash until the Bezier curve editor is togglet again (see steps to reproduce). This suggests the crash in 4.6 may be caused by the automatic reopening/refreshing of the Bezier editor rather than the animation change itself.

### Steps to reproduce

This can be easily reproduced in a new empty project:

**In Godot 4.6:**
1. Create a 3D Scene
2. Add an animation player to the root node
3. With the animation dock open, add a new animation called "anim1"
4. Add a bezier curve track to "anim1", select the root Node3D and its posititon property, click "open"
5. Right click on the track of position:x and "Insert key..."
6. Repeat step 5 to add a second keyframe
7. Add a second animation called "anim2"
8. Add a bezier curve track to "anim2", select the root Node3D and its posititon property, click "open"
9. Open "anim1" again
10. Switch from the track editor to the bezier curve editor
11. Select all of your keyframes
12. Open "anim2" again
13. Godot crashes

In Godot 4.5.1:
It's the same except after step 12 you have to toggle the Bezier curve editor again since it is not opened automatically when switching to a different animation. Godot still crashes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#116320.title: Crash when changing the currently edited animation while at least two keyframes are selected in the Bezier curve editor.

### Issue description

$FileDialog.show() does not use the native mode on android (14, pixel 4 with lineage) which it did on previos godot versions.

It's closely related to #115983 as the mentioned (Method 3) workaround for android seems to work for me as well, but I am not allowed to reopen it.


### Steps to reproduce

- add a FileDialog to root node
- set FileMode to "Open File", Access to "File System", Use Native System and visible to true
- start in remote deploy


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)


- unsure whether OS.request_permissions() is needed when using remote deploy, did not work with it included as well
