godotengine/godot#116364.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.3.stable (oldest version with wireframe in Compatibility), 4.6.stable, 4.7.dev 43c9a32b3d46f1333ee80b1e48ae77f8bf04b345 ### System information Godot v4.7.dev (40448082a) - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 580.119.02) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory ### Issue description The wireframe debug draw mode (or `render_mode wireframe;` in a custom shader) displays backfaces in Compatibility, but not Forward+/Mobile. Shaders that use `FRONT_FACING` are also ignored in Compatibility (the red sphere with green backfaces loses its green wireframes in Compatibility): Forward+/Mobile | Compatibility -|- | This inconsistency in appearance can be misleading if you are looking at wireframe mode to troubleshoot procedural mesh generation. I would lean towards making Compatibility not show wireframe backfaces (unless the material is double-sided), for consistency with Forward+/Mobile. **Edit:** According to https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/14220#issuecomment-3910486793, this is a design limitation: > Compatibility does not have backface culling for wireframes, because it uses a generated mesh of lines rather than glPolygonMode (presumably because this is not supported in WebGL without [an extension](https://registry.khronos.org/webgl/extensions/WEBGL_polygon_mode/) which does not have wide support, particularly on mobile devices). ### Steps to reproduce - Change the debug draw mode in the 3D editor's Perspective menu to **Display Wireframe** while using the Compatibility renderer. - Alternatively, use the wireframe debug draw mode at runtime by calling `get_viewport().get_viewport().debug_draw = Viewport.DEBUG_DRAW_WIREFRAME` in the main scene's `_ready()` function. Make sure you have this autoload script set up if you do that: ```gdscript extends Node func _init() -> void: RenderingServer.set_debug_generate_wireframes(true) ``` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [test_wireframe_backface.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25346745/test_wireframe_backface.zip)
godotengine/godot#116364.title: Wireframe debug draw mode displays backfaces in Compatibility, but not Forward+/Mobile

godotengine/godot#116454.body:
- Helps address #111218.
- ~Depends on #115623, ignore the first 3 "GLES3" commits.~ (Merged.)
- ~Depends on #116693, ignore the first `windows.h` commit.~ (Merged.)

A number of headers in the codebase included `rendering_server.h` just for some enum definitions. This means that any change to `rendering_server.h` or one of its dependencies would trigger a massive incremental rebuild.

With these changes, we decouple a number of classes from `rendering_server.h`, greatly speeding up incremental rebuilds for that area.

On my machine, compiling with `scons p=linux dev_build=yes dev_mode=yes fast_unsafe=yes linker=mold`, this reduces incremental compilation time after an edit of `rendering_server.h` from **2m57s to 1m17s** (i.e. 57% reduction, 100s saved).
It doesn't seem to affect the time it takes to build everything from scratch.

The changes are unfortunately somewhat intrusive, but after a few iterations and discussion on [RC](https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/devel?msg=jpdygWGCM8HFy4o28), I think what we land on isn't too bad. Instead of using RenderingServer enums with `RS::EnumName` or `RS::CONSTANT_NAME`, you replace `RS` by `RSE` and replace the `rendering_server.h` include by `rendering_server_enums.h` (which is a much smaller file with almost no dependencies).

> [!WARNING]
> This PR might have some adverse impacts on the PR backlog in two ways that we'll have to watch out for:
> - The removal of `rendering_server.h` and its dependencies as transitive includes in a large part of the codebase means that some headers that were previously taken from granted now need to be included explicitly. So some PRs may fail compiling if merged after this and not rebased prior to merging.
> - A lot of files are changed so there might be a bunch of merge conflicts.

> [!NOTE]
> **AI disclosure:** I designed and tested all changes myself, but I did use Copilot midway through to automate some of the busywork of moving all enums to a new file and adapting the rest of the codebase for the changes (it basically ran a couple vibe coded python scripts, based on initial work I had already done myself to validate the design). I reviewed all changes and spent a few additional ~hours~ days fixing remaining compilation errors and optimizing includes further. So I don't consider any of the code in this PR to be "AI generated", it's the same I would have done myself. The PR description is verbose but that's all written by me, there's just a lot to explain about this PR :sweat_smile: 

## Changes

~### 1. Fix `windows.h` include ordering mess with `platform_gl.h`~

- Superseded by #116693.

<details>
<summary>Previous description</summary>

Needs review from @bruvzg. I put this as the first commit as it's independent from the rest, but it was actually needed after the big commit adding `RSE`. I didn't look too deeply into why I was getting a `windows.h`/`object.h` conflict on ConnectFlags, possibly some reordering of includes or the fact that due to reduced transitive includes, `typedefs.h` would no longer be included after `windows.h`.

Moving the `#undef`s to `platform_config.h` and removing the `#pragma once` might be a bit controversial and/or risky, so I can also drop that commit and try to find another more localized approach to fix this issue. (E.g. duplicating the `#undef`s in `platform_gl.h`.)

</details>

### 2. ClassDB: Allow binding bitfield enums and constants from separate namespaces

Prerequisite for the rest, as RenderingServer has one BitField enum (`ArrayFormat`), and I moved some regular constants / anonymous enums to RSE too which require allowing and stripping `RSE::` in the `BIND_CONSTANT` call. I adapted the changes from #115963.

### 3. Move RenderingServer enums to a dedicated RenderingServerEnums (`RSE`) namespace

The main part of this PR. It creates `servers/rendering/rendering_server_enums.h` and moves all enums from `rendering_server.h` to it. I asked Copilot to ensure they're in the same order as they were in `rendering_server.h`, with similar area-defining comments. That might need double-checking.

The rest of the changes is necessary to make things compile:
- Replace the use of `RS::` / `RenderingServer::` with `RSE::`
- Replace explicit or implicit includes to `rendering_server.h` by `rendering_server_enums.h`
- Add a bunch of other headers here and there which are now needed due to not including `rendering_server.h` transitively everywhere, which means some places now need an explicit include for `Engine`, `OS`, `RBMap`/`RBSet`, etc. This is good and what we want.
- In particular, one goal I had was to remove `rendering_server.h` from all `.h` files, and especially ones in `scene`. It was notably included in `canvas_item.h` and `visual_instance_3d.h`, which means that all GUI, 2D, and 3D nodes included it transitively. Another significant removal is from `material.h`.
- I added a few `STATIC_ASSERT_INCOMPLETE_TYPE(class, RenderingServer);` checks in critical classes (CanvasItem, VisualInstance3D, Viewport, Window, SceneTree) to make sure we don't include `rendering_server.h` explicitly or transitively in their headers or dependencies.

~### 4. Move `RS::SurfaceData::LOD` to a separate file, to remove RS dependency in `mesh.h`~

This was merged into the commit decoupling MeshStorage from RS, as it supersedes it.

<details>
<summary>Previous description</summary>

`mesh.h` was the last `scene` header not covered in the previous commit, because it also depended on a struct nested deeply in RenderingServer.

So I created another namespace and file, RenderingServerTypes in `rendering_server_types.h` to put a new `RenderingServerTypes::SurfaceDataLOD` struct in there, and use that one in `mesh.h`, `rendering_server.h`, and the few other places which used that struct explicitly.

I kept this separate for review/discussion. It removes another \~23s from the incremental rebuild time for me, so it's definitely worth it, but the implementation can be adapted.

</details>

### 5. Decouple RenderingServer from XR/OpenXR headers

After all the above, I noticed that `xr_server.h` and `openxr_api.h` still included `rendering_server.h` directly, for some methods implemented directly in the header with `__FORCE_INLINE__`. I moved them to the .cpp files and dealt with the subsequent chain of missing includes, but this needs to be reviewed and ideally tested by @godotengine/xr. Was this `__FORCE_INLINE__` really key to performance, or are we confident it will be optimized well enough by the compiler anyway with the methods implemented in the .cpp?

This change removed another \~8s from the incremental rebuild time.

### 6. Decouple RenderingServer from ServersDebugger

Since I was on a roll, I dealt with the last remaining low-hanging fruit, also by moving the `RS::FrameProfileArea` struct to `RenderingServerTypes`. This made no impact on the rebuild time, `servers_debugger.h` isn't a hot include currently.
But I kept it anyway as I think we want to have a strict policy of not including `rendering_server.h` in a header if we can reasonably avoid it.

*Edit:* After edits this is the first commit creating RenderingServerTypes, for a change without a major impact. it's just because the big impactful change to `SurfaceData::LOD` was moved to a later commit.

### 7. Move BlitToScreen to RenderingServerTypes, fixing XRInterface cyclic dependency trap

XRInterface was forward-declaring BlitToScreen, forcing all implementations to include `renderer_compositor.h` explicitly (in practice, my previous change removed it as a transient include so things started to break). Looking into it, I tried to remove the forward-declare and everything broke because RendererCompositor includes RenderingMethod which includes... XRInterface. Nasty coupling there.

I moved BlitToScreen to the new RenderingServerTypes so it can now be included where needed without adding coupling. This only saves around 1s of incremental rebuild time, but it's mostly a code quality fix.

### 8. Remove `RenderingServer::map_scaling_option_to_stretch_mode` and cleanup boot splash code

This is a fixup to #109596, we should have allowed `project_settings.cpp` to be tightly coupled with `rendering_server.h`. The helper method isn't particularly needed in the first place, I just inlined it where it's used in the compositor, and hardcoded values in `project_settings.cpp` because it's unlikely to change and this is deprecated compat code anyway. Not much impact on compile time.

### 9. Misc dependency improvements for files depending on `rendering_server.h`

I did another pass on most remaining reverse dependencies of `rendering_server.h` as described in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116454#issuecomment-3926817152, and fixed what I could find. Notably:

- Remove direct `rendering_server.h` includes in a few places where it's not actually needed. Notably a big impact from removing it in `rasterizer_dummy.h`
- Cleanup some overreaching includes in a few place that would pull in `rendering_server.h` needlessly.
- Misc cleanup of includes / forward-declares in the files I was looking at, not all related to `rendering_server.h` specifically.

### 10. Move `RS::ShaderNativeSourceCode` to RenderingServerTypes to reduce dependencies on RS

Reduces the dependency on RS from MaterialStorage and paves the way for more internal renderer decoupling.

### 11. Minimize includes in `renderer_compositor.h`

`RenderingCompositor` is a virtual interface that ends up included in quite a few places, but it can totally use forward-declares instead of coupling all the rendering classes. This means that it's now the responsibility of the user to include the headers for the members it wants to use.

### 12. Decouple MeshStorage from RenderingServer

This one is a big one, as it decouples not only internal rendering components from each other, but also removes the dependency on RS from `mesh.h`, which is included in a lot of the codebase. So it saves a lot of time on incremental rebuilds.

### 13. Decouple TextureStorage from RenderingServer

More internal rendering code refactoring to decouple things and reduce which renderer files need to be recompiled when changing e.g. `rendering_server.h`.

### 14. Decouple RasterizerDummy from RenderingServer and DisplayServer

Looking at what were still reverse dependencies of `rendering_server.h`, I noticed that a bunch of DisplayServers would get recompiled when modifying it because they create `RasterizerDummy`. RasterizerDummy would directly include a lot of the renderer classes, so I moved those to pointers so they can be forward-declared.

### 15. Move RenderingMethod::RenderInfo to RenderingServerTypes

Helps decouple a number of files from `rendering_method.h`, which includes XRInterface and would lead to XRInterface being available way beyond where it should be.

## Future work

Some other potential changes I noted down while working on this, to be explored in other PRs:

- `RenderingServer::get_singleton()->get_video_adapter_name()` is used in a few places just to print it to stdout, we could reduce RS dependencies by moving it somewhere else.
- MeshStorage only depends on RS for SurfaceData and MeshInfo.
- MaterialStorage only depends on RS for ShaderNativeSourceCode.
- RS only includes DisplayServer for WindowID and VSyncMode. Prime candidate for further refactoring.
- All remaining direct includes of `rendering_server.h` in headers seem to be in rendering code. It's tightly coupled by design, so it's not surprising, but maybe we can uncouple things a bit and avoid recompiling all of `servers/rendering/` all the time.

```
$ rg -g'*.h' "include .*rendering_server\.h" --sort=path -l
servers/rendering/rendering_server_default.h
```
godotengine/godot#116454.title: Decouple RenderingServer from as much of the codebase as possible, moving enums to a new `RSE` namespace

godotengine/godot#116522.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.6 and 833889aec024b792b1c5ce5089eaebc5eb45692f

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable unknown - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 09 Feb 2026 17:16:33 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - KDE Plasma Version: 6.5.5, KDE Frameworks Version: 6.23.0, Qt Version: 6.10.2, Graphics Platform: Wayland

### Issue description

When using native Wayland with KDE (Klipper as clipboard manager), copying text from the editor or in-game works for the first attempt; however, subsequent copies are not logged in the clipboard history until you copy text from another application and then copy text in Godot again.

Example scenario :
1. Copy text "A" inside the Godot editor
2. Check the clipboard history, initially showing "A" in the list
3. Copy text "B" inside the Godot editor
4. Check the clipboard history, still shows "A" in the list
5. Copy text "C" outside Godot
6. Check the clipboard history, now shows "C", "A" in the list (ordered chronologically)
7. Copy text "D" inside the Godot editor
8. Check the clipboard history, now shows "D", "C", "A" in the list
9. Copy text "E" inside the Godot editor
10. Check the clipboard history, shows "D", "C", "A" in the list

Despite not being logged, pasting still works properly (according to the example scenario, it will paste "E")

Using X11 (set `run/platforms/linuxbsd/prefer_wayland` to false) does not seem to have this issue.

Not sure if this is reproducible in other clipboard managers or DEs

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch the editor with native Wayland
2. Copy any text in the editor
3. Open the clipboard history and observe that the copied text is logged
4. Copy different text in the editor again
5. Open the clipboard history again, and observe that the second copy is not logged.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Not needed
godotengine/godot#116522.title: Clipboard history only logs the first copy attempt from Godot in Wayland

godotengine/godot#116534.body:
### Tested versions

v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e]
Godot Engine v4.6.1.stable.official.14d19694e - https://godotengine.org
OpenGL API 3.3.0 NVIDIA 591.74 - Compatibility - Using Device: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650

Same Issue on v4.6 stable

### System information

Window 10 x64 Latest

### Issue description

When the Script Editor is detached into its own window and then closed using the window close button, the editor crashes instead of re-attaching the Script Editor back into the main Godot editor.

Expected behavior is that closing the detached Script Editor window should re-dock it into the main editor layout. Instead, the engine crashes completely.

This makes the detachable Script Editor workflow unsafe and unreliable.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open any project
2. Open Script Editor tab
3. Detach Script Editor into a separate window
4. . The Script Editor opens in a separate fullscreen/native window
5. Click the window close button (X) on the detached Script Editor window
6. Observe crash 
 
Expected result
- Script Editor window closes
- Script Editor re-attaches to main editor layout
- Editor continues running normally

Actual result
- Detached Script Editor window closes
- Entire Godot editor crashes immediately

Reproducibility
- Always

Version
- Godot 4.6.1
- Also present in 4.6

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Simple New Project with a script open in script editor will do.
godotengine/godot#116534.title: Crash when closing detached Script Editor window instead of re-attaching to main editor

godotengine/godot#116535.body:
### Tested versions

v4.6.1.stable.official
Godot Engine v4.6.1.stable.official.14d19694e - https://godotengine.org/
OpenGL API 3.3.0 NVIDIA 591.74 - Compatibility - Using Device: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650
Same Issue on v4.6 stable

### System information

Window 10 x64 Latest - Godot v4.6.1 stable - GTX 1650 - Ryzen 3 3100

### Issue description

When resizing any dock panel (Scene Tree, Inspector, FileSystem, History, Bottom Panel, etc.), the resize operation stops immediately if the mouse cursor enters the 3D viewport while still dragging.

This issue affects all dock panels and only occurs when interacting near the 3D viewport. Resizing works correctly when dragging in directions that do not cross into the 3D viewport.

The issue does NOT occur in:
2D viewport
Script editor
Scene editor UI areas

The issue ONLY occurs when the cursor enters the 3D viewport.
This suggests the 3D viewport is capturing or interrupting mouse drag events during active UI resize operations.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open any 3D project
2. Ensure 3D viewport is visible
3. Hover mouse over resize grab bar of any dock panel. Examples: [ Scene Tree, Inspector, FileSystem, Bottom Panel ]
4. Click and hold mouse button to resize
5. While still holding mouse button, move cursor into the 3D viewport
6. Observe resizing stops immediately

Expected result
- Resize operation continues normally regardless of cursor entering viewport
- Dock resizing remains active until mouse button is released

Actual result
- Resize operation stops immediately when cursor enters 3D viewport
- User must release mouse and start drag again

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Simple New Project will do.
godotengine/godot#116535.title: Dock panel resizing stops when mouse enters 3D viewport during drag

godotengine/godot#116632.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.1-stable
- Not reproducible in: 4.4.1-stable

### System information

iOS 26.2.1, iPhone 16 Pro

### Issue description

The project settings `display/window/ios/suppress_ui_gesture`, `display/window/ios/hide_status_bar`, and `display/window/ios/hide_home_indicator` are silently ignored on iOS.

The SwiftUI lifecycle migration (#109974) wraps `GDTViewController` inside a `UIViewControllerRepresentable`. The hosting controller becomes the window's root view controller, but it doesn't delegate `preferredScreenEdgesDeferringSystemGestures`, `prefersHomeIndicatorAutoHidden`, or `prefersStatusBarHidden` to child VCs. Since iOS queries the root VC for these, the Godot project settings have no effect.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable `display/window/ios/suppress_ui_gesture` in Project Settings (and ensure `hide_home_indicator` is disabled — the two interfere with each other due to an Apple API conflict, see #104411)
2. Export to iOS and run on a physical device
3. Swipe up from the bottom edge — the app backgrounds immediately instead of requiring two swipes


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any project with `suppress_ui_gesture` enabled, exported to iOS with 4.6.1.
godotengine/godot#116632.title: iOS: suppress_ui_gesture, hide_status_bar, and hide_home_indicator project settings have no effect since SwiftUI lifecycle migration

godotengine/godot#116637.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.7.dev1 ### System information Godot v4.7.dev (a3e84cc2a) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7260) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700H (20 threads) - 15.79 GiB memory ### Issue description The button associated with the script name added in #112796 is accessible even when there is no file open, while this is basically unnoticed normally, if you are navigating the editor using keyboard you will notice this button rapidly. The button is able to be pressed, although nothing happens and you can get the tooltip to appear. It could maybe be restricted to when accessibility mode is on. ### Steps to reproduce - Open the script tab with nothing open - Go to where the file name should be moving the focus using keyboard navigation, or moving the mouse there, it should appear down of the start of the 3d icon - The tooltip should appear if you used the mouse ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

godotengine/godot#116672.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 
- v4.6.stable.mono.official [89cea1439]
- v4.6.1.rc1.mono.official [7bfffc846]
- v4.7.dev1.official [bf95b6258]

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.rc1.mono - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9060 XT (RADV GFX1200) - AMD Ryzen 7 9700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 30.49 GiB memory

### Issue description

I use both Godot and external editor (vscodium, Version: 1.108.10359; Godot Tools version 2.5.1) to edit gdscript files. vscodium is connected to Godot language server. I have added breakpoints to the gdscript file. When I save the file in the external editor, the breakpoints disappear in Godot editor. It doesn't matter whether I have made any changes to the file. Just pressing Ctrl-S without any code changes in the external editor removes _all breakpoints_ from _all files_. So this doesn't affect only the saved file.

I would expect the breakpoints not to disappear. 



### Steps to reproduce

1. Add multiple breakpoints to different gdscript files.
2. Open _any_ gdscript file from the project in an external editor.
3. Save the file in the  external editor without any changes (ie. just press Ctrl-S). --> all breakpoints disappear.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#116672.title: Breakpoints disappear when file is saved in external editor

godotengine/godot#116674.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.1.stable, 4.7.dev [a3e84cc2af14aa4cffbefd8e13492e59567a64e3]
- Not reproducible in: 4.5.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev (a3e84cc2a) - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

The following script:

```gdscript
extends AnimationPlayer

func _ready() -> void:
    play("my_first_animation")
    queue("my_second_animation")
    await animation_finished
    stop()
```

Will cause the process to crash here:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/a3e84cc2af14aa4cffbefd8e13492e59567a64e3/scene/animation/animation_player.cpp#L344

... with the following call stack:

```
AnimationPlayer::_blend_post_process() (scene/animation/animation_player.cpp:344)
AnimationMixer::_process_animation(double, bool) (scene/animation/animation_mixer.cpp:999)
AnimationMixer::_notification(int) (scene/animation/animation_mixer.cpp:2349)
AnimationMixer::_notification_forwardv(int) (scene/animation/animation_mixer.h:43)
AnimationPlayer::_notification_forwardv(int) (scene/animation/animation_player.h:37)
Object::_notification_forward(int) (core/object/object.cpp:1012)
Object::notification(int, bool) (core/object/object.h:929)
SceneTree::_process_group(SceneTree::ProcessGroup*, bool) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:1201)
SceneTree::_process(bool) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:1281)
SceneTree::process(double) (scene/main/scene_tree.cpp:708)
Main::iteration() (main/main.cpp:4944)
OS_LinuxBSD::run() (platform/linuxbsd/os_linuxbsd.cpp:995)
main (platform/linuxbsd/godot_linuxbsd.cpp:121)
```

... because the access of `playback_queue.front()->get()` doesn't take into account that the emission of `animation_finished` just above it can clear `playback_queue`:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/a3e84cc2af14aa4cffbefd8e13492e59567a64e3/scene/animation/animation_player.cpp#L340-L342

This emission of `animation_finished` was added in #112571, so would seem to be cause for this regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP
2. Run the main scene
3. Note the crash after the first animation plays

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[animation-player-crash.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25495151/animation-player-crash.zip)
godotengine/godot#116674.title: Calling `AnimationPlayer.stop()` on `animation_finished` with queued animation crashes the process

godotengine/godot#116679.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.1 and a3e84cc2af14aa4cffbefd8e13492e59567a64e3 (master at time of writing)
- May be reproducible in older versions

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev (a3e84cc2a) - Arch Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 09 Feb 2026 17:45:06 +0000 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon 890M Graphics (radeonsi, gfx1150, LLVM 21.1.6, DRM 3.64, 6.18.9-zen1-2-zen) - AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 w/ Radeon 890M (24 threads) - 46.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

Wayland buffers must have a size divisible by their scale. The constraint is being violated intermittently on Sway using a scale=2 output. It appears to be a race condition, where the scale is updated after the correct size has been enqueued (`WindowRectMessage`) but before that message is processed. in that gap the new scale is committed along with an incorrectly sized buffer and Godot crashes with a Wayland protocol error.

[This commit](https://github.com/wmww/godot/commit/7e59c52c80ce36c2a1187661e1d2c5e26610176c) seems to fix my issue by always using the fractional scaling code path when the viewporter protocol is available. However this doesn't solve the underlying issue, so it could still be hit with compositors that don't support the protocol. Also it's claudeslop, so it could be wrong in other subtle ways. Happy to open a PR with it, or to human-write a better solution with guidance on how to go about that (I'm experienced with Wayland, less so with Godot's windowing code and thread safety model).

Here are logs with `WAYLAND_DEBUG=1` and print statements added to `WaylandThread::window_state_update_size()`, `WaylandThread::_frame_wl_callback_on_done()` and `DisplayServerWayland::_update_window_rect()`:

godotengine/godot#116706.body:
### Tested versions

godot   4.X  All Version


### System information

windows11 , AMD Ryzen 7900, 48GB , RTX4060ti , DX12,vulkan

### Issue description

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116649

In the previous link, the example was too complicated, so it seemed that the person in charge misunderstood the problem. Therefore, I simplified it.

One thing certain is that if a node is deleted while it is using a coroutine with await, the local objects inside the coroutine are not destroyed (they are not cleared).

### Steps to reproduce

```
# main.gd
extends Node2D

var test_node: Node

func _ready() -> void:
	test_node = preload("res://TestNode.gd").new()
	add_child(test_node)

	await get_tree().create_timer(5.0).timeout

	if is_instance_valid(test_node):
		print("[Main] delete test_node")
		test_node.queue_free()
		test_node = null

```

```
# TestNode.gd
extends Node

class TestClass extends RefCounted:
	func _notification(what):
		if what == NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE:
			print("[TestClass] PREDELETE")

func _exit_tree() -> void:
	print("[TestNode] exit_tree")
					
func _ready() -> void:	
	print("[TestNode] ready")
	_run()

func _run() -> void:
	##  this TestClass instance is not cleared
	var test = TestClass.new()
	while is_inside_tree():
		print("[TestNode] tick: ", Time.get_ticks_msec())
		await get_tree().create_timer(0.5).timeout
```


 in the main.gd source code, a TestNode is created and then deleted after 5 seconds.

In TestNode.gd, the run function is a coroutine running an infinite loop.
The problem is that inside this coroutine there is a local variable TestClass. Even though the coroutine is deleted, the memory for this local variable is not released (its destructor is not called).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[coroutine-problem.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25510782/coroutine-problem.zip)


[output]
[TestNode] ready
[TestNode] tick: 521
[TestNode] tick: 1023
[TestNode] tick: 1526
[TestNode] tick: 2029
[TestNode] tick: 2533
[TestNode] tick: 3035
[TestNode] tick: 3533
[TestNode] tick: 4036
[TestNode] tick: 4539
[TestNode] tick: 5042
[Main] delete test_node
[TestNode] exit_tree
godotengine/godot#116706.title: When an interrupted coroutine is deleted, local objects are not cleared

godotengine/godot#116717.body:
### Tested versions

- v4.6.1.stable.flathub [14d19694e]
- v4.7.dev1.official [bf95b6258]

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev1 - Freedesktop SDK 24.08 (Flatpak runtime) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon 860M Graphics (radeonsi, gfx1152, LLVM 19.1.7, DRM 3.64, 6.17.12-300.fc43.x86_64) - AMD Ryzen AI 7 PRO 350 w/ Radeon 860M (16 threads) - 58.49 GiB memory

### Issue description

Many methods of `AudioStreamInteractive` do not appear in the autocomplete menu in the Script editor. Some methods, such as `get_transition_fade_mode`, do appear, but `get_clip_name`, `get_clip_stream`, `get_clip_auto_advance`, and others do not.

These methods do appear in the in-editor documentation. And they can be called without issue.

I took a look at the `audio_stream_interactive.cpp` source code to see if I could see anything obvious. I can't but what's striking is that the affected methods are all those for which `bind_method` is called before `ADD_PROPERTY(..., "clip_count", ...)`. I can't understand why this would be the case!

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2327a823578a30f09068f97272598521896d5633/modules/interactive_music/audio_stream_interactive.cpp#L467-L483

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
extends Node

var stream: AudioStreamInteractive

func _ready() -> void:
	stream. # and now search the autocomplete list
```


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#116717.title: Setters and getters for indexed properties (e.g. AudioStreamInteractive set_clip_name) missing from autocomplete

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: Custom fork of 4.6.stable, with a few modifications

### System information

MacBook Pro M1 Max, macOS 15.5

### Issue description

This issue description will be a bit vague, as I am not able to reproduce this myself, but it seems likely there is (or was?) some issue with the game embedding on macOS, as we had this editor crash reported to us, which happened right as the embedded game itself crashed:

```
_sigtramp
ScriptEditorDebugger::is_session_active() (script_editor_debugger.h:333)

DisplayServerMacOS::_dispatch_input_events(Ref<InputEvent> const&) (display_server_macos.mm:440)
Input::_parse_input_event_impl(Ref<InputEvent> const&, bool) (input.cpp:987)
Input::flush_buffered_events() (input.cpp:1293)
DisplayServerMacOS::_process_events(bool) (display_server_macos.mm:3232)
invocation function for block in OS_MacOS_NSApp::start_main() (os_macos.mm:1118)
__CFRUNLOOP_IS_CALLING_OUT_TO_AN_OBSERVER_CALLBACK_FUNCTION__
__CFRunLoopDoObservers
__CFRunLoopRun
CFRunLoopRunSpecific
RunCurrentEventLoopInMode
ReceiveNextEventCommon
_BlockUntilNextEventMatchingListInModeWithFilter
_DPSNextEvent

-[NSApplication run]
OS_MacOS_NSApp::run() (os_macos.mm:1079)
```

```
Exception Type:    EXC_BAD_ACCESS (SIGABRT)
Exception Subtype: KERN_INVALID_ADDRESS at 0x00000000000009d0
```

Note that this is from a custom fork of 4.6-stable with a few changes here and there, so the line numbers might not perfectly match `master`, but as far as I know none of the more relevant classes here have had any changes done to them.

Judging by the call stack and the `KERN_INVALID_ADDRESS at 0x00000000000009d0`, it looks like it's crashing here in `LayerHost::gui_input`, due to `script_debugger` being `nullptr`:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/15a4311583fbaad2eee5d2ecda5437eb9fd68c63/platform/macos/editor/embedded_process_macos.mm#L348-L352

My best guess is that `EmbeddedProcessMacOS::set_script_debugger` was called with `nullptr` from `GameView::_detach_script_debugger` after the game process crashed, and at the next flushing of input events it ends up erroneously making its way to this `send_message` call in `LayerHost::gui_input`, but I've tried to simulate this by pushing input events every frame (in both a game and tool context) and manually killing the game process, but it seems to handle it gracefully every time.

### Steps to reproduce

None yet, unfortunately.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

None yet, unfortunately.

godotengine/godot#116753.body:
### Tested versions

4.7.dev

### System information

GLES3, Direct3D12, Windows

### Issue description

Before anything else, I would like to mention that the process of writing and debugging scene shaders is really not great, and the rendering team would greatly benefit from better tools (Better error messages, a setting for recompiling shaders at engine startup rather than engine build time, rudimentary compile errors within the IDE).

Now to the issue:
When you write any non-ascii character in GLSL shader for compatibility (`drivers\gles3\shaders\scene.glsl`), you get an infuriatingly unhelpful compilation error message:
`error C0000: syntax error, unexpected $end at token "<EOF>"`
This even happens when the respective character is part of a comment, which does not help when you try to fix the error by commenting out lines (happened to me when I was trying to refer to an algorithm by somebody named Ureña, and I swear, I wasted hours on this).

If you run the engine from the console, you get slightly more helpful errors: 
`Unicode parsing error, some characters were replaced with � (U+FFFD): Invalid unicode codepoint (ffffffb1), cannot represent as UTF-8`, but it still does not say the line, the file, the particular character (ffffffb1 means nothing), or that the characters should all be ASCII, rather than UTF-8.

Potentially related: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/61756

### Steps to reproduce

Add this comment to `drivers\gles3\shaders\scene.glsl`:
`// ñ`

recompile and run in compatibility rendering mode.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#116753.title: Crash with obscure error messages when adding non-ascii characters to Compatibility scene shader

godotengine/godot#116788.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproduced in 4.5.1-stable
- Reproduced in 15a4311583fbaad2eee5d2ecda5437eb9fd68c63

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21030.2001) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.91 GiB memory

### Issue description

When this code is run:
```
EditorInterface.reload_scene_from_path("res://main.tscn")
```

It can very occasionally call a segmentation fault, if the user is selecting nodes in the inspector! 

Because this is probably due to a race condition, it only occurs on very heavy scenes that take a while to load (i.e. lots of instancing). 

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the MRP
- Ensure that the buggy addon in `addons/foobar.gd` is running; "Foooo" should be printed to the console
- Open main.tscn
- Click on stuff in the inspector
- Your project should crash pretty quickly. It might take a minute. Just keep clicking around...
- If it crashes too fast or immediately upon startup, feel free to comment out the relevant line in `foobar.gd`. Then, uncomment it when you're ready to do some crashing.

Note that running it in a dev build might cause the reload to go so slow that the crash never happens! If that's the case, simplify the scenes by deleting some nodes, to speed up the reload.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-instanced-scene-4.6.1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25562696/test-instanced-scene-4.6.1.zip)
godotengine/godot#116788.title: `reload_scene_from_path` occasionally causes a segmentation fault while selecting nodes

godotengine/godot#116820.body: ### Tested versions reproducible on v4.7.dev.custom_build [ebc126193] not reproducible in v4.6.1.stable.arch_linux [14d19694e] ### System information Godot v4.7.dev (ebc126193) - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 09 Feb 2026 17:16:33 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon 780M Graphics (radeonsi, phoenix, LLVM 21.1.6, DRM 3.64, 6.18.9-arch1-2) - AMD Ryzen 7 8745HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads) - 28.18 GiB memory ### Issue description The variable `node reference` is not null, but doesn't show in the inspector when viewing the remote tree. The `@exported` variable shows fine though ### Steps to reproduce Set a variable value with `get_child` during runtime, and try to view it in the remote inspector. godotengine/godot#116822.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.7.dev [5cba18ff3bbe5adc9b4a8d7e970084979014b5f4], 4.6.1.stable, 4.5.2.stable

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev (5cba18ff3) - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

It might be a bit cheeky of me to frame this as a bug, but it seems `--ignore-error-breaks` (as introduced in #77015) was only implemented for `RemoteDebugger` and not `LocalDebugger`, which means that when you use `--debug` you will always end up hitting a debugger break on any GDScript error, which might not be desirable if you're just hoping to hit a particular breakpoint.

Not having `--ignore-error-breaks` available in `--debug` also creates problems for certain niche CI use-cases, as `--debug` is currently [the only way](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/ebc126193a7ebf5a128256eb9e76d8a9c4d3ad16/modules/gdscript/gdscript.cpp#L875-L881) to have GDScript warnings actually be logged as part of automated tests and whatnot.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the MRP with `/path/to/godot --debug --ignore-error-breaks --breakpoints res://main.gd:8`.
2. Note how the debugger stops at the error in `_do_something_silly` rather than at the breakpoint.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[no-local-ignore-error-breaks.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25584860/no-local-ignore-error-breaks.zip)
godotengine/godot#116822.title: `--ignore-error-breaks` does nothing with `--debug`

godotengine/godot#116826.body:
- Helps address #111218
- Related #116737
- Related #111573

During yesterday's core meeting, several topics were touched on with regards to the process by which we should be removing implicit includes for the comically-prevalent `class_db.h`. The current consensus seems to be that it's something that all `Object` subclasses rely on, so it would result in a fair amount of churn for the codebase when rolling out those changes. Tracking performance changes would also be tricky, as the number of files that actually *need* the include is hard to get a concrete answer for, even with the two above PRs in isolation (combined might paint a different story).

This stylistic PR is made with the intent of getting the churn itself over with as quickly and painlessly as possible. For every single implementation file that binds in some capacity, this PR adds `#include "core/object/class_db.h` explicitly. There are zero functional changes as a result of this PR, because every single one of these files *requires* this include to begin with. As such, this PR similar to #116539 (albeit much wider in scope), where it more clearly spells out the "problem" includes by going against the minimal-include default.

~~On a somewhat-related note: compatibility include order was brought up during the `ClassDB` core meeting topic, as it was discovered in #116737 that we cannot simply put `class_db.h` *after* `*.compat.inc`. Ivorforce suggested that we could just drop all those includes to the bottom of the file instead, something that `godot-cpp` already does. Since I'm already touching literally every single file that has those compatibility includes, as they're only a concept for `Object` subclass bindings, I tackled their migration in the same pass. This too resulted in zero functional changes, so it's similarly ignored within the same commit.~~ **EDIT:** In the interest of keeping this PR free of blockers, I've instead implemented the #116737 approach of adding `class_db.h` includes to the `*.compat.inc` files instead
godotengine/godot#116826.title: Style: Add `class_db.h` includes explicitly

godotengine/godot#116873.body:
### Tested versions

- v4.6.stable.mono.conservatory_edition [f4cbe9b46]

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable.mono (f4cbe9b46) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-10900K CPU @ 3.70GHz (20 threads) - 31.85 GiB memory

### Issue description

**This issue is not that big a deal.** It cannot occur in a stock build of the engine, which 99% of people are going to be using. And even still, 99% of the 1% that *do* have a custom fork aren't going around disabling GDScript. So ymmv. 😛 

Anyway... 
`create_script_dialogue.cpp` sets its `default_language` property based on if it can find `"GDScript"`. When it does, it sets the `default_language` property to the proper index and also updates the `OptionButton` to select that option (see L900, L901).

However, if GDScript is disabled, this check never succeeds, and so the value is left at its default of `-1` which causes a crash in `script_language.cpp` when calling `ScriptServer::get_language(int p_idx)` in the dialogue, because `p_idx` is `-1`.

I was able to resolve this by modifying the create script dialogue logic to just set the default index and the selected dropdown option to `0` if `"GDScript"` is not found, just an else block on top of the existing statement shortly after L900. Not a great fix because it fails in the hypothetical scenario where no script languages are available at all.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a custom build of the engine and compile with `module_gdscript_enabled = "no"`
2. Create a new project.
3. In the FileSystem panel, right click, go to Create > Script. This will crash the engine.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#116873.title: Create script dialogue crashes if gdscript module is disabled

godotengine/godot#116887.body:
### Tested versions

4.6

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8183) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10750H CPU @ 2.60GHz (12 threads) - 15.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

It seems similar to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/75050.

Basically `EditorPlugin._forward_3d_draw_over_viewport(overlay)` is not called when the camera is panned or rotated, while it seems to be updated when camera is zoomed.

https://youtu.be/2q9lPzk0AY8

To add `set_force_draw_over_forwarding_enabled()` to the `enter_tree()` of the plug-in does not work.

However, the overlay does update when dragging the gizmo handles.

My understanding is either is should be called for all camera movements, or for none.

### Steps to reproduce

Make a GizmoEditorPlugin and try to use `EditorPlugin._forward_3d_draw_over_viewport(overlay)` to display circles on top of the handles. Rotate the camera.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Can provide one if requested.
godotengine/godot#116887.title: EditorPlugin: `_forward_3d_draw_over_viewport()` is not called when camera rotates and pans, while being called when zooming.

godotengine/godot#116927.body: ### Tested versions Tested and reproduced in Godot 4.6, 4.6.1, 4.5.1 and 4.7 dev1. ### System information Fedora - Godot 4.6 - Compatability ### Issue description Flagged as a bug report instead of feature suggestion, as while I don't believe it is a regression, or a classic bug, the behavior is different from the description of the bbcode implementation [in the docs](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/ui/bbcode_in_richtextlabel.html). When specifying the width of an image in bbcode with `[img=100%]{path}[/img]`, the number gets interpreted as pixels, instead of the expected behavior as percentage of label width. Behavior of `[img width=100%]{path}[/img]` is correct. ### Steps to reproduce New Project -> Add Image ([example](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Six_weeks_old_cat_%28aka%29.jpg/330px-Six_weeks_old_cat_%28aka%29.jpg)) to project -> Add Control Node and RichTextLabel as a child -> make it take up the entire screen -> Enable `bbcode_enables` -> Add the following text: ``` [img=50%]res://cat.png[/img] [img width=50%]res://cat.png[/img] ``` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [MRP-bbcode.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25664030/MRP-bbcode.zip)

godotengine/godot#116936.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.6.stable.official.89cea1439 and v4.6.1.stable.official.14d19694e

### System information

macOS with Apple M3 CPU

### Issue description

`FileAccess` cannot access files using their `uid://` paths in exported builds, even when it can access their corresponding `res://` paths (got via `ResourceUID.uid_to_path` for example).
This only happens in exported builds.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clone the MRP
2. Export the project
3. Run the exported project
4. Check for the message `FileAccess.file_exists(uid_path) -> false` indicating that `FileAccess` cannot access the file from its `uid://` path, while `FileAccess.file_exists(res_path) -> true` indicates that it can access the same file via its `res://` path

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

https://github.com/gilzoide/godot-fileaccess-uid-bug
godotengine/godot#116936.title: `FileAccess` fails to access `uid://` paths in exported builds even when their corresponding `res://` path works

godotengine/godot#116975.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e]
v4.7.dev1.official [bf95b6258]

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - GeForce GTX 550 Ti - Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-2100 CPU @ 3.10GHz (4 threads) - 11.95 GiB memory

### Issue description

When the replace (ctrl + r) input box is focused, `enter` has to be pressed twice to replace the next occurrence.

A possible cause:
The blinking cursor disappears from the replace input box on the first `enter` press, and it returns on the second.

### Steps to reproduce

- open the project: issue-replace-text-requires-enter-twice.zip
- in the file system panel, double click: new_script.gd
- in the script editor, left click on the first line to place the cursor there
- press: `ctrl + f`
- type: `abc`
- press: `ctrl + r`
- type: `x`
- note the blinking cursor in the replace input box
- press: `enter`
- as expected, the first occurrence of 'abc' was replaced with 'x'
- but, now the blinking cursor has disappeared from the replace input box. The replace input box seems to still be focused because it has a blue border.
- press: `enter`
- nothing was replaced, but now the blinking cursor has returned to the replace input box
- press: `enter`
- now the second occurrence of 'abc' was replaced, but again the blinking cursor disappeared
- press: `enter`
- the blinking cursor returned
- press: `enter`
- now the third occurrence of 'abc' was replaced, but again the blinking cursor disappeared
- Issue, enter has to be pressed twice to replace the next occurrence.

Expected: pressing `enter` once should replace the next occurrence

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[issue-replace-text-requires-enter-twice.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25693257/issue-replace-text-requires-enter-twice.zip)
godotengine/godot#116975.title: Replace (ctrl + r) requires pressing enter twice

godotengine/godot#116985.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e]
v4.7.dev1.official [bf95b6258]

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - GeForce GTX 550 Ti - Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-2100 CPU @ 3.10GHz (4 threads) - 11.95 GiB memory

### Issue description

The script editor loses focus when the find and replace bar is closed with `esc`, when the Replace or Replace All buttons are focused.

Workarounds:
Before the issue occurs:
- remember to `shift + tab` to the replace input box before pressing `esc`

 After the issue has occurred:
- mouse click on the script editor to be able to continue typing
- restore focus from the keyboard with:
- `ctrl + F1` switch to the 2d view
- `ctrl + F3` switch to the script editor

Where the issue doesn't occur:
The script editor becomes focused when the find or replace input boxes are focused and `esc` is pressed.


### Steps to reproduce

- open the project: issue-script-editor-loses-focus-on-esc.zip
- open `test.gd`
- press: `ctrl + f`
- type: `abc`
- press: `ctrl + r`
- press: `tab` to focus the button: Replace
- press: `esc`
- the find and replace bar closes, 
- issue, the script editor isn't focused.

Expected:
Focus the script editor when the find and replace bar is closed with `esc`.
This already happens when the find or replace input boxes are focused and `esc` is pressed.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

godotengine/godot#117081.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.7.dev2 ### System information Godot v4.7.dev2 - macOS Tahoe (26.3.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro (Apple7) - Apple M1 Pro (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory ### Issue description It appears that Godot is assuming the return types of functions that do not have a return type specified. This is causing a lot of `Cannot return value of type "X" because the function return type is "Y"` errors in my plugin (GUT). I turned all "Error" to "Ignore" and the issue still happens. One simple example is I have a method that the user is expected to override if they want to make GUT skip a test script while running tests. ```gdscript class_name GutTest # res://addons/gut/test.gd ... func should_skip_script(): return false ``` GUT will skip the script if the user returns `true` or a `String`. All other values returned are treated as `false`. The `String` is displayed in the logs if it is returned. Here are two example implementations given in the wiki: ```gdscript extends GutTest func should_skip_script(): if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless": return "Skip Input tests when running headless" ``` ```gdscript extends GutTest func should_skip_script(): return EngineDebugger.is_active() ``` In the editor it marks the string return as an error because it thinks that the return value is a `bool`. There are zero declarations of `should_skip_script` in the GUT project that have a declared return type. I attempted to declare a return type of `Variant`, but that causes parsing errors on the `DisplayServer` example, since not all paths return a value. This would be a feasible workaround, but it isn't a great solution. There could be many users that are using this example in CICD pipelines. This also appears to be affecting my dynamic Script doubling logic as there are many tests failing due to `Cannot return value of type ...` errors, or `A void function cannot return a value.` errors. I have not looked in that yet though, since the above case is way less complicated. I imagine it has to do with the fact that return types weren't supplied in the metadata, so all my wrappers `return super()` by default. ### Steps to reproduce # Successful reproduction * clone https://github.com/bitwes/Gut * Open the editor * Click the "GUT" bottom panel * Click "Run All" # Failed to reproduce I was unable to reproduce the issue with a simple project that had two inner classes in the same file that replicated the definitions of `should_skip_script`. I was also unable to replicate it with two files with `class_name` that replicated the definitions of `should_skip_script`. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) clone https://github.com/bitwes/Gut
godotengine/godot#117081.title: Inferred return values are breaking existing code

godotengine/godot#117082.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.7.dev2.official [778cf54da] ### System information Godot v4.7.dev2 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8228) - AMD Ryzen 5 1500X Quad-Core Processor (8 threads) - 15.92 GiB memory ### Issue description "DPITexture" import does not have the "Fix Alpha Border" option, which means that in many circumstances it'll make these outlines (left). The "Texture2D" import (right) doesn't have this problem as it has "Fix Alpha Border" enabled by default. By manually disabling it you can get results that are practically identical in some circumstances. I deliberated for a while over whether this was technically a proposal but I eventually decided that it felt unintended, since "Fix Alpha Border" usually defaults to being enabled. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Import a `DPITexture` (with non-black borders), and place it onto a `Sprite2D`. - To better see the issue, place it on a background of a similar color (e.g. a `ColorRect` or even a copy of the same `Sprite2D`). 2. Adjust the `Sprite2D` in a way that it doesn't perfectly snap to the viewport. It's easiest to either: a. Zoom the editor viewport such that it is not a perfect multiple of the default b. Adjust the position, offset, rotation, or scale with non-integer values 3. There should be a fairly clearly visible black border. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25755360/issue.zip)

godotengine/godot#117103.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e], v4.7.dev2.official [778cf54da]
- Not reproducible in: v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - CachyOS Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 28 Feb 2026 14:31:21 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (nvidia; 590.48.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9700K CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads) - 31.13 GiB memory

### Issue description

When calling `is_valid_filename()` on a `String` or even `StringName` node property I get a crash when running a *release build on Linux*. There is no crash when running a debug build. Also there was no crash when running a Windows release build via wine.

The crash ends with a message `Segmentation fault (core dumped)` when running in a bash shell or `terminated by signal SIGSEGV (Address boundary error)` when running in a fish shell.

Example of a crashing code:
```gdscript
var x: String = $LineEdit.text
assert(x == "")
x.is_valid_filename()  # Crashes here
```
The following code doesn't crash:
```gdscript
var x: String = $LineEdit.text
assert(x == "")
x = ""  # Added line, is assert lying?
x.is_valid_filename()
```
Short version that also crashes:
```gdscript
LineEdit.new().text.is_valid_filename()
```

Similar thing happens with `StringName` properties such as `AnimationPlayer.current_animation`.

The culprit behind `String.is_valid_filename()` crashing seems to be https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104280 ~but `StringName.is_valid_filename()` is also crashing so more investigation is needed.~

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP project
2. Export the project with Linux preset (Make sure to uncheck "Export With Debug" to make a release build)
3. Run the resulting binary

Script version:
```
godot --headless --export-release Linux
./bin/segfault.x86_64
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[segfault.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25767107/segfault.zip)
godotengine/godot#117103.title: Segmentation fault in Linux release builds when calling is_valid_filename() on node properties.

godotengine/godot#117111.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.6.1

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Android 13 (SDK 33 build V14.0.2.0.TKIEUSF) - Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL ES 3 (Compatibility) - Adreno (TM) 642 -  (8 threads)

### Issue description

When trying to save a file from an exported Android project using Gradle Build and using the FileDialog's `Open Folder` file mode (`FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_DIR`), the app immediately crashes. This error appears twice in Godot's Debugger panel:
```
E 0:00:06:184   control.gd:22 @ _on_file_dialog_dir_selected(): File must be opened before use.
  <C++ Error>   Condition "!is_open()" is true. Returning: false
  <C++ Source>  platform/android/file_access_filesystem_jandroid.cpp:242 @ store_buffer()
  <Stack Trace> control.gd:22 @ _on_file_dialog_dir_selected()
```
If the exported project is not using Gradle build, the crash does not occur and the file gets saved normally.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create an empty project with a Button and a FileDialog. The FileDialog must be a native dialog and its file mode set to Open Folder, and Access mode set to File System.
- Attach a script to the root node and paste the following code (and connect the appropriate signals):
```gdscript
extends Control

const ICON := preload("res://icon.svg")

@onready var file_dialog: FileDialog = $FileDialog


func _on_save_image_button_pressed() -> void:
	file_dialog.popup_file_dialog()


func _on_file_dialog_dir_selected(dir: String) -> void:
	var file_path := dir
	if OS.get_name() == "Android":
		file_path += "#" + "icon.png"
	else:
		file_path = file_path.path_join("icon.png")
	ICON.get_image().save_png(file_path)
```
- Go to Project > Export, add an Android preset and enable "Use Gradle Build". Then, go to Project > Install Android Build Template
- Run the project in your Android device (I did it from my PC using Remote Deploy, while my phone was connected to my PC via USB)
- Attempt to save the file. The app should crash.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[android-saf-save-image.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25776788/android-saf-save-image.zip)
godotengine/godot#117111.title: Android: App using Gradle Build crashes when saving files with FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_DIR

godotengine/godot#117121.body:
Note:
- Better solution for `CLASS`: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/117850

When a GDScript function gets called there is an overhead from setting up and cleaning up the stack. Some of that time is spent referencing and unreferencing the script and the instance, because we always make those available on the stack and storing them in a variant triggers ref counting.

For functions that don't actually use those stack entries this work isn't really necessary. This PR saves whether a function uses self or its class. And if not leaves those stack spaces uninitialized, in an attempt to reduce the call overhead in some situations.


<details>
<summary>For a very specific benchmark that tests a pure function that just does math I see around a 10% improvement.</summary>

```gdscript
extends Node

var iter : int = 1_000_000
var test_strick : float = 0.0
var time : float = 0.0

func sqrt_v1(num:int) -> float:
	return num**0.5
	
func _ready() -> void:
	for ii in range(5):
		time = Time.get_unix_time_from_system()
		for i:int in range(iter):
			test_strick += sqrt_v1(i)
		print(Time.get_unix_time_from_system() - time)
	get_tree().quit()
```
</details>

However this is unlikely to translate to real projects, since a most functions will need those stack entries. I don't have any realistic projects to test against so I'll just put it as draft for now.

Actual benchmark data (benchmark script slightly altered): [stack_uninit.ods](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26064276/stack_uninit.ods)
godotengine/godot#117121.title: [POC] GDScript: Reduce call overhead for functions that don't use `self` or class

godotengine/godot#117159.body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible since 4.5 (accesskit integration) ### System information Godot v4.6.stable - Linux Mint 22.3 (Zena) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 (nvidia; 535.288.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8300H CPU @ 2.30GHz (8 threads) - 23.19 GiB memory ### Issue description In the editor, there is a setting to turn on accessibility warnings that show up on controls that are missing a text accessibility_name. I think this is a useful feature! If it was just applied to nodes that are relevant. I do want to know if a button is missing it. Having it show up on every container with the text "Accessibility name must not be empty" is incorrect and might have developers implement accessibility incorrectly (like me!). You should not be encouraged to add an accessibility name on every container. Most screen readers will probably skip it because it's not an interactive element, but some may not. And some elements are "interactive" like the ScrollContainer where you probably don't want the screen reader to speak its accessibility name. There are controls that clearly need it, like a button. And there are controls that clearly don't need it, like a vbox. Then there are a few controls that are up for debate: ScrollContainer, and Custom nodes to name two. I think the best choice here would be to display a warning on those, and change the tooltip to "Accessibility name is empty. Consider adding the name and description if this control should be announced by the screen reader." ### Steps to reproduce N/A ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A
godotengine/godot#117159.title: Accessibility warnings on controls are misleading

### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.7 220b0b2
Not reproducible in 4.6

Likely from #109184 CC @NasheethAhmedA 

### System information

W10

### Issue description

If there is a Path3D in a scene, every time you switch to that scene you get
```
ERROR: Signal 'curve_changed' is already connected to given callable 'Path3DEditorPlugin::_update_toolbar' in that object.
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add Path3D node
2. Change to another scene tab

### Issue description

In the provided scene there's a capsule-shaped dynamic body gliding over the boundary between two perfectly  joined height field shapes that are part of the same static body, and ends up with what seems like a ghost collision, despite 'Enhanced Internal Edge Removal' being enabled on both bodies.

### Tested versions

Reproduced in:
v4.7.dev2.official [778cf54da]
v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e]

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev2 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - GeForce GTX 550 Ti - Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-2100 CPU @ 3.10GHz (4 threads) - 11.95 GiB memory

### Issue description

In the FileSystem panel, dragging a script to or from a folder, shows the error message:

> ERROR: Another resource is loaded from path 'res://a/new_script.gd' (possible cyclic resource inclusion).

Reordering the script shows the error message every time.

The same error message is reported here:
Another resource is loaded from path (possible cyclic resource inclusion). #116551

But that issue describes having to create and remove an exported resource multiple times to cause the error.
> Check console output logs -- sometimes the error is difficult to reproduce.
After anywhere from 5 to 20 tries you should see the error.


### Steps to reproduce

- open the project: [error-when-reordering-script.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25822800/error-when-reordering-script.zip)
- in the filesystem panel
drag `new_script.gd` to the folder: `a`
- a dialog opens:
> Please Confirm...
Move 1 selected item to "res://a"?
- click: ok
- open the output panel

Issue:
The output panel shows:
>   ERROR: Another resource is loaded from path 'res://a/new_script.gd' (possible cyclic resource inclusion).

The same error appears when dragging the script from the folder `a` to `res://`
>  ERROR: Another resource is loaded from path 'res://new_script.gd' (possible cyclic resource inclusion).


Expected:
No error message when reordering a script.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[error-when-reordering-script.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25822800/error-when-reordering-script.zip)

It's just a new project with:
- a folder, named: a
- an empty script: new_script.gd

godotengine/godot#117246.body:
### Tested versions

4.6.1

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19044) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 27.20.100.9664) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1165G7 @ 2.80GHz (8 threads) - 39.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

When editing a script in the script editor, autocomplete is inaccessible. It appears, but NVDA doesn't see it, or any suggestions it may have. This makes using it rather annoying since I don't know what it will suggest before I accept it.

It is definitely possible to make it so that NVDA can see autocomplete; for example, this is done in both VS Code, Visual Studio itself, and Notepad++ (although Notepad++ requires an add-on to NVDA). I'm not sure how helpful the NPP add-on source code would be to the Godot project though.

### Steps to reproduce

With NVDA or another screen reader active:

1. Create a project as you ordinarily would.
2. Attach a script to a node and open the script editor.
3. Try typing in something that would trigger autocomplete. NVDA will not detect the list at all, although your focus may be trapped in the list and you may need to press escape quite a few times to break out of it. If you navigate with the up and down arrows, NVDA will read the same line repeatedly, instead of reading the next or prior lines, which is an indicator that autocomplete is active.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#117246.title: [Accessibility] Autocomplete in script editor is inaccessible

godotengine/godot#117276.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.7.dev2, 4.6.1.stable
Not reproducible in: 4.5.1.stable


### System information

Windows 11 (build 26200) - Godot v4.6.1.stable - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9159) - AMD Ryzen AI 7 H 350 w/ Radeon 860M (16 threads) - 31.11 GiB memory

### Issue description

When right-clicking an existing key(suppose at time `t1`) and then left-clicking an empty space on the same track(suppose at time `t2`), and deselect the key, then select any key again, these keys visually shift by `t2-t1` during selection. When deselected, the key shifts back to its correct original time.

Expected behavior: Keys should remain at their actual time positions when selected.

Behavior in 4.6.1-stable:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4f3eec2-203f-4572-888d-49f3730d2db2

Behavior in 4.5.1-stable:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/51310874-6177-44cb-a986-bdd77ec300ab

### Steps to reproduce

1. Right-click on an existing keyframe (suppose this is at time `t1`).
2. Left-click on an empty position on the track of that keyframe (suppose this is at time `t2`).
3. Left-click somewhere else in the editor to deselect the key.
4. Select any keyframe again.
5. The selected keys visually shift their position by `t2 - t1`. 
6. Deselect the keys, they shift back to their actual correct time.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25857694/mrp.zip)

godotengine/godot#117315.body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible : 4.5.1, 4.6.1
Not reproducible : 4.4.1

### System information

Linux Pop!_OS 22.04 jammy (x86-64), Godot v4.6.1.stable.flathub [14d19694e]

### Issue description

When checking if a process is running, it raises an error even if the process exists. But, it seems like raising an error only when it believes the process is not running.

```
    var is_running = OS.is_process_running(%SpinBox.value)
    print(["Is processing running: ", is_running])

    is_running = OS.is_process_running(OS.get_process_id())
    print(["Is processing running: ", is_running])
```

Maybe I'm wrong, but I expect the `is_process_running` to just return false or true, not to raise an error if the id I passed is not running ; no matter the reason it's not running, not existing for example.

I believe the issue to be from this commit (not sure though) : https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/d5738b5cdd43cb5e5f118cc206af6b2a711169f2

It changed the code where the issue is links to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/f62fdbde15035c5576dad93e586201f4d41ef0cb/drivers/unix/os_unix.cpp#L853

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start the 4.5.1 project and enter a PID (a real one or fake one, it doesn't seem to change anything)
2. See the error in the logs
3. Start the 4.4.1 project
4. See the lack of errors.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

You will find two projects, one with 4.4.1 and one with 4.5.1. One working as expected, one raising errors.
[test_process.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25894757/test_process.zip)

godotengine/godot#117325.body:
### Tested versions

4.6.1, 4.7 (from git)

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev (9095a76ff) - Windows 10 (build 19044) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 27.20.100.9664) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1165G7 @ 2.80GHz (8 threads) - 39.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

Currently, properties in the inspector, project settings, editor settings, etc., don't tell us what they actually do. As a (very obvious) example, I know that, e.g., `Node3D` has a `Position` property, but it doesn't tell me what that does. For `Position` the meaning is obvious, but for others this is not so.

My request is that the Accessible Description attribute be set on the property, if this information is available from code, describing what the property does. For example, if the property has a doc comment or the engine can figure out the documentation for this property, set this attribute to a (plane text) description of it. I'm not entirely sure how this works internally, so I hope this is enough detail. My idea is that, for something like inspector properties, the engine would just extract doc comments, and if a doc comment existed for that property, the engine would set the Accessible Description attribute for that property to the doc comment. This wouldn't be set on the inspector-generated controls for that property (e.g., x/y/z spin boxes for a Vector3), but on the property node itself.

### Steps to reproduce

Using a screen reader (e.g., NVDA), open any project and go into the inspector or any of the settings dialogs. If you go to "Property: X" where `X` is the property name, what it does is uncertain.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#117325.title: [Accessibility] Feature request: set accessible description attribute on properties

### Tested versions

Godot v4.6.1.stable

I think this was working in 4.5

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA T1000 8GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8142) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-14700 (28 threads) - 47.69 GiB memory

### Issue description

I'm getting a crash in a plugin I developed which was working fine in Godot 4.0 through 4.5, but has an issue in 4.6 and the master 4.7 dev branch.  

I was able to track it down to :
```
margin_container.cpp, line 44
		Size2 s = c->get_combined_minimum_size();
```

For some reason the method wrapper is passing a null pointer as a Variant to the parameter list which causes the crash.

This is caused indirectly by adding a dock control to the editor, which in turn tries to resize a MarginContainer, which leads to the above call.

### Steps to reproduce

Load the attached project.  Open the main.tscn panel in the editor.  In the Scene panel, click back and forth between the CoolBeans nodes and the background to select/deselect them a few times.  After a couple of clicks back and forth, the project will crash.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

godotengine/godot#117356.body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: master [e295eba0eb4767fd45ea8534765307d8e9b1c328]
- Not reproducible in: v4.7.dev2.official [778cf54da]

### System information

 Godot v4.7.dev (e295eba0e) - Linux Mint 22.3 (Zena) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon 780M Graphics (RADV PHOENIX) - AMD Ryzen 7 8745H w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads) - 25.22 GiB memory

### Issue description

After starting the editor, opening the document first and then the script may result in the following error:


```bash
ERROR: Parameter "script_text_editor" is null.
   at: _update_bookmark_list (editor/script/script_editor_base.cpp:137)
ERROR: Parameter "script_text_editor" is null.
   at: _update_breakpoint_list (editor/script/script_text_editor.cpp:139)
```

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ce79ef75-4f0c-4de6-b08a-766f1c314880

Bisected to f51857cbafc6bbea442c5717631056aaabf8a866 ( #117126 ) CC @kitbdev 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch the editor;
2. Ensure no scripts or documents are open;
3. If any are open, close all (scripts) and reload the current project;
4. Open any document;
5. Open any GD script;
6. Observe for errors.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#117356.title: An error may occur when opening the script for the first time

godotengine/godot#117386.body:
### Tested versions

Regression from d34bfe89fdc7ac029e2ea1940c32d92f6172e6c5 (#116971)


### System information

Godot v4.7.dev (d34bfe89f) - Linux Mint 22.3 (Zena) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon 780M Graphics (RADV PHOENIX) - AMD Ryzen 7 8745H w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads) - 25.22 GiB memory

### Issue description

Launch the Project Manager and enter some key presses for testing. The following error message may occur:

```bash
ERROR: Unknown Shortcut: editor/open_search.
   at: is_shortcut (editor/settings/editor_settings.cpp:2071)
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch the Project Manager using the terminal;
2. Press certain keys, such as: `Ctrl`, `Alt`, `Shift`;
3. Observe the output in the terminal.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
godotengine/godot#117386.title: Pressing keys in the Project Manager may result in errors

godotengine/godot#117389.body:
### Tested versions

4.6 stable

### System information

Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8183) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10750H CPU @ 2.60GHz (12 threads) - 15.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

This is one issue I want to report for soooo long, but I never got the time.

When working with Path3D and Modo navigation shortcuts (which are very nice for people without a middle button, e.g. pen tablet), Godot will spawn new points each time you zoom, pan, rotate the camera:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87b64b36-5020-4478-b979-fa368c23145c

That is very annoying 🥲 

A similar issue was solved for the Gridmap, not sure if it makes sense to use the same solution: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104213

### Steps to reproduce

- Change navigation scheme to Modo
- Create a Path3D, and go in Add Point mode
- Try to use ALT, ALT+SHIFT, ALT+CTRL to navigate

You can also experience the issue in Select Point mode, when using ATL+CTRL.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Default Empty Project.
godotengine/godot#117389.title: Modo shortcuts conflicts with Path3D shortcuts: adding new points when navigating

godotengine/godot#117396.body:
### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.7.dev [a8e37fc01048823f639c4011af645d2e8e2b79d3] ### System information Godot v4.7.dev (a8e37fc01) - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory ### Issue description Despite the efforts made in #105453 and [the claim](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/a8e37fc01048823f639c4011af645d2e8e2b79d3/doc/classes/Object.xml#L534) in the `Object` documentation about everything related to signals being thread-safe, they are unfortunately not so. This script should crash the process, especially if you run it with a `use_asan=yes` build: ```gdscript extends Node class TransientClass: signal some_signal class PersistentClass: func do_nothing() -> void: pass static var persistent := PersistentClass.new() static func _on_task(_index: int) -> void: TransientClass.new().some_signal.connect(persistent.do_nothing) func _ready() -> void: var task := WorkerThreadPool.add_group_task(_on_task, 100_000) WorkerThreadPool.wait_for_group_task_completion(task) get_tree().quit() ``` ... with the following AddressSanitizer report:
