godotengine/godot-proposals#12093.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

A 2D platforming game featuring moving and *rotating* platforms.

Note: this is related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7483

In my fork, with 1-way collision direction. The platform is updating its 1-way direction to always point up

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1f7205ee-b870-4bef-9f02-d9d31f504459

In 4.4, without:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cb238dc4-137d-4852-ae2f-b77c59a0f254

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

CollisionShape2Ds support 1-way collisions, but the engine only allows for the 1-way collision direction to be "up" (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/87f897ae0ad5a6770c6ee267e2f3458c89a06df7/modules/godot_physics_2d/godot_space_2d.cpp#L640 and elsewhere).

As my platforms rotate, I want the 1-way direction to remain globally up, but this is not currently possible.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

If collision geometry supports a one way collision direction, then I can update this collision to continue pointing up as the shape rotates.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

My fork with the feature in action.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1f7205ee-b870-4bef-9f02-d9d31f504459

Add a Vector2 to Shape called one_way_collision_direction which should be a normalized vector (similar to `up_direction` on CharacterBody2D). This would also be an exported property. Then, when creating a `GodotPhysicsServer2D::CollCbkData`, the `valid_dir` is set to

`col_obj_shape_xform.columns[1].normalized().rotated(one_way_collision_direction.angle())`

rather than `col_obj_shape_xform.columns[1].normalized()`. In essence, this means that the collision direction is the shape's "up", rotated by collision direction.

This Vector2 would be exposed on `CollisionShape2D` as a property called `one_way_collision_direction` and getters and setters would be created for it.

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

Unfortunately, it cannot. `GodotPhysicsServer2D::CollCbkData` has support for a `valid_dir`, but that variable is only set in cpp (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/87f897ae0ad5a6770c6ee267e2f3458c89a06df7/modules/godot_physics_2d/godot_space_2d.cpp#L640 and elsewhere) and it is only set to `col_obj_shape_xform.columns[1].normalized()`. I cannot simply override the value of `col_obj_shape_xform.columns[1].normalized()`, as this has other implications.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

I don't think that it can be done in an asset.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12093.title: Add one-way collision direction for CollisionShape2Ds

godotengine/godot-proposals#12234.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

The Godot editor 🙂 

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

Godot runs into many driver bugs when using Vulkan on Windows. All GPU vendors are affected, but Intel generally has the Vulkan driver that runs into the most issues in Godot (such as https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/86833).

On top of that, Vulkan allows applications to install *layers* which affect the rendering of any Vulkan application on the system. While this is an useful tool, applications often install broken layers or don't clean up layer files after uninstalling, which leads to various issues. In the worst case scenario, [Godot is completely unable to start with Vulkan](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/82483) if a broken layer makes it crash on startup. In comparison, Direct3D 12 doesn't have a layer system, so it's impossible for this to happen.[^1]

[^1]: Vulkan layers are also supported on Linux, but are far less problematic overall due to apps being more behaved with layer usage there.

To summarize, the state of Vulkan drivers on Windows is less than ideal. It's overall better than OpenGL, but not quite at the level of Direct3D.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

[Godot has a Direct3D 12 rendering driver since 4.0](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/70315), but it's not used by default.

Using Direct3D 12 by default would sidestep the Vulkan driver bugs that Godot runs into. Additionally, we gain a few upsides for using Direct3D 12:

- Godot is automatically presented using DXGI when using D3D12, [which is not the case when using Vulkan](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/5692).
- [Special K](https://www.special-k.info/) injection works correctly when using D3D12, while it always crashes Godot on startup when using Vulkan (at least in my experience).
  - It's possible that this is a Godot bug, but I don't know what's causing this.

There are still a few roadblocks to using Direct3D 12 by default that we need to resolve first:

- There is currently no shader pipeline caching. This impacts loading times and can result in increased shader compilation stutter.
  - This was resolved by https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102552, but only if you export the project with the shader baker enabled. This must be done from a Windows device, which is an issue for those who don't have a Windows setup or are exporting from a Linux CI setup (as it's typically more convenient than using a dedicated Windows instance).
- ~~[OpenXR lacks Direct3D 12 support with Godot.](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10078)~~
  - This was implemented by https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104207.
- There are several bugs specific to the Direct3D 12 driver.
  - These are listed in the [tracker issue](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/95775).

#### Does this affect visuals in existing projects?

Visuals are expected to be 100% identical across rendering drivers (barring renderer or driver bugs). Therefore, this should not lead to visual changes in existing projects unless you run into a bug. Please report any such bugs when using Direct3D 12; make sure to include comparison screenshots between Direct3D 12 and Vulkan.

#### What about performance?

Unlike the Metal rendering driver that replaces MoltenVK by default on macOS, there isn't expected to be a significant performance difference between Vulkan and Direct3D 12 on most GPUs. This may differ on Intel GPUs which are known to have performance problems in Vulkan applications, so the D3D12 driver *may* perform better on those GPUs.

If you want to check this for yourself, try switching your project's `rendering/rendering_device/driver.windows` project setting to `d3d12`. If you run into large performance drops that don't occur in Vulkan, please open an issue with a minimal reproduction project attached.

#### What if my GPU doesn't support Direct3D 12?

Any GPU that supports Vulkan also supports Direct3D 12 on Windows. If this was not the case anyway (or if you use a binary compiled without Direct3D 12 support), Godot automatically falls back to Vulkan since 4.4. If Vulkan is not supported either, then Godot automatically falls back to OpenGL.

> [!NOTE]
>
> **Projects that use the Compatibility rendering method are not affected by this proposal.** Compatibility uses OpenGL by default, with a fallback to ANGLE on specific graphics drivers (which translates OpenGL to Direct3D 11).

#### What about users compiling their own binaries?

You need to go through [some extra steps](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/compiling/compiling_for_windows.html#compiling-with-support-for-direct3d-12) to enable Direct3D 12 support in self-compiled binaries.

This proposal would also enable Direct3D 12 support by default in self-compiled binaries. Compilation will fail if the D3D12 dependencies aren't installed. Users can still pass `d3d12=no` if they wish to build Vulkan-only binaries without having to install Direct3D 12 dependencies.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

Change this line in `main/main.cpp`:

```diff
-		GLOBAL_DEF_RST(PropertyInfo(Variant::STRING, "rendering/rendering_device/driver.windows", PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM, "vulkan,d3d12"), "vulkan");
+		GLOBAL_DEF_RST(PropertyInfo(Variant::STRING, "rendering/rendering_device/driver.windows", PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM, "d3d12,vulkan"), "d3d12");
```

Also change this line in `SConstruct`:

```diff
-opts.Add(BoolVariable("d3d12", "Enable the Direct3D 12 rendering driver on supported platforms", False))
+opts.Add(BoolVariable("d3d12", "Enable the Direct3D 12 rendering driver on supported platforms", True))
```

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

No.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

This is about changing a rendering default setting.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12234.title: Use Direct3D 12 rendering driver by default on Windows

godotengine/godot-proposals#12323.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

Godot core.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

A first-class types is when you can ask an Object for its type, and then use or interact with that type as if it were an object itself.

Godot is lacking first-class types.
Instead, `ClassDB` usually stands in for class-related functionality, such as:
- Whether `A` inherits from `B` (although admittedly, `Object` duplicates this behavior).
- The parent class name of `A` (`get_parent_class_nocheck`)
- The class name hierarchy of `A` (`get_inheritance_chain_nocheck`)
- Whether `A` is virtual (`is_virtual`)
- Which properties `A` has (`get_property_setter`)
- Which virtual methods `A` has (`add_virtual_method`)
- ... and, well, almost everything else type related.

This is unidiomatic and slow:
- It requires the global read lock for every type query, even for trivial questions such as the parent class.
- It requires `HashMap` lookups by class name, even if the type is known statically.

Meanwhile, `Object` also offers some of its own class-like functionality as virtual function hacks. For example, `is_class` is a virtual function that goes through each subclass, and checks a single `StringName` against the input string. It would be much faster if there was a single, static, contiguous list of the class name hierarchy to make a quick check.

- This is a necessary pre-step for https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10115.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

It would be better if class information was stored in first-class `Object` types, rather than scattered around `ClassDB` hash maps and `Object` virtual functions.

First-class types could be accessed by pointer, which can be _far_ faster than the previously mentioned hacks. It can also be more easily reasoned with.

The types can (later) be expanded to be used by `Variant` types as well, to de-duplicate functionality.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

`Object` instances need a pointer to their type (`GDType`).

We can start with a simple implementation that stores information such as super type and class name.
Later, we can add other functionality such as method and property infos (basically most of what's stored in `ClassDB` right now).

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

Nope.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

It's core.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12323.title: Core: Add first-class `Object` types, replacing (most of) `ClassDB`

godotengine/godot-proposals#12487.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

A stealth action game with complex blended animations.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

When any node is placed as a point within a `BlendSpace` through the interface, the point is assigned a int index as its name, from 0, ascending from its creation time.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b55e758-fc54-470c-8d59-387308071be8)

So, if I create a `BlendSpace2D` and create four nested `BlendTree`s within, and want to manipulate them in code, I have to keep track of their indexes 0, 1, 2 and 3. However, if I delete number 1, then 3 becomes 2, 2 becomes 1, and all lines in my code referencing these paths need to be edited to accommodate this.

As you can imagine, this gets very unwieldy when there are a large number of points being edited, in addition to the fact that each point's index is impossible to glance without entering every individual point's editor. I would imagine any new user encountering this quirk would feel an incentive to never nest within `BlendSpace`s at all, I know I did.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

Therefore, I propose some changes, which could aid consistency across `AnimationTree`:
1. Adding Class properties on the Inspector for all `AnimationNode` resources, not just `AnimationNodeAnimation`.
2. In this, expose a "Name" property that can be manually defined by the user, the same property as the node names in `BlendTree` or state names in `StateMachine`.
3. In `BlendSpace`, instead of the index system, default names be assigned in normal `BlendTree` and `StateMachine` convention: First added `BlendSpace1D` has the name "BlendSpace1D", the second has "BlendSpace1D 2", then "BlendSpace1D 3", and so on. This "name" is now what is used as the path for points nested within a `BlendSpace`.
5. Allow editing of blend position from the sidebar as well, so that precise entry is now possible for a `BlendSpace` point, as well as being able to see it's name without needing to enter its editor with "Open Editor" first.
6. Finally, a small QOL feature: when clicking away to deselect a node/point in `BlendTree`, `StateMachine` or `BlendSpace` editors, revert the selection to the parent. So, if I have a `BlendSpace2D` within which is an `AnimationNodeAnimation` point, clicking on the point will give me its inspector, but clicking away into blank space will revert the currently selected node to be the `BlendSpace2D` that we are currently inside.

From what I can tell, this index system that `BlendSpace` currently offers no way to properly change points' index via the interface, and also is inconsistent to the intuitive experience offered by `BlendTree` and `StateMachine`, that use `name: StringName` in their methods instead of the arbitrarily assigned and constantly changing `point: int`.

I have been unable to find someone online using it as part of their game code aside from #4417, and I couldn't personally think of a use case for the index system that is not possible with a name. I am wrong in this, please let me know.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c7dfa7dc-f294-42f8-8d91-5960df4ca1d3)

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

It will be used often.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

It is a core part of the engine interface and affects usability.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12487.title: Allow naming of BlendSpace points

godotengine/godot-proposals#12608.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

A 3D spaceship game with automatic gun tracking.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

There is no simple way to check if a basis is orthonormalized (due to floating-point errors, mentioned in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106789), and 3D rotations slowly accumulate rounding error, so orthonormalized() needs to be called after some time.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

Exposing `Basis::is_orthonormal()` in `basis.cpp` would provide a cheap way to check if a basis needs to be orthonormalized, since it only calculates squared lengths and dot products of the basis vectors.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

Add method bindings to `Basis::is_orthonormal()` in `variant_call.cpp`. The test cases already exist in `test_basis.h`. 

I can help open a PR if approved.

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

It can be worked around with `Basis.is_conformal()` and `Vector3.is_normalized()`, but not as conveniently or intuitively.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

`Basis` is a key property of 3D nodes in Godot.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12608.title: Expose `Basis.is_orthonormal()` to check if a basis is orthonormalized

godotengine/godot-proposals#12701.body: ### Describe the project you are working on Any project with content updates ### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project Godot can exclude resources from export, but it exports paths to all resources and metadata for all named classes in the project regardless of the export settings: This makes it impossible to keep anything in the project directory private. For example if you're working on a new map or a new mob type and don't want to leak it until you make an announcement, you can only do that by deleting all of those files entirely from your project and restarting godot. Which means you can't release patches for your game without leaking all of your future unreleased content. It also bloats pck unnecessarily ### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation Don't export uids and class metadata for unexported files ### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams I haven't looked into code but by looking at the exported `.pck` that data looks similar to data in `.godot/uid_cache.bin` and `.godot/global_script_class_cache.cfg`. So instead of including data from them directly it could be filtered based on what actually needs to be exported ### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script? You can only delete files and restart the editor to get them out of those caches ### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library? Not leaking excluded content is probably what developers expect
godotengine/godot-proposals#12701.title: Don't export metadata of excluded files in game builds

godotengine/godot-proposals#12780.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

A complex™ game with multiple language support.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

POT Generator is a useful tool, but it sometimes adds strings that shouldn't be added, or there are strings that can't be (easily) extracted. Unfortunately there is no way to easily customize POT Generator strings. There is EditorTranslationParserPlugin, but custom plugins can only replace existing plugins, not extend them, so you can't customize scene extraction.

I did once open https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11841, but it only handles deleting strings (based on my use-case).

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

EditorTranslationParserPlugin could get a new method: `_customize_strings()`. When generating POT, after all strings are extracted, the generator would go over all plugins and call this method, allowing to modify the list (add or delete strings).

Unlike https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11841, this approach is more flexible.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

The method could be: `_customize_strings(PackedStringArray &r_strings)`. If a plugin overrides this method, the generator would call it, providing the current array of strings. Then the plugin can modify the list of strings by directly modifying the provided array.

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

Not really. I do have a workaround, which involves manually reading the POT file and then writing it again with new set of strings. It works, but it's rather ugly and was annoying to write. I can't be the only person with this problem (right? ;_;).

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

It improves POT generator, making it more useful.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12780.title: Allow customizing POT Generator string list

godotengine/godot-proposals#12873.body:

Using procedural texture generation similar to custom NoiseTextures but focused on the GPU (for speed) and flexibility.
Generally this feature should be a positive for anyone using texture generation, RD textures, or texture Mipmaps.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

Rd Texture storage doesn't handle SRGB texture formats the same way that standard 2d textures do resulting in textures that can only be used in 2D or 3D (I need to use them in both).
In addition to that the texture generation means it is helpful to check on the mipmap layers to ensure they are as expected (I have also off loaded the mipmap generation in my project to the GPU, I have not included that here). 

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

Adds SRGB format support to rd_textures, enables TexturePreview for rd_textures and adds a mipmap selector to TexturePreview.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109004
See PR above for code, this is currently compiled and in use for my project locally so I figured it would be good to share.

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

It cannot, TexturePreview isn't accessable in gdscript not is the inner working of the rd_texture initilise function.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

As above, it couldn't function as an addon.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12873.title: Add LOD to TexturePreview and improve rd_texture usage

godotengine/godot-proposals#12945.body: ### Describe the project you are working on Godot ### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project Populating a scene in specific patterns/grid, or simply placing many instance of a given scene has always been a struggle (you need to drag&drop the scene many time or duplicate existing scenes). In the case of the grid placement, scene tiles from TileMapLayer have kind of solved the issue, but it lacks the editing flexibility the scene-tree editor (and the inspector) provide. Sadly, adding properties there as [this PR](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108626) implements has some issues: it saves a lot of data to the TileMapLayer node (hurting performance) and make an already complex API even more complex. ### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation I suggest we implement some sort of scene painter like suggested by [this proposal](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/6886), and kind of implemented by [this plugin](https://github.com/KoBeWi/Godot-Instance-Dock). However, those do not really cover the case of TileMap, and the plugin implementation maybe isn't as tightly integrated with the CanvasItemEditor as it could be. ### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams So, I suggest we implement a new mode in the 2D editor, with some options: We have: - A dropdown to select a scene to paint, with, compared to the usual .tscn selector, gives you a list of recently used scenes. Could be some sort of a palette too, if we want to save those. - A dropdown to select where to instantiate the scenes. If not set, use the root node as parent. - A configuration menu where you can select from 3 modes: - free painting: paint when you click, then every X pixels while dragging (X is configurable) Right click remove all scenes in a small area around the mouse that are children of the selected instances parent (in the top toolbar). - grid: use the 2D editor grid to paint scenes. A left click paints, and possibly replaces, in a given spot. Right click erases. - TileMapLayer: allows you to select a TileMapLayer node to use as the source of your grid. Works same as grid mode otherwise. I am not 100% sure about how to present the configuration, but you get the general idea. ### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script? This is core. ### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library? This is core.
godotengine/godot-proposals#12945.title: Add a 2D scene painter tool

godotengine/godot-proposals#13158.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

An SVG editor that often implements complex controls procedurally. I do so when performance is needed, when there's some fine-grained behavior Godot's controls can't accomplish on their own, or when a tree hierarchy adds more complexity than its worth.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

There are a lot of tools under Control to make complex interfaces work with a single node, only requiring the node to manage drawing, such as a `_get_tooltip()` method that allows different areas to have different tooltips. This is very useful for procedural controls, it exists because ItemList and Tree need optimized versions that work without separate nodes.

Mouse shape, however, is something you can't customize based on the cursor position.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

Godot controls allow themselves to override the `get_cursor_shape()` method, for example in code_edit.cpp. If this were a virtual method, I'd be able to override it too, allowing me to make more elaborate procedural controls. If Godot's controls can just override a method and get different cursor shapes based on the mouse position, why should I not be able to?

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

A virtual method `_get_cursor_shape(pos: Vector2) -> Control.CursorShape` should be implemented in Control, exposed, and called by `get_cursor_shape()`. In the internals, all `get_cursor_shape()` methods outside of control should be replaced with the virtual method, while the calls can stay as they are.

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

With separate nodes, which is pretty cursed.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

It's already core and used by Godot controls, it should just be exposed properly to be available to users too.
godotengine/godot-proposals#13158.title: Add a `_get_cursor_shape()` virtual method to Control

godotengine/godot-proposals#13350.body: ### Describe the project you are working on Some projects in Godot that I want to keep more stable, as well as other projects that are more experimental or dealing with Godot engine development ### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project When working with different versions of Godot, users may click on a project not realizing that it's on a lower version of Godot than the current one they are using. There is a pop-up that asks the user to confirm they'd like to upgrade the project, so it is difficult to accidentally do it. Given that there is already an indicator for a project being from a newer-than-current version of Godot, it does not seem that this would be a very difficult addition. ### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation Add an indicator to projects in the Project Manager that a project's version is *less than* the current Godot version (and thus that opening the project will upgrade it to the current version). ### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams Here, the project on Godot 4.7 has the exclamation icon because opening it would *downgrade* it; the project on 4.5 has a new "update" icon, because opening it would *upgrade* it. The icon choice certainly isn't set in stone; I chose it because [that is what came up when I Googled "update icon"](https://www.google.com/search?q=update+icon). A different icon could be chosen instead, although I think it should be easily distinguishable from the "warning" icon used for projects on newer versions. ### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script? It's a minor UX improvement that matches an already-existing behavior in the Project Manager. ### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library? The Project Manager cannot be modified by addons as far as I know.

godotengine/godot-proposals#13747.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

VFX and Godot Engine

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

Consolidating multiple proposal in a single one to give a better overview of my vision for particles.
- angle3d part: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13020
- transform align proposal: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/8367

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

One of the weaknesses left to tackle in the particle system is definitely the options that Godot offers for orienting and rotating particles:
- billboarding has to be implemented in a render shader, which is bad both for UX  and pipeline (explodes material variants on filesystem). Not to mention, VFX artists never have to do this in other engines themselves.
- tranform align "billboarding "option does not work with the built-in angle given in process material.
- tranform align "align to velocity" doesn't work at all for animated velocities like orbit, radial, etc.
- there's no built-in way as of now to orient particles in 3d space with some randomness
- there's no built-in way as of now to rotate particles around a local axis

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

1. Add "random orientation" property in particle process (supersedes angle3d from https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/112447, with a clearer name)

In transform align:
Rework properties in the following way

transform_align is removed

new options dropdown called `alignment` (name in progress, feel free to suggest)
1. billboard
    - camera, vertical, horizontal, (perhaps add screen later)
    - use angle (none, custom.x(default), custom.y, custom.z, custom.w)
2. velocity billboard (aligns to velocity and squishes when too tangent to the camera)
    - align to velocity (allow to not allign to velocity and just locally billboard)
    - axis : x,y,z
    - min axis stretch
    - falloff range
3. local rotate
   - axis (local x, localy, localz)
   - read from: custom.x,y,z, or w (no "none" option since it doesn't make sense here)
4. trails (currently align Y to velocity)
5. billboarded trails (currently align Y to velocity and z billboard)


### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

It can be worked around with shaders, but it's clunky, long, and worsen the project bloat

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

particle copy shader can't be changed in any other way
godotengine/godot-proposals#13747.title: Give better rotation options for particles

godotengine/godot-proposals#13786.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

Misc VR experiences.

In particular, the feature I wish to implement in my current project is one which gracefully handles the user donning/doffing a VR headset in social VR settings, so that removing their headset is not a jarring and body-distorting visual experience to other users.

My current approach is to make assumptions based on HMD positioning and inverse kinematics, but access to a ground-truth boolean from the XR device would be much more robust, accurate, and comfortable for end-users.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

Currently, I believe there is no way to obtain the XR device's commonly-existing presence sensor (often implemented as a proximity sensor inside an HMD).  The closest alternative is the [`session_begun`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/4.5/classes/class_openxrinterface.html#class-openxrinterface-signal-session-begun) signal, however, as the OpenXR documentation for [XRSessionState](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.0/man/html/XrSessionState.html) explains:

> If the system supports a user engagement sensor and runtime is in XR_SESSION_STATE_IDLE state, the runtime **may** wait until the user starts engaging with the device before transitioning to the XR_SESSION_STATE_READY state.

Additionally, the OpenXR documentaiton for [XR_EXT_user_presence](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XR_EXT_user_presence) further emphasizes:

> The user presence is fundamentally decoupled from the session lifecycle. Although the core spec for [XrSessionState](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XrSessionState) hinted potential correlation between the session state and user presence, in practice, such a connection **may** not consistently hold across various runtimes. Application **should** avoid relying on assumptions regarding these relationships between session state and user presence, instead, they should utilize this extension to reliably obtain user presence information.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

In order to accurately obtain user presence, implementing [XR_EXT_user_presence](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XR_EXT_user_presence) would resolve the current lack of confidence/consistency from relying on XR_SESSION_STATE_READY.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

I'm not sure how OpenXR extensions are implemented, but I'm open to learn if someone can point me in the right direction.

I would imaging this could produce signals like `presence_lost` and ~~`presence_gained`~~ `presence_regained`.  If there is a boolean value keeping track of the presence state, I would suggest it be named in the negative, akin to `presence_is_lost` and default to `false`.  That way, if a certain device does not have this extension implemented, the default value is to assume (but not outright claim) presence at all times.

*(To clarify if it were instead named in the positive such as "user_is_present", that could yield confusing assumptions for devices which do not implement this extension.  "user_is_present" returning "true" would imply that we **know** the user is present, whereas "presence_is_lost" returning "false" would only imply that presence has not been lost.  For devices which don't implement the extension, you cannot lose something that you never had* 🙂 *)*

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

I'm not sure if OpenXR extensions can be added via scripting.  If it can, I would love to receive resources on how I can go about doing this for my projects!

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

The presence sensor of XR devices (often headsets) is a very foundational and common feature; but, if others feel this should not be in core, I would accept this being an addon if someone can point me in the direction of how I can add an OpenXR extension via a custom addon.

EDIT:  
The supported runtimes for this extension can be found [here](https://github.khronos.org/OpenXR-Inventory/extension_support.html#XR_EXT_user_presence)  
And although not yet listed on Khronos' page, SteamVR added support for this extension [earlier this year](https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/250820/view/534338514199774696)
godotengine/godot-proposals#13786.title: Add `XR_EXT_user_presence` OpenXR extension

Patchwork, a real-time version control plugin for Godot.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

This exposes core functionality, so yes it must be in the core.

- *Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12262.*

### Describe the project you are working on

The Godot editor 🙂 

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

When using FileDialog or EditorFileDialog, you currently can't reference environment variables or the tilde symbol (`~`) as a shorthand for `$HOME` (`%USERPROFILE%` on Windows). In contrast, most system file managers support these.

This provides a quick way to jump to commonly used folders across applications, even if they are not listed in the favorites list.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

Expand environment variables and tilde symbol (`~`) as a shorthand for the user's home folder in FileDialog folder/file path fields.

The allowed syntax should match the host operating system (`%VARIABLE%` on Windows, `$VARIABLE` on all other platforms). Note that environment variable names are case-insensitive on Windows, while they're case-sensitive on all other platforms.

`~` as a shorthand for `$HOME` is normally only supported on non-Windows platforms, but it doesn't hurt to support on Windows too for convenience. In this case, it would map to `%USERPROFILE%`.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

Example with KDE's Dolphin:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bcadec29-de8e-4583-9e5b-98bd2c0dd1b2

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

No.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

This is about improving FileDialog and EditorFileDialog's usability, bringing it closer to native file dialogs.
godotengine/godot-proposals#13823.title: Expand environment variables in FileDialog folder/file path fields

godotengine/godot-proposals#13905.body:
### Describe the project you are working on

A GDScript-only project that has an Android build where I need to call an SDK with async events.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

I want to integrate Android APIs without having to write a Java/Kotlin plugin. [This docs page](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/tutorials/platform/android/javaclasswrapper_and_androidruntimeplugin.html) says it should be possible via `JavaClassWrapper` and `AndroidRuntime`. However, there's a limitation because you can't pass in callbacks  -- other than `Runnable` and `java.util.concurrent.Callable` that you may create via `AndroidRuntime`, but that is too specific (e.g. both take no arguments and cannot be used as callbacks everywhere).

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

There should be a way to pass arbitrary callbacks like how you can with `JavaScriptBridge.create_callback(callable: Callable)`, so you can truly interface with Java from GDScript-only code.

In Java, that would be an anonymous object for either:
1. a functional interface (equivalent to a lambda in Java code), i.e. it has only one method
2. or more generically, any interface(s), so it implements multiple methods; or even more generically, for any class, which extends and implements arbitrary Java classes and interfaces

So we need a generic way to create a Java object that can receive arbitrary callbacks from GDScript.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

The possible solutions depend on how much you'll allow to do from GDScript. The best implementation would be the last one, which is more complete and powerful, and allows to do almost any integration. But even the first one with just a single callback would already help a lot.
I'm assuming it's possible to do these using some reflection wizardry in Java.

#### 1. Allow creating callbacks
This solution would be the equivalent to `JavaScriptBridge.create_callback(callable: Callable)`.

Add a `JavaClassWrapper.callback(implementing: String|JavaClass, callable: Callable) -> JavaObject`:
- `implementing`: Java class which represents a functional interface (i.e., it has just one method to be implemented)
    - could be a `String` for its fully qualified name, or alternatively a `JavaClass` obtained from `wrap` or otherwise
- `callable`: a Godot callable which will be invoked as callback, passing the arguments from the Java interface method's signature
- `return`: an anonymous object implementing the specified functional interface

#### 2. Allow creating anonymous objects that can extend any class and implement any interfaces
This solution would be a much more powerful version. It could co-exist with the one above, or be just this, which already solves what can be achieved with the one above. It's basically a "reverse wrap", i.e., it makes a Java object that will call GDScript when called.

Add a `JavaClassWrapper.proxy(implementation: Object, super_class: String|JavaClass = 'java.lang.Object', ...interfaces: String|JavaClass) -> JavaObject`:
- `implementation`: Godot object to be wrapped in Java. Virtual methods defined by the super-class or the interfaces that are implemented in this object will be called from Java.
- `super_class`: Java class to be extended
- `interfaces`: Java interfaces to be implemented
- `return`: an anonymous object extending the class and implementing the interfaces specified

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

The alternative is writing an Android plugin. But the point of the proposal is so you don't have to -- which is the purpose of `JavaClassWrapper` in the first place. I want to make it more useful, like `JavaScriptBridge`.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

This is functionality meant for the existing `JavaClassWrapper`.

## Example
Assuming we have:
```java
package org.example;
public interface EventListener { void onTrigger(String s); }
public class SomeJavaObj { public void setEventListener(EventListener e) {...} }
```

We could use it like this:
```gdscript
var EventListener: JavaClass = JavaClassWrapper.wrap('org.example.EventListener')
var printer: JavaObject = JavaClassWrapper.callback(EventListener, func(s: String) -> void:
  print(s)
)
java_obj.setEventListener(printer)
```

The equivalent Java code for `printer` wrapper would be something like:
```java
new org.example.EventListener() { @Override public void onTrigger(String s) { gdscriptPrinter(s); } }
```
godotengine/godot-proposals#13905.title: Allow implementing Java interfaces from GDScript via `JavaClassWrapper`

### Describe the project you are working on

A simple 3d level editor.

### Describe the problem or limitation you are having in your project

Created a class named EditorCamera that wouldn't appear in Godot's editor at all, both in C# and GDScript.

### Describe the feature / enhancement and how it helps to overcome the problem or limitation

Add a warning when creating or setting the name of a class starting with "Editor" about it not appearing in Godot's editor.

### Describe how your proposal will work, with code, pseudo-code, mock-ups, and/or diagrams

When user sets the name starting with "Editor" in GDScript `class_name` display a warning message like unused variables or similar to  
`Line 1 (INCOMPATIBLE_CLASSNAME): The class name "EditorCamera" starts with "Editor" and won't be visible.` Or when creating C# class within the editor display a warning below the path similar to invalid class names.

### If this enhancement will not be used often, can it be worked around with a few lines of script?

Yes, simply renaming the class fixes the issue but lack of warnings make finding the issue hard.

### Is there a reason why this should be core and not an add-on in the asset library?

People new to the engine may run into this issue without knowing what it is, and might not consider addons as a possible fix.

godotengine/godot-proposals#14217.body:
### Problem or limitation

**The goal**: Implementing support for the OpenXR extension [XR_MSFT_composition_layer_reprojection](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XR_MSFT_composition_layer_reprojection) via a GDExtension.

**The problem**: The OpenXR struct [XrCompositionLayerProjection](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XrCompositionLayerProjection) can be extended via its `next` pointer with the following structures:

- [XrCompositionLayerReprojectionInfoMSFT](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XrCompositionLayerReprojectionInfoMSFT)
- [XrCompositionLayerReprojectionPlaneOverrideMSFT](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XrCompositionLayerReprojectionPlaneOverrideMSFT)
- [XrCompositionLayerDepthTestVARJO](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XrCompositionLayerDepthTestVARJO)

In order to implement the OpenXR extensions providing these ([XR_MSFT_composition_layer_reprojection](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XR_MSFT_composition_layer_reprojection) and [XR_VARJO_composition_layer_depth_test](https://registry.khronos.org/OpenXR/specs/1.1/html/xrspec.html#XR_VARJO_composition_layer_depth_test)) with an `OpenXRExtensionWrapper` a mechanism inside Godot is necessary for populating the `next` chain of `XrCompositionLayerProjection`.

There currently is no possible workaround.

### Proposed improvement

The following needs to be implemented into core Godot in order for a GDExtension to implement the OpenXR extensions mentioned above:
- A list of `OpenXRExtensionWrapper`s which need to extend this next chain
- Methods for registering classes to be added to the list
- A virtual method for actually setting the next pointer
- A loop going through the list and calling the appropriate method on the `projection_layer` of the OpenXR render state

The following PR implements this proposal: [https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116207](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116207)

### Proposal review

- [x] Yes, this proposal is highly specific and actionable.
godotengine/godot-proposals#14217.title: OpenXR: Add support for projection layer extensions

godotengine/godot-proposals#14231.body:
### Problem or limitation

The project I'm working on uses R8 and R8G8 DDS textures for bump maps and effect data, but Godot currently lacks the ability to import them. I could convert the DDS textures to R8G8B8 which Godot can import, or convert them to KTX textures which support importing the R8 and R8G8 formats, but it would make more sense to simply extend import support to DDS.

### Proposed improvement

DDS R8 and R8G8 textures should be imported as `FORMAT_R8` and `FORMAT_RG8` textures respectively. Due to the way the DDS format is structured, it's technically possible to create DDS textures that implement any single or any pair of channels in any order. For example you could create an image that uses any of these formats: R8, G8, B8, R8G8, R8B8, G8B8, G8R8, B8R8, B8G8. A conservative approach would only accept the R8 and R8G8 formats as valid. While a greedier approach would be to convert any single component texture into `FORMAT_R8` and any dual component texture into `FORMAT_RG8`.

It's also important to support [DXGI_FORMAT](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/dxgiformat/ne-dxgiformat-dxgi_format) style formats as defined in [DX10](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/direct3ddds/dds-header-dxt10) headers. Specifically the `DXGI_R8_UNORM` and `DXGI_R8G8_UNORM` formats.

### Proposal review

- [x] Yes, this proposal is highly specific and actionable.
godotengine/godot-proposals#14231.title: Add support for importing R8 and R8G8 DDS textures

godotengine/godot-proposals#14251.body:
### Problem or limitation

The new VirtualJoystick uses textures for user customization, ex joystick_texture. If there's no texture, it pulls from the theme properties for a standard flat colored ring.

### Proposed improvement

I believe there is a much better way of doing this - just use styleboxes to draw the rings and tip. Remove ring_normal_color, tip_normal_color etc as theme properties and remove joystick_texture and tip_texture as normal properties.

These should be replaced by "ring" and "joystick" as theme stylebox properties.

You can easily make a circle and a ring using a StyleBoxFlat - set the corners to be a super high value on all 4 sides and you have yourself a circle. Remove the center and you have a ring.

Anyone who wants the behavior of using a texture could just set the stylebox to a StyleBoxTexture.

### Proposal review

- [x] Yes, this proposal is highly specific and actionable.
godotengine/godot-proposals#14251.title: VirtualJoystick should use styleboxes entirely instead of hardcoded rings & texture

godotengine/godot-proposals#14300.body:
### Problem or limitation

Dragging multiple SplitContainers in the editor takes more clicks than it needs to. It is not possible to move multiple SplitContainers at the same time, so moving 2 of them requires dragging them independently. Some other applications allow dragging multiple splits at their intersection, moving 3 panels at once.
For instance, if you want to expand one dock in particular, you need to separately resize its height and width.

Also when using 3 or 4 viewports in the 3D editor, it is possible to drag the intersection for these, so it is somewhat inconsistent that you cannot do this with the rest of the editor.
The 3D viewport doesn't use SplitContainers for its multiple viewports, and it has some issues like the grab area being too small, no accessibility support, or no touch dragger support. I would expect it to have the same functionality as SplitContainers.

### Proposed improvement

It is not too hard to do this with your own SplitContainers, you can add a Control child to the SplitContainer dragger with mouse filter pass and have it set the child SplitContainer's split_offset with gui events. 
The tricky part is to make it work automatically with nested SplitContainers and making sure it updates correctly in all cases. and it would have to use not just the direct children for the Editor, since some SplitContainers are nested in other controls, like the center split container is in a VBoxContainer.
To make it automatically detect changes, SplitContainers need to check their ancestors for other SplitContainers and tell them to update when needed. This would then add children to the draggers to allow dragging both SplitContainers at once.
This would also be useful for users making UIs with multiple SplitContainers, so it could be exposed as an option.

I don't think these will need their own accessibility elements, and I don't think the accessibility action API would support them. I also don't think they should have their own touch draggers, adding them for each intersection would get busy and have overlaps.

The 3D viewport could be probably be refactored to use SplitContainers for better consistency once this feature is added.

I have a WIP:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3470a57-5e57-48a2-9ca3-de6ebe530930

### Proposal review

- [x] Yes, this proposal is highly specific and actionable.
godotengine/godot-proposals#14300.title: Drag nested SplitContainers at intersection

godotengine/godot-proposals#14320.body:
### Problem or limitation

The iOS plugins I have developed currently bundle all required dependency frameworks directly within the plugin’s release archive. During installation, these frameworks are copied into the Godot project and registered with the generated Xcode project using `EditorExportPlugin.add_apple_embedded_platform_embedded_framework`.

While this guarantees that dependencies are self-contained and avoids additional setup steps for users, it significantly increases the size of the plugin’s release archive. This becomes especially problematic when integrating large SDKs (e.g., Firebase), which introduce numerous and sizable transitive framework dependencies. As a result, plugin distribution artifacts become unnecessarily large and difficult to maintain.

Godot’s current iOS export lifecycle for `EditorExportPlugin` is effectively:

1. `_export_begin()` — plugins collect configuration data
2. Godot generates the complete `.xcodeproj` (including `project.pbxproj`)
3. Godot executes `xcodebuild archive` (which triggers linking and package resolution)
4. `_export_end()` — plugins may run post-build scripts

There is **no lifecycle hook between steps 2 and 3** that allows a plugin to modify the generated Xcode project before the archive build begins.

This prevents clean integration of Swift Package Manager (SPM), CocoaPods, or similar dependency managers:

* SPM dependencies must be added **after** the `.xcodeproj` is generated.
* `xcodebuild -resolvePackageDependencies` must run **before** `xcodebuild archive`.
* Currently, there is no supported point in the lifecycle to perform this work.

The only viable workaround is requiring users to enable **“Export Project Only”**, causing Godot to stop after project generation. While functional, this breaks the expected one-click export workflow and shifts additional manual build responsibility onto the user.

### Proposed improvement

Introduce a new Apple-embedded export lifecycle method in `EditorExportPlugin`:

```cpp
// editor/export/editor_export_plugin.h
virtual void _end_generate_apple_embedded_project(
    const String &p_path,
    bool p_will_build_archive
);
```

For Apple-embedded platforms, this hook would be invoked immediately after the Xcode project is written to disk and **before** the archive build is triggered (i.e., before the `export_project_only` conditional branch).

Insertion point (current engine source):

```
editor/export/editor_export_platform_apple_embedded.cpp
```

Specifically, directly after project generation and prior to the archive execution logic, here:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2327a823578a30f09068f97272598521896d5633/editor/export/editor_export_platform_apple_embedded.cpp#L2061

### Rationale

This hook would:

* Enable clean SPM integration (e.g., GoogleMobileAds, Firebase, etc.)
* Allow programmatic modification of the generated `.xcodeproj`
* Support dependency resolution steps such as `xcodebuild -resolvePackageDependencies`
* Eliminate the need to bundle large precompiled frameworks
* Preserve the one-click export experience for users

### Scope & Compatibility

* Minimal surface-area change
* Non-breaking (purely additive virtual method)
* Scoped exclusively to Apple-embedded exporters
* No behavioral changes for existing plugins

This provides a targeted extension point that resolves a structural limitation in the current iOS export lifecycle without introducing architectural complexity.

### Proposal review

- [x] Yes, this proposal is highly specific and actionable.
godotengine/godot-proposals#14320.title: [iOS Export] Add EditorExportPlugin project-generated hook after the project is written but before build
